EDCS-06000051.summary: This is a public dedication from the people of Sabratha to Emperor Philip the Arab and his son.

EDCS-06000052.text_edition: \n Quinto Herennio Etrusco Messio Decio filio Augusti domini nostri tribunicia [potestate ------\n
EDCS-06000052.translation: To Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, son of Augustus, our lord, [holding] tribunician power for the [number]th time. [...]
EDCS-06000052.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius, a co-emperor with Decius.

EDCS-06000053.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Publio Licinio Gallieno Pii Felici Augusto domino nostro pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XIIII consuli VI designato VII patri patriae proconsuli et Corneliae Saloninae Augustae coniugi Gallieni Augusti Sabrathenses publice\n
EDCS-06000053.translation: To Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, our lord, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 14th time, consul for the 6th time, designated consul for the 7th time, father of the country, proconsul, and to Cornelia Salonina Augusta, wife of Gallienus Augustus. The people of Sabratha publicly [dedicated this].
EDCS-06000053.summary: This is a public dedication from the people of Sabratha to Emperor Gallienus and his wife Cornelia Salonina.

EDCS-06000058.text_edition: \n Aedem Liberi Patris quam antiqua ruina cum labe PIVS instaurationem EARISO ddominorum nnostrorum Flavi Iuli Constantii maximi et Flavi Iuli Constantis maximi triumphatorum semper Augustorum amrimum praesidicium viri perfectissimi Flavi Victoris Calpurni servavit hanc Lucius Aemilius Caelestinus duovir quinquennalis flamen perpetuus amori patriae studiose respondita viro perfectissimo patrono provinciae dedicanteR fecit ex decreto ordinis\n
EDCS-06000058.translation: Lucius Aemilius Caelestinus, duovir quinquennalis, perpetual priest, dedicated this temple of Liber Pater, which the ancient ruin with its collapse had destroyed, and which the most excellent Flavius Victor Calpurnius, a guard of our lords Flavius Julius Constantius, greatest triumphant Augustus, and Flavius Julius Constans, greatest triumphant Augustus, had preserved with affection and the greatest protection, out of love for his country, responding eagerly to the most excellent Flavius Victor Calpurnius, patron of the province, by decree of the council.
EDCS-06000058.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a restored temple of Liber Pater by Lucius Aemilius Caelestinus, a local magistrate, likely during the reign of Constantius II and Constantius Gallus.

EDCS-06000079.text_edition: \n invicti Augusti pontificis maximi Germanici maximi tribunicia potestate \n
EDCS-06000079.translation: [holding] tribunician power. [...] of the unconquered Augustus, chief priest, greatest Germanicus.
EDCS-06000079.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription that refers to an emperor's titles, likely from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-06000081.summary: This is a very short inscription dedicating something to an emperor named Aurelius Augustus.

EDCS-06000090.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning honorific titles of an emperor.

EDCS-06000101.text_edition: \n Caio Anicio Quirina Frontoni equo publico ornato Anici Bassiani filio quaestori aedili IIviro IIviro quinquennali designato amatori patriae cives ob merita curante Quinto Flavio Hiceta\n
EDCS-06000101.translation: To Gaius Anicius Fronton, son of Anicius Bassianus, of the voting tribe Quirina, honored with the public equestrian rank, quaestor, aedile, duovir, designated duovir quinquennalis, lover of his country. Erected by the citizens because of his merits, under the supervision of Quintus Flavius Hicetas.
EDCS-06000101.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Anicius Fronton, a prominent local official, and records his career and posthumous recognition by the citizens.

EDCS-06000102.text_edition: \n Avitio Quinti filio Quirina (tribu) Rufo tribuno militum legionis VII geminae felicis praefecto cohortis I Augustae Thracum proximo tertiae militiae IIviro decreto ordinis ob merita eius erga rem publicam eximia Quintus Avitius Lucanus pater titulo et honore contentus sua pecunia posuit\n
EDCS-06000102.translation: Quintus Avitius Lucanus, the father, content with the title and honor, placed this at his own expense to Avitius Rufus, son of Quintus, of the voting tribe Quirina, tribune of the soldiers of the seventh legion Gemina Felix, prefect of the first cohort of Thracians Augusta, recently holding the third military command, duovir, by decree of the council, because of his outstanding merits towards the state.
EDCS-06000102.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by a father for his son, Quintus Avitius Rufus, a military officer and local magistrate, honoring his military service and civic career.

EDCS-06000106.text_edition: \n DEM statuam co curante Calpurnio viro perfectissimo praeside provinciae Tripolitanae\n
EDCS-06000106.translation: A statue, erected under the supervision of Calpurnius, most excellent man, governor of the province of Tripolitana.
EDCS-06000106.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue, dedicated to Calpurnius, the governor of Tripolitana, by an unknown individual or group.

EDCS-06000109.text_edition: \n Flavi Vivi Benedicti viri perfectissimi totius integritatis moderationis iustitiae provisionis fidei benignitatis fortitudinis ac beneficentiae viro Flavio Vivio Benedicto viro perfectissimo praesidi provinciae Tripolitanae inter cetera beneficia sua quibus omnem provinciam conpendiis sic remediis et virtutibus fovit sublevabit erexit etiam ob ea quae sibi specialiter conlata sunt civitas Sabrathensis exsultans quod post ruinam et abnegatum thermarum populo exercitium citra ullius dispendium ornamentis patriae revocavit ordo populusque concinentibus omnibus votis statuam patrono praestantissimo gratanti studio conlocavit Dedicata consulum dominorum nostrorum Valentis VI et Valent<in>iani II Augustorum die V Kalendas Augustas\n
EDCS-06000109.translation: To Flavia Vivi Benedicta, a man of utmost integrity, moderation, justice, foresight, loyalty, kindness, strength, and beneficence, and to Flavia Vivi Benedicto, a man of utmost excellence, governor of the province of Tripolitana. In addition to his other benefits, with which he fostered and supported the entire province through efficiencies, remedies, and virtues, the city of Sabratha also rejoiced, elevating him because of these special honors bestowed upon him. As after the ruin and denial of exercise in the baths to the people, he restored the ornaments of the fatherland without any loss, the council and the people, with all their wishes in unison, placed a statue for their most outstanding patron with jubilant zeal. Dedicated under the consulship of our lords Valens VI and Valentinian II Augusti, on the fifth day before the Kalends of August.
EDCS-06000109.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue to the governor Flavia Vivi Benedicto by the city of Sabratha, celebrating his restoration of public bath facilities and general good governance, dating to the consulship of Valens VI and Valentinian II.

EDCS-06000111.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely recording the title or honorific of a 'vir perfectissimus' and mentioning his parentage or origin.

EDCS-06000114.text_edition: \n Dis manibus sacrum Varigata Lucius Munatius Dexter veteranus pius vixit annis LV ossa tibi bene quiescant terra tibi levis sit hic situs est\n
EDCS-06000114.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Varigata, Lucius Munatius Dexter, a pious veteran, lived 55 years. May your bones rest well; may the earth lie lightly on you. Here he is buried.
EDCS-06000114.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Munatius Dexter, a veteran, recording his name, status, age at death, and a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-06000115.text_edition: \n Ulpiano V sagittario natione nus nnos plus minus directo anni curante Flavio P\n
EDCS-06000115.translation: To Ulpianus, a sagittary (archer) by nation, with... more or less [X] years, under the supervision of Flavius P...
EDCS-06000115.summary: This fragment likely records the dedication of a monument or honors to Ulpianus, an archer, specifying his nationality and age, and naming Flavius P as supervisor.

EDCS-06000119.text_edition: \n Publio o Publi filio Galeria (tribu) Maximo Numerio Avito V G C V rarissimo Sabrathenses publice patrono \n
EDCS-06000119.translation: To Publius o Publi son, of the Galeria tribe, Maximus Numerius Avitus, most excellent man, most rare, from the people of Sabratha, publicly, as patron.
EDCS-06000119.summary: This inscription honors Publius o Publi filio Galeria Maximo Numerio Avito as a patron of the people of Sabratha, describing him as a 'most excellent' and 'most rare' man.

EDCS-06000120.text_edition: \n Aurelius Flacianus P sacerdotalis scola felix et cum uiveret moreretur fuit fil vixit annis L\n
EDCS-06000120.translation: Aurelius Flacianus P, priest of the college, happy, both when alive and when dying, his son lived 50 years.
EDCS-06000120.summary: This inscription records the life and death of Aurelius Flacianus P, a priest, mentioning his son's age at death and implying a posthumous dedication.

EDCS-06000121.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a son of Cornelius and the office of aediles.

EDCS-06000123.text_edition: \n Caio Flavio Quinti filio Papiria Pudenti flamini Liberi Patris IIviro flamini perpetuo cuius pater Flavius Tullus post multas liberalitates per quas patriam suam exornavit aquam privata pecunia induxit item lacus numero XII exstruxit eosdemque crustis et statuis marmoreis excoluit praeterea HS CC milia nummum ad tutelam eiusdem aquae rei publicae promisit et intulit quod ipse quoque Pudens super numerosam munificentiam quam in cives suos contulit etiam muneris gladiatori spectaculum primus in patria sua per dies quinque splendidissimum ediderit ordo Sabrathensium populo postulante quadrigam ei de publico ponendam censuit Flavius Pudens honore contentus sua pecunia posuit\n
EDCS-06000123.translation: To Gaius Flavius Quintus' son, Papiria Pudens, flamen of Liber Pater, duovir, perpetual flamen. His father, Flavius Tullus, after many acts of generosity with which he adorned his homeland, brought water into the city with his private funds, and also constructed 12 basins, decorating them with marble veneers and statues. Furthermore, he pledged and contributed 200,000 sesterces to the state for the maintenance of the same water system. And that he himself, Pudens, in addition to the numerous munificence he bestowed upon his citizens, also presented the first most splendid gladiatorial spectacle in his homeland for five days, the council of Sabrathenes, at the people's request, decreed that a chariot should be erected for him at public expense. Flavius Pudens, content with the honor, placed it with his own money.
EDCS-06000123.summary: This inscription details the honors and public works undertaken by father and son, Flavius Tullus and Gaius Flavius Pudens, in the city of Sabratha, including water supply, public basins, and gladiatorial games, culminating in a statue erected for Pudens.

EDCS-06000168.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, possibly part of a name or a dedication, with illegible parts.

EDCS-06000169.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Domiti simi tri V \n
EDCS-06000169.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, [son] of Domitius, [lived] 5 years.
EDCS-06000169.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Domitius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-06000174.text_edition: \n i iussit et in singul Proculus S\n
EDCS-06000174.summary: This fragment of an inscription mentions an order by a person named Proculus, its purpose is unclear.

EDCS-06000175.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription that likely contains a personal name, possibly 'Rufus'.

EDCS-06000178.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that likely contains a personal name, possibly 'Trebius'.

EDCS-06000203.text_edition: \n spiritus Athica bonae memoriae dulcis nomen anima mellea petimus orationibus tuis exaudiat nos vixit annos LXVII\n
EDCS-06000203.translation: Athica, good memory, sweet name, honeyed soul, we ask that you may hear us with your prayers. You lived 67 years.
EDCS-06000203.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman named Athica, who lived 67 years, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-06000205.text_edition: \n Biocena bonae memoriae bixit sic nnos XXXV plus minus et in XRChristo requievit sub die XV Kalendas Maias indictione VII\n
EDCS-06000205.translation: Biocena, good memory, lived about 35 years and rested in Christ on the 15th day before the Kalends of May, in the 7th indiction.
EDCS-06000205.summary: This is an early Christian funerary inscription for Biocena, who died around the age of 35, dated by indiction to the 7th century.

EDCS-06000206.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Bonus bixit sic in pake sic plus minus annos LXXX et requiebit sic in pace\n
EDCS-06000206.translation: Good memory, Bonus lived about 80 years and rested in peace.
EDCS-06000206.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bonus, who lived approximately 80 years, likely from the late Roman or early Christian period.

EDCS-06000207.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Corcondiae bixit sic plus minus annos XII\n
EDCS-06000207.translation: Good memory, Corcondia lived about 12 years.
EDCS-06000207.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Corcondia, who lived about 12 years, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-06000208.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Crisomali bixit sic in pace annos LX plus minus\n
EDCS-06000208.translation: Good memory, Crisomali lived about 60 years in peace.
EDCS-06000208.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Crisomali, who lived approximately 60 years, likely from the late Roman or early Christian period.

EDCS-06000209.text_edition: \n \xce\x91 \xce\xa9 Hik requiebit sic bonae memoriae S Dominika sic bixit sic in pake sic annos decem plus minus sub die III mensis Maii indictione XI\n
EDCS-06000209.translation: Alpha Omega, Requiebit, good memory, Dominika, lived about ten years, on the 3rd day of May, in the 11th indiction.
EDCS-06000209.summary: This is an early Christian funerary inscription for Dominika, who died around the age of 10, dated by indiction to the 6th or 7th century.

EDCS-06000210.text_edition: \n Bom(nae) memoriae Esidorus bixit sic in pace annos LXX plus minus sub die XI mensis Octobris indictione IIII\n
EDCS-06000210.translation: To the good memory of Esidorus, who lived thus in peace for 70 years more or less, on the 11th day of the month of October, in the 4th indiction.
EDCS-06000210.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Esidorus, dated by indiction.

EDCS-06000211.text_edition: \n \xce\x91 \xce\xa9 Bonae memoriae Ferrandus bixit in pace nnos XL plus minus sub die XII Kalendas Maias\n
EDCS-06000211.translation: To the good memory of Ferrandus, who lived in peace for 40 years more or less, on the 12th day before the Kalends of May.
EDCS-06000211.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Ferrandus, dated by the Roman calendar.

EDCS-06000212.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Fuzani bixit sic in pake sic annos XCV plus minus et requiebit sic in pake sic sub die VIII mensis Februari indictione XII\n
EDCS-06000212.translation: To the good memory of Fuzani, who lived thus in peace for 95 years more or less, and rested thus in peace, on the 8th day of the month of February, in the 12th indiction.
EDCS-06000212.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan and resting of Fuzani, dated by indiction.

EDCS-06000213.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Georgi bixit sic in pace annos plus minus XX sub die XXII mensis Aprilis indictione XI\n
EDCS-06000213.translation: To the good memory of Georgi, who lived thus in peace for 20 years more or less, on the 22nd day of the month of April, in the 11th indiction.
EDCS-06000213.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Georgi, dated by indiction.

EDCS-06000214.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Ianacis bixit sic annos III bonae memoriae Iobris bixit sic in pace annos VI plus minus\n
EDCS-06000214.translation: To the good memory of Ianacis, who lived for 3 years; to the good memory of Iobris, who lived in peace for 6 years more or less.
EDCS-06000214.summary: This funerary inscription records the short lifespans of two individuals, Ianacis and Iobris.

EDCS-06000215.text_edition: \n Ioanes bonae me moriae bixit sic annos LXXXX\n
EDCS-06000215.translation: Ioanes, to good memory, lived thus for 90 years.
EDCS-06000215.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Ioanes.

EDCS-06000216.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Ioanes bixit sic in pace annos LXXXV plus minus s<u=Y>(b) die XXVII mensis Ianuari indictione V
EDCS-06000216.translation: To the good memory of Ioanes, who lived thus in peace for 85 years more or less, on the 27th day of the month of January, in the 5th indiction.
EDCS-06000216.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Ioanes, dated by indiction.

EDCS-06000217.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Iohannes in XPO requievit vixit nnm unum menses VIIII dies XX sub die XIII mensis Aprilis indictione VIII
EDCS-06000217.translation: To the good memory of Iohannes, rested in Christ, lived one year, 9 months, 20 days, on the 13th day of the month of April, in the 8th indiction.
EDCS-06000217.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Iohannes, dated by indiction, and mentions his rest in Christ.

EDCS-06000219.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Irene bixit sic in pace annos VIIII plus minus sub die XIII mensis Maii indictione XI\n
EDCS-06000219.translation: To the good memory of Irene, who lived thus in peace for 9 years more or less, on the 13th day of the month of May, in the 11th indiction.
EDCS-06000219.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Irene, dated by indiction.

EDCS-06000221.text_edition: \n Indictione III Bonae memoriae Liberatae bixit sic annos XL plus minus pauci et mali et requiebit sic in Christo mense Iulio com VIII Audias in illadie bocem Domini Iesu Christi dicentis remissa sunt tibi unibersa delicta\n
EDCS-06000221.translation: In the 3rd indiction, to the good memory of Liberatae, who lived for 40 years more or less, and rested in Christ, in the month of July with 8 days, on that day the voice of the Lord Jesus Christ saying 'Your sins are forgiven you' being heard.
EDCS-06000221.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Liberatae and includes a religious phrase about forgiveness of sins, dated by indiction.

EDCS-06000223.text_edition: \n \xce\x91 \xce\xa9 Marcella bonae memoriae in XRChristo requievit die Kalendarum Ianuariarum indictione \xce\xb8\n
EDCS-06000223.translation: Marcella of good memory rested in Christ on the Kalends of January, in the eighth indiction.
EDCS-06000223.summary: This inscription records the death of Marcella, who died on January 1st during the eighth indiction, likely from the late antique or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-06000225.text_edition: \n In nomine Domini (Iesus) Christi memoria Nadese filia Misekor bixit sic nnos VII dies LX reqie \n
EDCS-06000225.translation: In the name of the Lord Christ, Nadese daughter of Misekor lived thus for 7 years and 60 days, and rested.
EDCS-06000225.summary: This inscription records the life and death of Nadese, daughter of Misekor, who lived for 7 years and 60 days.

EDCS-06000227.text_edition: \n Ispiritus bonae memoriae tecum Segeddutis qui bixit sic annis LX plus minus\n
EDCS-06000227.translation: May the spirit of good memory be with you, Segeddutis, who lived for more or less 60 years.
EDCS-06000227.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Segeddutis, mentioning his lifespan of approximately 60 years.

EDCS-06000228.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Stefanus bixit sic in pace annos LV indictione VIII\n
EDCS-06000228.translation: Stefanus of good memory lived in peace for 55 years, in the eighth indiction.
EDCS-06000228.summary: This epitaph records the life of Stefanus, who lived for 55 years and died during the eighth indiction.

EDCS-06000230.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Teodora bixit sic in pace annos X plus minus\n
EDCS-06000230.translation: Teodora of good memory lived in peace for more or less 10 years.
EDCS-06000230.summary: This funerary inscription records the short life of Teodora, who lived for approximately 10 years.

EDCS-06000236.text_edition: \n qui legis ora pro me peccatore\n
EDCS-06000236.translation: You who read, pray for me, a sinner.
EDCS-06000236.summary: This is a brief plea for prayers for the deceased.

EDCS-06000237.summary: This is a brief plea for prayers for the deceased female.

EDCS-06000247.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Aemilio Pacoro qui vixit annis L menses IIII dies X Aemilia Calliope coniux marito piissimo fecit\n
EDCS-06000247.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aemilia Calliope made this for her most devoted husband, Lucius Aemilius Pacorus, who lived 50 years, 4 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-06000247.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Aemilia Calliope to her husband Lucius Aemilius Pacorus, specifying his age at death.

EDCS-06000248.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of an epitaph, mentioning the name Ariston.

EDCS-06000261.text_edition: \n In ok tumulo positus est bonae memoriae Ianes biksit sic in pake sic plus minus annos XXX rekiebit sic sub karta decima mensis Aprilis\n
EDCS-06000261.translation: In this tomb lies Ianes of good memory, who lived approximately 30 years, and rested on the tenth day of the month of April.
EDCS-06000261.summary: This tomb inscription records the burial of Ianes, his approximate age, and the date of his rest in April.

EDCS-06000262.text_edition: \n cia Narsis laris filia vixit plus minus annos VIII menses VI\n
EDCS-06000262.translation: Cia, daughter of Iaris, lived more or less 8 years and 6 months.
EDCS-06000262.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Cia, recording her age at death.

EDCS-06000263.translation: To the good memory of Tednus. Lived [..] years.
EDCS-06000263.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tednus, recording the number of years they lived (though the total is lost).

EDCS-06000264.text_edition: \n Bonae memoriae Voilim in pace bixit sic annos\n
EDCS-06000264.translation: To the good memory of Voilim. Lived in peace [..] years.
EDCS-06000264.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Voilim, recording their lifespan in peace (though the total is lost).

EDCS-06000268.text_edition: \n Publius Cornelius Attax Marcianus Lucius Appius Amicus Rufinianus curratores refectionis thermarum tertium deo Aesculapio votum solverunt\n
EDCS-06000268.translation: Publius Cornelius Attax Marcianus and Lucius Appius Amicus Rufinianus, curators of the repair of the baths, paid their vow for the third time to the god Aesculapius.
EDCS-06000268.summary: This inscription records a vow being paid by two curators for the repair of baths to the god Aesculapius.

EDCS-06000273.text_edition: \n Cereri Augustae sacrum Caius Rubellius Blandus consul pontifex proconsul dedicavit Suphunibal ornatrix patriae Annobalis Rusonis de sua pecunia faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-06000273.translation: Sacred to Ceres Augusta. Caius Rubellius Blandus, consul, pontifex, proconsul, dedicated it. Suphunibal, mother of her country, commissioned it to be made at her own expense by Annobalis Rusonis.
EDCS-06000273.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a sacred monument to Ceres Augusta by Caius Rubellius Blandus, with Suphunibal commissioning its construction.

EDCS-06000274.text_edition: \n Cereri Luciferae Antoniae Quinti filiae Victorinae coniugi rarissimae QQuintus Iulius Iustus ex decreto ordinis\n
EDCS-06000274.translation: To Ceres Lucifera. Quintus Julius Iustus, by decree of the council, [dedicated this] to his most rare wife, Victorina, daughter of Antonia, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-06000274.summary: This inscription records Quintus Julius Iustus dedicating a monument to his wife, Victorina, by order of the local council.

EDCS-06000277.text_edition: \n Dis Augustis Quintus Marcius Cai filius Barea consul XVvir sacris faciundis fetialis proconsul II patronus dedicavit Iddibal Magonis filius Tapapius Lepcitanus de sua pecunia fecit\n
EDCS-06000277.translation: To the August Gods. Quintus Marcius Caius Barea, consul (XV), officiant of sacrifices, fetialis, proconsul (II), patron, dedicated it. Iddibal, son of Mago, of Tapapius, from Lepcis, made it at his own expense.
EDCS-06000277.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the August Gods by Quintus Marcius Caius Barea, with Iddibal Magonis funding its construction.

EDCS-06000279.text_edition: \n Dibus Lepcis Magnae Marcus Vipsanius Clemens redemtor marmorarius templi Liberi Patris de sua pecunia votum solvit sub cura Quinti Servili Candidi amatoris patriae amatoris civium ornatoris patriae flaminis divi Vespasiani\n
EDCS-06000279.translation: To the Gods of Lepcis Magna. Marcus Vipsanius Clemens, a marble worker and contractor of the temple of Liber Pater, fulfilled his vow at his own expense, under the supervision of Quintus Servilius Candidus, lover of his country, patron of citizens, and flamen of the deified Vespasian.
EDCS-06000279.summary: This inscription records Marcus Vipsanius Clemens, a contractor, fulfilling a vow for the temple of Liber Pater at his own expense, supervised by Quintus Servilius Candidus.

EDCS-06000280.text_edition: \n Fortunae Iuliae Quinti filiae Iustae Iustus pater ex decreto ordinis\n
EDCS-06000280.translation: To Fortuna Iulia, daughter of Quintus Iusta. Iustus the father, by decree of the council.
EDCS-06000280.summary: This inscription records Iustus dedicating a monument to his daughter, Fortuna Iulia, by order of the local council.

EDCS-06000281.text_edition: \n Fortunae Crescenti et Horae Bonae Hostiliae Quinti filiae Honestae Quintus Iulius Iustus avos ex decreto ordinis\n
EDCS-06000281.translation: To Fortuna Crescenti and Bona, wife of Quintus Honestus. Quintus Julius Iustus, the grandfather, by decree of the council.
EDCS-06000281.summary: This inscription records the grandfather Quintus Julius Iustus dedicating a monument to Fortuna Crescenti and his wife Bona, by order of the local council.

EDCS-06000283.text_edition: \n Genio coloniae Lepcis Magnae Lepcitani publice\n
EDCS-06000283.translation: To the Genius of the colony of Lepcis Magna, from the citizens.
EDCS-06000283.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to the Genius of the colony of Lepcis Magna.

EDCS-06000284.text_edition: \n Genio coloniae Lepcis Magnae Ulpius Rogatus Pao structor dono dedit\n
EDCS-06000284.translation: Ulpius Rogatus Pao, a builder, dedicated this to the Genius of the colony of Lepcis Magna.
EDCS-06000284.summary: This is a dedication of a monument or object by Ulpius Rogatus Pao, a builder, to the Genius of the colony of Lepcis Magna.

EDCS-06000294.text_edition: \n Iuliae Domnae Augustae deae Iunoni orbis terrae Quintus Fulvius Dida Bibulianus votum solvit\n
EDCS-06000294.translation: Quintus Fulvius Dida Bibulianus fulfilled his vow to Julia Domna Augusta, the goddess Juno of the world.
EDCS-06000294.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Quintus Fulvius Dida Bibulianus to Julia Domna, identified with the goddess Juno, likely during her lifetime.

EDCS-06000297.text_edition: \n Iovigena Liber Pater votum quod destinaveram Lari Severi patrio Iovigenae solis mei Pudens pater pro filio ob tribunatus candidam et ob praeturam proximam tantamque in nos princippum conlatam indulgentiam conpos votorum omnium dentes duos Lucae bovis Indorum tuorum dico\n
EDCS-06000297.translation: Pudens, father and patron, fulfilling all his vows, offers two teeth of your Indian ox, Iovigena, to Liber Pater, his own patron Iovigena, for the sake of his son, on account of his candidacy for the tribunate, his upcoming praetorship, and the great indulgence shown to us by the princes.
EDCS-06000297.summary: This inscription is a complex votive offering by Pudens for his son's political advancement, thanking the deities Liber Pater and Iovigena, and possibly referring to Antonine or Severan era.

EDCS-06000302.text_edition: \n Marti Augusto sacrum auspiciis Imperatoris Caesaris Augusti pontificis maxumi patris patriae ductu Cossi Lentuli consulis XVviri sacris faciundis proconsulis provincia Africa bello Gaetulico liberata civitas Lepcitana\n
EDCS-06000302.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, by the auspices of the Emperor Caesar Augustus, chief priest, father of the fatherland, under the leadership of Cossus Lentulus, consul, XVviri sacris faciundis, proconsul, for the province of Africa, after liberation from the Gaetulian war, by the citizens of Lepcis.
EDCS-06000302.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars Augustus by the citizens of Lepcis, made under the auspices of the Emperor and Cossus Lentulus, and commemorates the liberation of Africa from the Gaetulian war.

EDCS-06000308.text_edition: \n Augusta salutaris Caius Vibius Marsus pro consul III dedicavit\n
EDCS-06000308.translation: Caius Vibius Marsus, three times proconsul, dedicated this to Augusta Salutaris.
EDCS-06000308.summary: This is a dedication by Caius Vibius Marsus, a former proconsul, to Augusta Salutaris, likely an imperial deity or personification.

EDCS-06000310.text_edition: \n Lucius Tettius Eutychus in suo sua pecunia cisternam Veneri et Leptitanis donui sic cura Luci Tetti Comi liberti faciendam curavit\n
EDCS-06000310.translation: Lucius Tettius Eutychus, with his own money, had a cistern built by Lucius Tettius Comus, his freedman, and dedicated it to Venus and the people of Leptis.
EDCS-06000310.summary: This inscription records the construction and dedication of a cistern by Lucius Tettius Eutychus for the benefit of Venus and the inhabitants of Lepcis.

EDCS-06000312.text_edition: \n Numini Veneris Adquisitricis Augustae sacrum Iucundus Augusti nostri verna vegtigalis sic IIII publicorum Africae vilicus Lepcis magnae terrestris de suo posuit\n
EDCS-06000312.translation: Sacred to the divine power of Venus the Acquirer, Augusta. Iucundus, a servant (verna) of our Emperor, an official of the public revenues in Africa, and a land steward at Lepcis Magna, placed this from his own resources.
EDCS-06000312.summary: This is a dedication to Venus Augusta by Iucundus, an imperial freedman and tax official in Africa, likely during the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-06000313.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatoris Antonini Augusti Pii liberorumque eius Veneri Calchidicae Cupidinis statuam Caius Claudius Septimius Afer dono dedit dedicavit posuit\n
EDCS-06000313.translation: For the safety of Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius and his children, Caius Claudius Septimius Afer gave, dedicated, and placed this statue of Venus of Chalcis to Venus Chalcidica.
EDCS-06000313.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue of Venus Chalcidica by Caius Claudius Septimius Afer for the well-being of Emperor Antoninus Pius and his family.

EDCS-06000318.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Divi filio Augusto pontifici maximo consuli designato XIII tribunicia potestate XXI Fulvii Lepcitani conservatori\n
EDCS-06000318.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the Divine, Augustus, chief priest, consul-designate for the 13th time, with tribunician power for the 21st time, Fulvius Lepcitanus, preserver, [dedicates this].
EDCS-06000318.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Augustus by Fulvius Lepcitanus, likely a prominent citizen of Lepcis, during Augustus' reign.

EDCS-06000322.text_edition: \n Numini Imperatoris Caesaris divi fili Augusti pontificis maximi imperatoris XX consulis XIII tribunicia potestate XXXIIII calchidicum sic et porticus et porta et via ab XVviris sacrorum dedicata est Iddibal Himilis filius Caphada Aemilius de sua pecunia faciendum curavit calchidicum sic et porticus et portam et viam Iddibal Himilis filius Caphada Aemilius de sua pecunia faciendum curavit calchidicum sic et porticus et porta sic et via sic\n
EDCS-06000322.translation: To the divine spirit of Emperor Caesar Augustus, pontifex maximus, imperator XX, consul XIII, holding tribunician power for the 34th time. The chalcidicum, the colonnade, the gate, and the road were dedicated by the board of fifteen priests. Iddibal Himilis son of Caphada Aemilius saw to the construction of the chalcidicum, the colonnade, the gate, and the road at his own expense.
EDCS-06000322.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a chalcidicum, colonnade, gate, and road by the XVviri sacrorum and its construction at his own expense by Iddibal Himilis Caphada Aemilius, likely dating to the reign of Augustus.

EDCS-06000328.text_edition: \n Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio Augusto Divi Iuli nepoti pontifici maximo consuli V imperatori VIII tribunicia potestate XXXVII Caius Rubellius Blandus quaestor divi Augusti tribunus plebis praetor consul proconsul pontifex patronus ex reditibus agrorum quos Lepcitanis restituit vias omnis civitatis Lepcitanae sternendas silice curavit Marcus Etrilius Lupercus legatus pro praetore patronus sub hasta faciendum locavit Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio Augusto Divi Iuli nepoti pontifici maximo consuli V imperatori VIII tribunicia potestate XXXVII Caius Rubellius Blandus quaestor divi Augusti tribunus plebis praetor consul proconsul pontifex patronus ex reditibus agrorum quos Lepcitanis restituit vias omnis civitatis Lepcitanae sternendas silice curavit Marcus Etrilius Lupercus legatus pro praetore patronus sub hasta faciendum locavit\n
EDCS-06000328.translation: To Tiberius Caesar, son of the divine Augustus, grandson of the divine Julius, pontifex maximus, consul V, imperator VIII, holding tribunician power for the 37th time. Caius Rubellius Blandus, quaestor of the divine Augustus, tribune of the plebs, praetor, consul, proconsul, pontifex, and patron, from the revenues of the lands which he restored to the people of Lepcis, took care to have all the roads of the Lepcis community paved with stone. Marcus Etrilius Lupercus, legate pro praetore, patron, contracted for its construction by auction.
EDCS-06000328.summary: This inscription details the paving of roads in Lepcis by Caius Rubellius Blandus and Marcus Etrilius Lupercus, likely in honor of Tiberius, dating to Tiberius's reign.

EDCS-06000329.text_edition: \n Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio Augusto divi Iuli nepoti pontifici maximo consuli V imperatori VIII tribunicia potestate XXXVII Caius Rubellius Blandus quaestor divi Augusti tribunus plebis praetor consul proconsul pontifex patronus ex reditibus agrorum quos Lepcitanis restituit vias omnis civitatis Lepcitanae sternendas silice curavit Marcus Etrilius Lupercus legatus pro praetore patronus sub hasta faciendum locavit\n
EDCS-06000329.translation: To Tiberius Caesar, son of the divine Augustus, grandson of the divine Julius, pontifex maximus, consul V, imperator VIII, holding tribunician power for the 37th time. Caius Rubellius Blandus, quaestor of the divine Augustus, tribune of the plebs, praetor, consul, proconsul, pontifex, and patron, from the revenues of the lands which he restored to the people of Lepcis, took care to have all the roads of the Lepcis community paved with stone. Marcus Etrilius Lupercus, legate pro praetore, patron, contracted for its construction by auction.
EDCS-06000329.summary: This inscription details the paving of roads in Lepcis by Caius Rubellius Blandus and Marcus Etrilius Lupercus, likely in honor of Tiberius, dating to Tiberius's reign.

EDCS-06000331.text_edition: \n Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio Augusto\n
EDCS-06000331.translation: To Tiberius Caesar, son of the divine Augustus.
EDCS-06000331.summary: This is a dedication to Tiberius Caesar, son of the divine Augustus.

EDCS-06000333.text_edition: \n Druso Caesari Imperatoris Tiberi Caesaris Augusti filio divi Augusti nepoti Perperna Lepcitanus\n
EDCS-06000333.translation: To Drusus Caesar, son of Emperor Tiberius Caesar Augustus, grandson of the divine Augustus. Perperna of Lepcis [dedicated this].
EDCS-06000333.summary: This is a dedication to Drusus Caesar, son of Tiberius, by Perperna of Lepcis.

EDCS-06000335.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Caesari Augusto Germanico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate V imperatori XI patri patriae consuli designato IIII\n
EDCS-06000335.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 5th time, imperator XI, father of the country, designated consul IIII.
EDCS-06000335.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, listing his titles and powers.

EDCS-06000342.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare divi Vespasiani filio Domitiano Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate II imperatore III patre patriae consule VIIII Lucius Nonius Luci filius Pomptina Asprenas Luci Noni Asprenatis VIIviri epulonum proconsulis provinciae Africae III nepos triumvir aere argento auro flando feriundo sevir Salius Palatinus quaestor Caesaris Augusti centurio equitum Romanorum hastis honoratus octo vexillis IIII coronis muralibus duabus vallaribus duabus aurea una praetor inter cives et peregrinos legatus pro praetore provinciae Galateae Paphlagoniae Pamphyliae Pisidiae consul VIIvir epulonum proconsul provinciae Africae patronus municipii dedicavit legato pro praetore Marco Cornelio Firmo\n
EDCS-06000342.translation: By Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus Germanicus, son of the divine Vespasian, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, imperator III, father of the country, consul VIIII. Lucius Nonius Asprenas, son of Lucius Nonius Asprenas, member of the college of the VIIviri epulonum, proconsul of the province of Africa III, grandson of a triumvir for casting bronze, silver, and gold, sevir of the Palatine Salians, quaestor of Caesar Augustus, centurion of the Roman knights honored with eight spears and IIII vexilla, with two mural crowns, two vallar crowns, and one golden crown, praetor between citizens and foreigners, legate pro praetore of the province of Galatia, Paphlagonia, Pamphylia, Pisidia, consul VIIviri epulonum, proconsul of the province of Africa, patron of the municipality, dedicated [this monument] by the legate pro praetore Marcus Cornelius Firmus.
EDCS-06000342.summary: This extensive inscription records the dedication of a monument by Lucius Nonius Asprenas, a distinguished Roman official, in honor of Emperor Domitian, dating to Domitian's reign.

EDCS-06000343.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare divi Vespasiani filio Domitiano Augusto Germanico pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XI imperatore XXI consule XVI censore perpetuo patre patriae Tiberius Claudius Quirina Sestius Tiberi Claudi Sesti filius praefectus sacrorum flamen divi Vespasiani sufes flamen perpetuus amator patriae amator civium ornator patriae amator concordiae cui primo ordo et populus ob merita maiorum eius et ipsius lato clavo semper uti concessit podium et aram de sua pecunia facienda curavit\n
EDCS-06000343.translation: By Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus Germanicus, son of the divine Vespasian, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 11th time, imperator XXI, consul XVI, censor in perpetuity, father of the country. Tiberius Claudius Sestius, son of Tiberius Claudius Sestius of the Quirina tribe, prefect of ceremonies, flamen of the divine Vespasian, sufes, perpetual flamen, lover of his country, lover of its citizens, adornment of his country, lover of harmony, to whom first the order and the people granted the right to use the broad stripe always, for the merits of his ancestors and himself, took care to have the podium and the altar constructed at his own expense.
EDCS-06000343.summary: This inscription records the construction of a podium and altar by Tiberius Claudius Sestius at his own expense, honoring Emperor Domitian and detailing Sestius's many prestigious religious and civic offices, dating to Domitian's reign.

EDCS-06000345.translation: son of the divine Vespasian, Domitian.
EDCS-06000345.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning Emperor Domitian, son of Vespasian.

EDCS-06000350.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII consul III aquae aeternitati consuluit Quintus Servilius Candidus pecunia sua perduxit\n
EDCS-06000350.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 4th time, consul III. Quintus Servilius Candidus, at his own expense, brought the waters to eternity.
EDCS-06000350.summary: This inscription records the completion of an aqueduct project by Quintus Servilius Candidus at his own expense, in honor of Emperor Hadrian, dating to Hadrian's reign.

EDCS-06000354.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVII consuli III patri patriae Lepcitani publice per omnia conseruatori suo\n
EDCS-06000354.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 17th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, their public preserver, by the people of Lepcis.
EDCS-06000354.summary: This is a public dedication by the people of Lepcis to Emperor Hadrian, dating to his 17th tribunate and 3rd consulship.

EDCS-06000356.translation: To Trajan, by the people of Lepcis.
EDCS-06000356.summary: This is a brief public dedication by the people of Lepcis to Emperor Trajan.

EDCS-06000358.translation: To Lucius Aelius Caesar, son of Augustus.
EDCS-06000358.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Aelius Caesar, adopted son of Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-06000360.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XVI imperatore II consule IIII patre patriae templum Augusti Calpurnia Honesta sua pecunia fecit\n
EDCS-06000360.translation: Under the reign of Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 16th time, emperor for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, Calpurnia Honesta built this temple to Augustus with her own money.
EDCS-06000360.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple to Augustus by Calpurnia Honesta at her own expense, during the reign of Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-06000366.text_edition: \n Imperatori Antonino Augusto Pio Quintus Pompeius Quirina Saturninus statuam quam pro honore aedilitatis promisit posuit\n
EDCS-06000366.translation: To Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, Quintus Pompeius Quirina Saturninus erected the statue which he had promised for the honor of his aedileship.
EDCS-06000366.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue by Quintus Pompeius Quirina Saturninus to Emperor Antoninus Pius, as fulfillment of a promise made for his aedileship.

EDCS-06000368.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadr[iano Antonino Augusto Pio ------\n
EDCS-06000368.translation: To Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrian[us Antoninus Augustus Pius ------
EDCS-06000368.summary: This is a fragmented dedication to Emperor Hadrian Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-06000370.translation: To the divine Faustina, by the people of Lepcis.
EDCS-06000370.summary: This is a public dedication by the people of Lepcis to the deified Faustina.

EDCS-06000371.text_edition: \n Lucio Aelio Caesaris Antonini Augusti filio consuli II Lepticani publice\n
EDCS-06000371.translation: To Lucius Aelius Caesar, son of Caesar Antoninus Augustus, consul for the 2nd time, by the people of Leptis.
EDCS-06000371.summary: This is a public dedication by the people of Leptis to Lucius Aelius Caesar, son of Antoninus Pius, during his second consulship.

EDCS-06000373.summary: This is a heavily damaged inscription beginning with the name Pius.

EDCS-06000377.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto patri patriae tribunicia potestate V consuli II imperatori VIII conservatori orbis ob publicam et in se privatam pietatem Lepcitani publice\n
EDCS-06000377.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, father of the fatherland, endowed with tribunician power for the fifth time, consul for the second time, Imperator for the eighth time, preserver of the world, for his public and private devotion, the people of Lepcis publicly [dedicate this].
EDCS-06000377.summary: An honorific inscription dedicated by the people of Lepcis to the emperor Septimius Severus, likely dating to his reign (193-211 AD).

EDCS-06000378.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VI imperatori XI consuli II patri patriae Marcus Ulpius Cerealis procurator Auggustorum devotissimus numini eius posuit\n
EDCS-06000378.translation: Marcus Ulpius Cerealis, procurator of the Emperors, most devoted to his divine majesty, erected this.
EDCS-06000378.summary: A procurator dedicated a monument to Emperor Septimius Severus, likely during Severus' 6th tribunician power.

EDCS-06000379.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filio divi Commodi fratri divi Antonini Pii nepoti divi Hadriani pronepoti divi Traiani Parthici abnepoti divi Nervae adnepoti Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo tribunicia potestate VII imperatori XI consuli II patri patriae proconsuli Lepcitani publice\n
EDCS-06000379.translation: For Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, who holds tribunician power for the 7th time, Imperator for the 11th, Consul for the 2nd, Father of the Fatherland, proconsul, publicly by the people of Lepcis.
EDCS-06000379.summary: The citizens of Lepcis dedicated a monument to Emperor Septimius Severus, honoring his titles and authority during his 7th tribunician power.

EDCS-06000380.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XIII imperatori XI consuli II patri patriae proconsuli Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus et Marcus Calpurnius Attianus filius numini eorum devotissimi posuerunt\n
EDCS-06000380.translation: Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus and his son Marcus Calpurnius Attianus, most devoted to their divine majesty, erected this for Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, who holds tribunician power for the 13th time, Imperator for the 11th, Consul for the 2nd, Father of the Fatherland, proconsul.
EDCS-06000380.summary: A father and son dedicated a monument to Emperor Septimius Severus, acknowledging his 13th tribunician power.

EDCS-06000382.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VIIII imperatori XI consuli II patri patriae proconsuli Marcus Iunius Punicus procurator sexagenarius provinciae Thraciae centenarius Alexandriae ad Mercurium\n
EDCS-06000382.translation: Marcus Iunius Punicus, a procurator of sixty years, a centenarian in Alexandria, near the shrine of Mercury, for Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, who holds tribunician power for the 9th time, Imperator for the 11th, Consul for the 2nd, Father of the Fatherland, proconsul, governor of the province of Thrace.
EDCS-06000382.summary: Marcus Iunius Punicus, a high-ranking procurator, dedicated a monument to Emperor Septimius Severus, noting his 9th tribunician power and his governorship of Thrace.

EDCS-06000383.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate X imperatori XI patri patriae proconsuli consuli III Lepcitani Septimiani publice ob eximiam ac divinam in se indulgentiam\n
EDCS-06000383.translation: Publicly by the citizens of Lepcis Septimius Severus, for Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, who holds tribunician power for the 10th time, Imperator for the 11th, Father of the Fatherland, proconsul, Consul for the 3rd, because of his exceptional and divine indulgence towards them.
EDCS-06000383.summary: The citizens of Lepcis dedicated a monument to Emperor Septimius Severus, thanking him for his favor, and noting his 10th tribunician power.

EDCS-06000385.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filio divi Commodi fratri divi Antonini Pii nepoti divi Hadriani pronepoti divi Traiani Parthici abnepoti divi Nervae adnepoti Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XII imperatori XI consuli III patri patriae proconsuli propagatori imperi ob caelestem in se indulgentiam eius Decimus Clodius Galba procurator regionis Thevestinae et Hipponensis et procurator rationis privatae per Flaminiam Aemiliam Liguriam\n
EDCS-06000385.translation: Decimus Clodius Galba, procurator of the region of Theveste and Hippona, and procurator of the private treasury, through Flaminia Aemilia and Liguria, for Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, who holds tribunician power for the 12th time, Imperator for the 11th, Consul for the 3rd, Father of the Fatherland, proconsul, enlarger of the empire, because of his heavenly indulgence towards them.
EDCS-06000385.summary: Decimus Clodius Galba, a procurator responsible for multiple regions and the imperial treasury, dedicated a monument to Emperor Septimius Severus, recognizing his 12th tribunician power and his imperial achievements.

EDCS-06000390.text_edition: \n Septimius Severus Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Publius Septimius Geta\n
EDCS-06000390.translation: Septimius Severus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Publius Septimius Geta.
EDCS-06000390.summary: This inscription lists the names of members of the imperial family, likely Septimius Severus and his sons.

EDCS-06000392.text_edition: \n Divo Severo patri Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aurelli sic Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Parthici maximi Brittanici maximi Germanici maximi pontificis maximi tribuniciae potestatis XVIII imperatori sic III consulis IIII patris patriae Lepcitani Septimiani\n
EDCS-06000392.translation: For the deified Severus, father of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 18th time, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, by the citizens of Lepcis Septimius Severus.
EDCS-06000392.summary: The citizens of Lepcis dedicated a monument to the deified Septimius Severus, father of Caracalla, listing his numerous titles and 18th tribunician power.

EDCS-06000393.text_edition: \n Divo Pio Severo Augusto Quintus Marcius Dioga praefectus annonae sacrae urbis\n
EDCS-06000393.translation: For the deified Pius Severus Augustus, by Quintus Marcius Dioga, prefect of the grain supply of the sacred city.
EDCS-06000393.summary: Quintus Marcius Dioga, an official responsible for the grain supply, dedicated a monument to the deified Emperor Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000394.text_edition: \n Iulia Augustae matri castrorum matri Augustorum coniugi invicti Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus et Marcus Calpurnius Attianus filius numini eorum devotissimi posuerunt\n
EDCS-06000394.translation: Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus and his son Marcus Calpurnius Attianus, most devoted to their divine majesty, erected this for Julia Augusta, mother of the camps, mother of the Augusti, wife of the unconquered Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus.
EDCS-06000394.summary: A father and son dedicated a monument to Empress Julia Domna, wife of Septimius Severus, honoring her titles.

EDCS-06000395.text_edition: \n Iuliae Augustae matris sic castrorum matri Aug[gustorum] coniugi invicti Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Marcus Iunius Punicus procurator sexagenarius provinciae Thraciae centenarius Alexandriae ad Mercurium\n
EDCS-06000395.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the emperor and mother of the camp, wife of the invincible Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, Marcus Junius Punicus, procurator, sixty years old, a centenarian of Alexandria, dedicated this to Mercury.
EDCS-06000395.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Marcus Junius Punicus, a procurator, to Julia Augusta, wife of Emperor Septimius Severus, likely dating to his reign.

EDCS-06000396.text_edition: \n Iuliae Augustae matri Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aurelli sic Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et castrorum senatus et patriae Lepcitani Septimiani\n
EDCS-06000396.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the emperor, Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, and mother of the camp, of the senate, and of the fatherland, from Lepcis Septimiana.
EDCS-06000396.summary: This inscription honors Julia Augusta, mother of Emperor Caracalla, likely from Lepcis Magna.

EDCS-06000397.text_edition: \n Iuliae Augustae matri castrorum matri Aug[gustorum] coniugi invicti Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi curia Augusta numini eius devotissima posuit\n
EDCS-06000397.translation: The Curia Augusta, most devoted to her divine will, erected this to Julia Augusta, mother of the camp, mother of the emperors, wife of the invincible Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus.
EDCS-06000397.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by the Curia Augusta to Julia Augusta, wife of Emperor Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000398.text_edition: \n Iuliae Augustae matri castrorum matri Auggus[torum] coniugi invicti Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi curia Iulia numini eius devotissima posuit\n
EDCS-06000398.translation: The Curia Iulia, most devoted to her divine will, erected this to Julia Augusta, mother of the camp, mother of the emperors, wife of the invincible Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus.
EDCS-06000398.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by the Curia Iulia to Julia Augusta, wife of Emperor Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000399.text_edition: \n Iuliae Augustae matri Augusto[rum] et castrorum Decimus Clodius Galba procurator\n
EDCS-06000399.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of the emperors and of the camp, Decimus Clodius Galba, procurator.
EDCS-06000399.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Decimus Clodius Galba, a procurator, to Julia Augusta, mother of emperors, likely during the reign of Septimius Severus or his sons.

EDCS-06000400.text_edition: \n Iuliae Augustae matri Auggustorum et castrorum Messius Atticus centurio cohortis VII praetoriae piae vindicis votum solvit\n
EDCS-06000400.translation: Messius Atticus, centurion of the VII Praetorian cohort, pious defender, fulfilled his vow to Julia Augusta, mother of the emperors and of the camp.
EDCS-06000400.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Messius Atticus, a praetorian centurion, to Julia Augusta, mother of emperors.

EDCS-06000401.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that likely refers to Julia Augusta.

EDCS-06000402.text_edition: \n Pacciae Marcianae quondam uxoris sic domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Lepcitani Septimiani publice ex voto posuerunt\n
EDCS-06000402.translation: The communities, acting publicly and by vow, erected this to Paccia Marciana, formerly wife of our lord Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus of Lepcis Septimiana.
EDCS-06000402.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by communities to Paccia Marciana, the former wife of Emperor Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000403.text_edition: \n Pacciae Marcianae quondam uxoris domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi curiae tres Plotina et Nervia et Matidia posuerunt\n
EDCS-06000403.translation: The three curiae, Plotina, Nervia, and Matidia, erected this to Paccia Marciana, formerly wife of our lord Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus.
EDCS-06000403.summary: This inscription records a dedication by three curiae to Paccia Marciana, former wife of Emperor Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000404.text_edition: \n Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi tribunicia potestate X imperatoris XI consulis III patris patriae proconsulis avo domini nostri Lucio Septimio Severo sufeti praefecto publice creato cum primum civitas Romana adacta est duumviro flamini perpetuo in decuriis et inter selectos Romae iudicavit Lepcitani publice\n
EDCS-06000404.translation: The people of Lepcis publicly judged [this monument] for Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 10th time, acclaimed imperator for the 11th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, grandson of our lord Lucius Septimius Severus, a former sufes and prefect appointed publicly when the Roman citizenship was first imposed, and perpetual flamen in the decuries and among the selected officials in Rome.
EDCS-06000404.summary: This inscription details the public honors granted by the people of Lepcis to Emperor Septimius Severus, listing his titles and family lineage, likely dating to his later reign.

EDCS-06000405.text_edition: \n Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi tribunicia potestate XI imperatoris XII consulis III proconsulis Lucio Septimio Severo flamini perpetuo avo curiae duae Traiana Dacica ex voto statuerunt\n
EDCS-06000405.translation: The two Trajanic Dacian curiae, with vows fulfilled, erected this to Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, holder of tribunician power for the 11th time, imperator for the 12th time, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, and to Lucius Septimius Severus, eternal flamens, and to his grandfather.
EDCS-06000405.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by two curiae to Emperor Septimius Severus, likely during his 11th tribunician power, commemorating his titles and achievements.

EDCS-06000406.text_edition: \n Publio Septimio Getae patri domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate VIIII imperatoris XI consulis II patris patriae proconsulis curia Nervia ex voto statuit\n
EDCS-06000406.translation: The Nervian curia, with vow fulfilled, erected this to Publius Septimius Geta, father of our lord Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 9th time, imperator for the 11th time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country, proconsul.
EDCS-06000406.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by the Nervian curia to Publius Septimius Geta, father of Septimius Severus, honoring his titles.

EDCS-06000407.text_edition: \n Fulviae Piae matris sic domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi Lepcitani Septimiani publice ex voto posuerunt\n
EDCS-06000407.translation: The citizens of Lepcis, with vows fulfilled, erected this to Fulvia Pia, mother of our lord Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia.
EDCS-06000407.summary: This inscription records a public vow fulfilled by the citizens of Lepcis to Fulvia Pia, mother of Emperor Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000408.text_edition: \n Fulviae Piae matri domini nostri Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi curiae tres Severae Pia et Ulpia et Augusta ex voto posuerunt\n
EDCS-06000408.translation: The three Severan curiae, Pia, Ulpia, and Augusta, with vows fulfilled, erected this to Fulvia Pia, mother of our lord Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia.
EDCS-06000408.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by three curiae, Pia, Ulpia, and Augusta, to Fulvia Pia, mother of Emperor Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000409.text_edition: \n Septimiae Octavillae sorori Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi clarissimae memoriae feminae curiae tres Iulia et Germanica et Marciana posuerunt\n
EDCS-06000409.translation: The three curiae, Julia, Germanica, and Marciana, erected this to Septimia Octavilla, sister of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, a woman of most illustrious memory.
EDCS-06000409.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by three curiae to Septimia Octavilla, sister of Emperor Septimius Severus, honoring her memory.

EDCS-06000410.text_edition: \n Marco Aurellio sic Antonino Caesari imperatori destinato Imperatoris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici filio Lepcitani publice\n
EDCS-06000410.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Caesar, designated emperor, son of Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, of Lepcis.
EDCS-06000410.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (later Caracalla) as designated Caesar and son of Septimius Severus, originating from Lepcis.

EDCS-06000411.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Augusto tribunicia potestate III Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus et Marcus Calpurnius Attianus filius numini eorum devotissimi posuerunt\n
EDCS-06000411.translation: Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus and his son Marcus Calpurnius Attianus, most devoted to their divine majesty, erected this to Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, holder of tribunician power for the 3rd time, son of the most fortunate Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, father of the country.
EDCS-06000411.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Marcus Calpurnius Geta Attianus and his son to Emperor Caracalla, celebrating his 3rd tribunician power and filial relationship to Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000412.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Pio tribuniciae potestatis IIII proconsuli Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio curia Pia Severiana ex voto statuit\n
EDCS-06000412.translation: The Pia Severiana curia, with vow fulfilled, erected this to Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, holder of tribunician power for the 4th time, proconsul, son of the most fortunate Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, father of the country.
EDCS-06000412.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by the Pia Severiana curia to Emperor Caracalla, honoring his 4th tribunician power and his status as son of Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000413.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Pio tribuniciae potestatis IIII procons(uli) Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio curia Ulpia ex voto statuit\n
EDCS-06000413.translation: The Ulpia curia, with vow fulfilled, erected this to Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, holder of tribunician power for the 4th time, proconsul, son of the most fortunate Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, father of the country.
EDCS-06000413.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by the Ulpia curia to Emperor Caracalla, honoring his 4th tribunician power and his status as son of Septimius Severus.

EDCS-06000414.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Pio tribuniciae potestatis IIII proconsuli Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio Marcus Iunius Punicus procurator sexagenarius provinciae Thraciae centenarius Alexandriae ad Mercurium\n
EDCS-06000414.translation: Marcus Iunius Punicus, a procurator of sixty years, a centenarius of Alexandria, to Mercury, erected this to Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, holder of tribunician power for the 4th time, proconsul, son of the most fortunate Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, father of the country, [in the province] of Thrace.
EDCS-06000414.summary: This inscription records an offering made by Marcus Iunius Punicus, a high-ranking official, to Emperor Caracalla, likely during his 4th tribunician power, alongside a dedication to Mercury.

EDCS-06000416.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Severi Augusti filio divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nep(oti) divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti diui Traiani Parthici et divi Neruae adnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici tribunicia potestate VII consuli designato II proconsuli propagatori imperi ob caelestem in se indulgentiam eius Decimus Clodius Galba procurator regionis Thevestinae et Hipponensis et procurator rationis privatae per Flaminiam Aemiliam Liguriam\n
EDCS-06000416.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, grandson of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, who holds tribunician power for the seventh time, consul-designate for the second time, proconsul, preserver of the empire, on account of his divine favor towards them, Decimus Clodius Galba, procurator of the region of Theveste and Hippona, and procurator of the imperial treasury for Flaminia, Aemilia, and Liguria.
EDCS-06000416.summary: This dedication honors Emperor Caracalla for his divine favor, set up by Decimus Clodius Galba, a procurator, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000418.text_edition: \n Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio Clementis viri egregi filio a militiis civi patrono ordo splendidissimus Lepcimagnensium\n
EDCS-06000418.translation: To the most distinguished city council of Lepcimagnus, as patron, son of the most excellent man, Clement, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country.
EDCS-06000418.summary: This fragmentary inscription honors Clement, son of Emperor Septimius Severus, as a patron by the council of Lepcimagnus, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000424.text_edition: \n Publio Septimio Getae Caesari Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio Marcus Iunius Punicus procurator sexagenarius provinciae Thraciae centenarius Alexandriae ad Mercurium\n
EDCS-06000424.translation: To Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country, Marcus Junius Punicus, a sexagenarian procurator of the province of Thrace, a centenarian in Alexandria, at the shrine of Mercury.
EDCS-06000424.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Geta Caesar by Marcus Junius Punicus, a procurator, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000425.text_edition: \n Publio Septimio Getae Caesari Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio Lepcitani Septimiani publice ex voto posuerunt\n
EDCS-06000425.translation: The inhabitants of Leptis Septimiana, by public vote, set up this monument to Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country.
EDCS-06000425.summary: This inscription records a public votive offering by the people of Leptis Septimiana to Geta Caesar, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000426.text_edition: \n Publio Septimio Getae Caesari Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae filio curia Matidia ex voto statuit\n
EDCS-06000426.translation: The Curia Matidia has erected this monument by votive offering to Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country.
EDCS-06000426.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by the Curia Matidia to Geta Caesar, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000428.text_edition: \n Lucio Septimio Getae Caesari Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filio et Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aurelli sic Antonini Augusti fratri Messius Atticus primus sacerdos votum solvit\n
EDCS-06000428.translation: Messius Atticus, the first priest, has fulfilled his vow to Lucius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, and brother of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus.
EDCS-06000428.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Messius Atticus, a priest, to Geta Caesar and his brother Caracalla, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000429.text_edition: \n Publio Septimio [Getae] Caesari Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi fortissimi felicissimi patris patriae filio et Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aurelli sic Antonino sic Augusti tribunicia potestate proconsulis fratri Messius Atticus centurio cohortis X urbanae votum solvit\n
EDCS-06000429.translation: Messius Atticus, a centurion of the 10th Urban Cohort, has fulfilled his vow to Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most brave, most fortunate, father of the country, and brother of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, who holds tribunician power and is proconsul.
EDCS-06000429.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Messius Atticus, a centurion, to Geta Caesar and his brother Caracalla, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000434.text_edition: \n Publio Septimio Getae Augusto Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi felicissimi patris patriae [filio] Quintus Pompeius Cerealis Felix procurator Augggustorum devotissimus numini eius posuit\n
EDCS-06000434.translation: Quintus Pompeius Cerealis Felix, most devoted to his divine authority, has erected this monument to Publius Septimius Geta Augustus, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Arabicus, Adiabenicus, Parthicus Maximus, most fortunate, father of the country, procurator of the Augusti.
EDCS-06000434.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by Quintus Pompeius Cerealis Felix, a procurator, to Geta Caesar, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000441.translation: The inhabitants of Leptis Septimiana, by public vote...
EDCS-06000441.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a public dedication by the people of Leptis Septimiana, dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06000443.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Caio Iulio Vero Maximino Pio Felici Augusto patri patriae tribuniciae potestatis II consuli proconsuli conservatori orbis Lepcitani Septimiani publice\n
EDCS-06000443.translation: The inhabitants of Leptis Septimiana, by public vote, to Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, father of the country, who holds tribunician power for the second time, consul, proconsul, preserver of the world.
EDCS-06000443.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the people of Leptis Septimiana to Emperor Maximinus Thrax, likely from his reign (235-238 CE).

EDCS-06000445.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate I Lepcitani publice Domino nostro invicto ac nobilissimo Caesari Flavio Valerio Severo Lepcitani devoti numini maiestatique eius publice\n
EDCS-06000445.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, from the people of Lepcis, devoted to the divine majesty of our unconquered and most noble Caesar Flavius Valerius Severus.
EDCS-06000445.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Gordian III and Caesar Severus, dedicated by the citizens of Lepcis.

EDCS-06000447.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Publio Licinio Gallieno Germanico Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo Germanico maximo tribunicia potestate XIII imperatori XII consuli VI patri patriae proconsuli et Corneliae Saloninae sanctissimae Augustae nostrae coniugi Gallieni Augusti nostri Lepcitani Septimiani Saloniniani publice\n
EDCS-06000447.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus Germanicus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, Germanicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the 13th time, Imperator for the 12th time, Consul for the 6th time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, and to Cornelia Salonina, our most sacred Augusta, wife of Gallienus Augustus, from the people of Lepcis Septimiani Saloniniani.
EDCS-06000447.summary: This inscription dedicates honors to Emperor Gallienus and Empress Salonina from the people of Lepcis, dating to approximately 265-268 AD based on titles.

EDCS-06000451.text_edition: \n Publio Cornelio Licinio Valeriano nobilissimo Caesari Lepcitani Septimiani publice\n
EDCS-06000451.translation: To Publius Cornelius Licinius Valerianus, most noble Caesar, from the people of Lepcis Septimiani.
EDCS-06000451.summary: This inscription dedicates honors to Caesar Valerianus II (likely Gallienus' son) from the citizens of Lepcis.

EDCS-06000452.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely honored a Publius Cornelius, with the rest of the text missing.

EDCS-06000453.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Caro Pio Felici invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli II patri patriae proconsuli Lucius Iulius Paulinus vir clarissimus proconsul \n
EDCS-06000453.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus Pius Felix unconquered Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, Consul for the 2nd time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, from Lucius Julius Paulinus, most distinguished man, proconsul.
EDCS-06000453.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Carus by Lucius Julius Paulinus, the proconsul, likely in 282-283 AD.

EDCS-06000454.text_edition: \n Fortissimis et invictissimis imperatoribus ddominis nnostris Marco Flavio Valerio Constantio Pio Felici invicto Augusto et Caio Galerio Valerio Maximiano Pio Felici invicto Augusto Lepcitani devoti numini maiestatique eorum publice\n
EDCS-06000454.translation: To our most brave and unconquered Lords Emperors Marcus Flavius Valerius Constantius Pius Felix unconquered Augustus and Gaius Galerius Valerius Maximianus Pius Felix unconquered Augustus, from the people of Lepcis, devoted to their divine majesty.
EDCS-06000454.summary: This inscription honors Emperors Constantius I and Maximian, dedicated by the citizens of Lepcis, likely dating to the Tetrarchy period (late 3rd to early 4th century AD).

EDCS-06000456.text_edition: \n Indulgentissimo ac libertatis restitutori victoriosissimoque imperatori domino nostro Maxentio Pio Felici invicto Augusto Valerius Alexander vir perfectissimus agens vices praefecti praetorio numini maiestatique eius dicatissimus\n
EDCS-06000456.translation: To our most indulgent and restorer of liberty, most victorious Emperor, our Lord Maxentius Pius Felix unconquered Augustus, from Valerius Alexander, most perfect man, acting in place of the praetorian prefect, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-06000456.summary: This inscription dedicates honors to Emperor Maxentius by Valerius Alexander, acting praetorian prefect, likely from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-06000457.text_edition: \n Indulgentissimo ac libertatis restitutori victorioriosissimoque Imperatori domino nostro Maxentio Pio Felici Invicto Augusto Volusius Donatianus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Tripolitanae numini maiestatique eius dicatissimus\n
EDCS-06000457.translation: To our most indulgent and restorer of liberty, most victorious Emperor, our Lord Maxentius Pius Felix Unconquered Augustus, from Volusius Donatianus, most perfect man, governor of the province of Tripolitana, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-06000457.summary: This inscription dedicates honors to Emperor Maxentius by Volusius Donatianus, governor of Tripolitana, likely from the early 4th century AD.
