EDCS-10300631.summary: A public work commissioned by Roman citizens, veterans, and pagans from the district of Fortunalis, whose ancestors benefited from land grants under Augustus, for Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, dating to his reign (c. 193-211 AD).

EDCS-10300636.text_edition: \n Augusto deo cives Romani qui Thinissut negotiantur curatore Lucio Fabricio\n
EDCS-10300636.translation: To the deified Augustus, by the Roman citizens who trade in Thinissut, under the curator Lucius Fabricius.
EDCS-10300636.summary: A dedication to the deified Augustus by Roman merchants of Thinissut, overseen by Lucius Fabricius, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10300871.translation: Marcus Ulpius Menophoon, freedman of Augustus, assistant to Licinius Maximus and Felicior, procurators of Augustus, has paid his vow to the genius of the Trajanic aqueducts.
EDCS-10300871.summary: A votive offering to the genius of the Trajanic aqueducts by Marcus Ulpius Menophoon, an assistant to imperial procurators, likely during the Trajanic or Hadrianic period.

EDCS-10300894.text_edition: \n Lucio Iulio Luci filio Quirina Cereali quaestori aedili praefecto iure dicundo flamini Augusti perpetuo municipi sui flamini Augusti provinciae Africae anni XXXX quem honorem ex municipio suo Bullensium Regiorum primus gessit huic cum provinciae Africae leg\n
EDCS-10300894.translation: To Lucius Iulius Cerealis, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, quaestor, aedile, prefect for dispensing justice, perpetual Augustan flamen of his municipality, Augustan flamen of the province of Africa for 30 years, an honor which he was the first from his municipality of Bulla Regia to hold, this man, along with the province of Africa, [received]...
EDCS-10300894.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Iulius Cerealis with various civic and religious titles and outlines his distinguished career serving his municipality and the province of Africa, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10301026.text_edition: \n Sextiliae Clodiae Fadianianillae filiae Luci Sextili Felicis Clodiani decurionis coloniae Antonianae Karthaginiensis res publica Thibaritanorum ob merita patris eius decreto decurionum pecunia publica dedicata ex VI Kalendas Ianuarias Modesto II et Probo consulibus\n
EDCS-10301026.translation: For Sextilia Clodia Fadianilla, daughter of Lucius Sextilius Felix Clodianus, decurion of the colony of Antoniana Karthago, the community of Thibaritanorum dedicated this from public funds, on the decree of the decurions, on account of the merits of her father, starting from the 6th day before the Kalends of January, when Modestus II and Probus were consuls.
EDCS-10301026.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by the community of Thibaritanorum to Sextilia Clodia Fadianilla, honoring the merits of her father, Lucius Sextilius Felix Clodianus, and is dated by the consulship of Modestus II and Probus in the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10301035.translation: For the safety of the Emperors Caesar [Lucius] Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, father of the fatherland, and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix [Augustus], and [Publius] Septimius Geta Augustus, and Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the Augusti and of the camps and of their entire divine household, the community of the municipium of Septimius Aurelius Liber, Thugga...
EDCS-10301035.summary: This inscription is a dedication for the safety of the ruling emperors, including Septimius Severus, Caracalla, Geta, and Julia Domna, by the community of the municipium of Thugga, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10702021.text_edition: \n Impperatoribus Diocletiano et Maximiano sennioribus Auggustis et Impperatoribus Constantio et Maximiano Auggustis et Severo et Maximino nobilissimis Caesaribus Marcus Valerius Victor vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Mauretaniae Caesariensis novas portas turresque dedicavit provinciae CCLXVII\n
EDCS-10702021.translation: Under the Emperors Diocletian and Maximian, elder Augusti, and the Emperors Constantius and Maximian, Augusti, and Severus and Maximinus, most noble Caesars, Marcus Valerius Victor, most perfect man, praeses of the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, dedicated new gates and towers, in the province in the year 267.
EDCS-10702021.summary: This inscription records the dedication of new gates and towers by Marcus Valerius Victor, the governor of Mauretania Caesariensis, under a tetrarchic imperial administration, dated to 267 (likely from the founding of the province or a regnal year).

EDCS-10900694.translation: Of Marcus Laurentius Silanus, the father.
EDCS-10900694.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that likely records the name of Marcus Laurentius Silanus, father, possibly as part of a funerary monument or a dedication.

EDCS-10900697.summary: This inscription simply records the name Tiberius Claudius Orfitus, likely as an owner or dedicatee of a monument.

EDCS-10900698.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus Aristonis Adrumucus sua pecunia fecit Diodorus Adrumucus frater curavit\n
EDCS-10900698.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aristo Adrumucus made this at his own expense. Diodorus Adrumucus, his brother, arranged it.
EDCS-10900698.summary: This funerary inscription records that Aristo Adrumucus commissioned his tomb at his own expense, and his brother Diodorus Adrumucus oversaw its construction.

EDCS-10900699.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus fecit Marcus Eppi<us=I> sibi et suis et patrona sua \n
EDCS-10900699.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Eppius made this for himself and for his family and for his patroness.
EDCS-10900699.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Marcus Eppius dedicated a tomb for himself, his family, and his patroness.

EDCS-10900700.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Luciae Siliae Luci filiae Piae Lucius Silius Plautius Haterianus vir clarissimus patruus heres fecit\n
EDCS-10900700.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Lucia Silia Pia, daughter of Lucius. Lucius Silius Plautius Haterianus, a most distinguished man, his uncle and heir, made this.
EDCS-10900700.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucia Silia Pia and states that the monument was erected by her uncle and heir, Lucius Silius Plautius Haterianus.

EDCS-10900709.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aemilia Decimi filia Aprulla pia vixit annis LXXXXIII mensibus II hic sita est Dis Manibus sacrum Nabor Felicis Balsillecis Maraxae pius vixit nnos LXXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-10900709.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aemilia Aprulla, daughter of Decimus, of the Balsillecis family, pious, lived 93 years and 2 months. She is buried here. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Nabor Felicis Maraxus, pious, lived 80 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-10900709.summary: This funerary inscription records the burials and lifespans of two individuals, Aemilia Aprulla and Nabor Felicis Maraxus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10900710.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Iulius Sanus Maximinus vixit annum menses III horas IIII caementarius hic situs\n
EDCS-10900710.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Iulius Sanus Maximinus lived for one year, three months, and four hours; a builder, buried here.
EDCS-10900710.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Publius Iulius Sanus Maximinus, a builder, with details of his age at death.

EDCS-10900738.text_edition: \n Clodius Quartus filius ex Taeis vixit annis XXV posuit pietatis causa ex |(centurione) Caius Iulius hic situs est\n
EDCS-10900738.translation: Clodius Quartus, son of Taia, lived for 25 years. Caius Iulius, buried here, erected this out of filial piety. He was a centurion.
EDCS-10900738.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Clodius Quartus, erected by Caius Iulius, a former centurion, out of filial piety.

EDCS-11300842.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a tombstone, commemorating Corneli Bassus.

EDCS-11300858.translation: Of Lucius Silius Amicus, a most distinguished man.
EDCS-11300858.summary: This inscription likely records the name and status of Lucius Silius Amicus, possibly a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-11300863.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Marcus Junius Crescens, possibly a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-11300865.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Geminius Orfitianus miles cohortis XI urbanae vixit annis XXXVIIII hic situs est Geminius Restutus filio pio posuit Dis Manibus sacrum Sornia Maxima vixit annis XXXVI hic sita est Geminius Restutus matri pie posuit Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Geminius Pusissus vixit annis LVII hic situs est Geminius Restutus patri posuit Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Geminius Salfenius Quirina vixit annis L filii patri optimo pio posuerunt hic situs est\n
EDCS-11300865.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Geminius Orfitianus, a soldier of the 11th Urban Cohort, lived for 39 years; he is buried here. Geminius Restutus erected this for his pious son. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sornia Maxima lived for 36 years; she is buried here. Geminius Restutus erected this for his pious mother. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Geminius Pusissus lived for 57 years; he is buried here. Geminius Restutus erected this for his excellent and pious father. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Geminius Salfenius and Quirina erected this for their most excellent and pious father; he is buried here.
EDCS-11300865.summary: This extensive funerary inscription records the deaths and burial places of multiple family members, with Geminius Restutus acting as the dedicator for his father, mother, and son.

EDCS-11300870.text_edition: \n Cal Fol Dis Manibus sacrum Mevius Ingenuus vixit nnos XXXIII hic situs est Marcus Mevius Campesis veteranus legionis III Augustae piae vindicis fratri pio posuit Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Mevius Campesis veteranus legionis III Augustae piae vindicis vixit nnos ipse se vivo fecit\n
EDCS-11300870.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Mevius Ingenuus lived for 33 years; he is buried here. Marcus Mevius Campesis, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, pious and victorious, erected this for his pious brother. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Mevius Campesis, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, pious and victorious, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-11300870.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Mevius Ingenuus, dedicated by his brother, and also records a self-made monument by Marcus Mevius Campesis, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-11300873.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fabia Dativa vixit annis XXXX hic sita est Marcus Mevius Campesis veteranus legionis III Augustae piae vindicis uxori piae fecit\n
EDCS-11300873.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fabia Dativa lived for 40 years; she is buried here. Marcus Mevius Campesis, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, pious and victorious, made this for his pious wife.
EDCS-11300873.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Fabia Dativa, erected by her husband, Marcus Mevius Campesis, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion.

EDCS-11300875.text_edition: \n Lucius Pacuvius Servatus veteranus legionis III Augustae vixit nnos LXXV militavit nnos hic situs est Pacuvii Felix et Claudia filii patri poserunt\n
EDCS-11300875.translation: Lucius Pacuvius Servatus, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, lived for 75 years; he served [for X years]; he is buried here. His children, Pacuvii Felix and Claudia, erected this for their father.
EDCS-11300875.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and military service of Lucius Pacuvius Servatus, a veteran, erected by his children.

EDCS-11300876.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Allius Iucundinus vixit annos XLV hic situs est Ianuaria uxor marito amantissimo pio posuit Dis Manibus sacrum Petilia Romana vixit annos LXV hic sita est Allius Fortunatio uxori piae posuit IVVIT Dis Manibus sacrum Petilius Rusticus miles praetorianus |(duplicarius qui vixit annos XXVIII Allius Fortunatio pater filio p(osuit)\n
EDCS-11300876.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Allius Iucundinus lived for 45 years; he is buried here. Ianuaria, his wife, erected this for her most loving and pious husband. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Petilia Romana lived for 65 years; she is buried here. Allius Fortunatio erected this for his pious wife. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Petilius Rusticus, a praetorian soldier, a duplicarius, lived for 28 years. Allius Fortunatio, his father, erected this for his son.
EDCS-11300876.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Allius Iucundinus and Petilia Romana, with dedications by their spouses, and the death of Petilius Rusticus, a praetorian soldier, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-11300879.text_edition: \n Lucius Regilius Titi filius Galeria Luguduno cornicen legionis III Augustae |(centuria) Gennii Maioris vixit annis X militavit annos XIX hic situs est cornicines commilitoni posuerunt\n
EDCS-11300879.translation: To Lucius Regilius, son of Titus, of the Galeria tribe, from Lugdunum, a cornicen of the Third Augustan Legion, in the century of Gnaeus Maior. He lived 10 years, served 19 years. He is buried here. His fellow soldiers erected this for their comrade.
EDCS-11300879.summary: This funerary monument records Lucius Regilius, a cornicen of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his fellow soldiers.

EDCS-11300880.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Octaviae Sterceiae Lucius Sempronius Liberalis signifer legionis III Augustae post annos XVI stipendiorum matri posuit\n
EDCS-11300880.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Sempronius Liberalis, standard-bearer of the Third Augustan Legion, erected this for his mother, Octavia Sterceia, after 16 years of service.
EDCS-11300880.summary: This funerary monument is dedicated by Lucius Sempronius Liberalis, a standard-bearer of the Third Augustan Legion, to his mother Octavia Sterceia.

EDCS-11300881.text_edition: \n miles legionis III Augustae centuria Volusi hic situs est militauit annos XVI vixit annos XXXV Caius Iulius Rufus comanupularis de suo curavit\n
EDCS-11300881.translation: A soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, in the century of Volusius. He is buried here. He served 16 years, lived 35 years. Caius Julius Rufus, a comrade, took care of this from his own funds.
EDCS-11300881.summary: This funerary monument commemorates a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, who served for 16 years and lived for 35 years, with the expenses covered by a fellow soldier named Caius Julius Rufus.

EDCS-11300884.text_edition: \n Gaius Iulius Africanus i C vixsit sic nnos LI Gaius Iulius Dux |(centurio) pater \n
EDCS-11300884.translation: Gaius Julius Africanus lived 51 years. His father, Gaius Julius Dux, a centurion, [erected this].
EDCS-11300884.summary: This funerary monument records the life of Gaius Julius Africanus, who lived 51 years, and was dedicated by his father, a centurion.

EDCS-11300891.text_edition: \n Frugifero Augusto sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Parthici Arabici Parthici Adiabenici pontificis maximi patris patriae decuriones cives Romani aedem de sportulis fecerunt idemque dedicaverunt\n
EDCS-11300891.translation: Sacred to the fruitful Augustus. For the health of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Parthicus Arabicus, Parthicus Adiabenicus, pontifex maximus, father of the fatherland. The decurions and Roman citizens built the temple from their contributions and dedicated it.
EDCS-11300891.summary: This inscription records the construction and dedication of a temple to Emperor Septimius Severus by the decurions and citizens for his health.

EDCS-11500953.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Aelius Saturninus lived 7 years.
EDCS-11500953.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Lucius Aelius Saturninus, who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-11600228.text_edition: \n Faustino Felicis Sidiathonis filio flamini Augusti perpetuo civitas Thuggensis ob meritum\n
EDCS-11600228.translation: To Faustinus, son of Felix Sidiathon, perpetual flamen of Augustus. The community of Thugga [honors him] for his merit.
EDCS-11600228.summary: The community of Thugga honors Faustinus, son of Felix Sidiathon and perpetual flamen of Augustus, for his merit.

EDCS-11600230.text_edition: \n L(ucio) Calpurnio Felici civitas Thuggensis ob merita et ipsius et patris eius Faustini curatoribus Iulio Rogato Mucio Kapitone\n
EDCS-11600230.translation: To Lucius Calpurnius Felix. The community of Thugga [honors him] for his merits and those of his father Faustinus, with Julius Rogatus Mucio Capito as curators.
EDCS-11600230.summary: The community of Thugga honors Lucius Calpurnius Felix and his father Faustinus for their merits, with specific curators mentioned.

EDCS-11600235.text_edition: \n Mercurio Augusto sacrum Lucius Liconius Rogatianus \n
EDCS-11600235.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Lucius Liconius Rogatianus [erected this].
EDCS-11600235.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication to Mercury Augustus, likely erected by Lucius Liconius Rogatianus.

EDCS-11600240.text_edition: \n Aulus Gabinius Quirina Datus patronus pagi et civitatis Thuggensis Marcus Gabinius Quirina Bassus flamen Augusti perpetuus patronus pagi et civitatis nomine suo et Auli Gabini Dati filii in quinque decuriis ab Imperatore Caesare Traiano Hadriano Augusto adlecto sic patroni pagi et civitatis templa solo suo a fundamentis sua pecunia struxerunt itemque dedicaverunt Aulus Gabinius Quirina Datus patronus pagi et civitatis Thuggensis Marcus Gabinius Quirina Bassus flamen Augusti perpetuus patronus pagi et civitatis templa Concordiae Frugiferi Liberi Patris cum reliquis templis et xystis solo suo a fundamentis sua pecunia struxerunt in quod opus cum HS Lmilia promisissent multiplicata pecunia consummaverunt\n
EDCS-11600240.translation: Aulus Gabinius Datus, Quirina tribe, patron of the pagus and community of Thugga, and Marcus Gabinius Bassus, Quirina tribe, perpetual flamen of Augustus, patron of the pagus and community, in his own name and that of his son Aulus Gabinius Datus, who was appointed to the five decuries by the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, [state that] as patrons of the pagus and community, they built the temples of Concordia and Liber Pater Frugiferus, along with other temples and xysti, on their own land from the foundations with their own money, and likewise dedicated them; although they promised 50,000 sesterces for this work, they completed it with the money multiplied.
EDCS-11600240.summary: This inscription details the construction and dedication of several temples, including those of Concordia and Liber Pater Frugiferus, by patrons Aulus Gabinius Datus and Marcus Gabinius Bassus for the community of Thugga, funded substantially from their own resources.

EDCS-11600241.text_edition: \n Lucio Tadio Luci filio Horatia Vero Caeciliano praefecto iure dicundo aedili quaestori equo publico et in quinque decurias adlecto sacerdoti nni CLXXX Uchitani Maiores patrono ob merita\n
EDCS-11600241.translation: To Lucius Tadius Verus Caecilianus, son of Lucius, of the Horatia tribe, who served as praefect, judge, aedile, quaestor, was a member of the equestrian order and appointed to one of the five decuries, priest of the Uchitani Maiores, for his merits as patron.
EDCS-11600241.summary: This inscription is a dedication honoring Lucius Tadius Verus Caecilianus for his public service and patronage, likely erected by the Uchitani Maiores community.

EDCS-11600249.summary: This is a short religious dedication to the goddess Laverna.

EDCS-11600252.text_edition: \n Augusto s(acrum) Cai filius Felix et Mucia instar decurionatus amplius pecunia a se adiecta\n
EDCS-11600252.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Gaius Felix, son of Gaius, and Mucia added to the wealth of the decurionate with money from their own resources.
EDCS-11600252.summary: This inscription records a financial contribution made by Gaius Felix and Mucia to enhance the status or resources of the decurionate, dedicated to Augustus.

EDCS-11600253.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Allius Victorinus vixit annos XI\n
EDCS-11600253.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Allius Victorinus lived 11 years.
EDCS-11600253.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Marcus Allius Victorinus, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-11600254.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Ambia Fortunata pia vixit annis \n
EDCS-11600254.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Ambia Fortunata, dutiful, lived [x] years.
EDCS-11600254.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and piety of Ambia Fortunata, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-11600259.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Durvius Felix pius vixit annos XXXVII hic situs est\n
EDCS-11600259.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Durvius Felix, dutiful, lived 37 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-11600259.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, age, and burial place of Gaius Durvius Felix, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-11600260.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Durvius Fortunatus pius vixit annis XII\n
EDCS-11600260.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Durvius Fortunatus, dutiful, lived 12 years.
EDCS-11600260.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Quintus Durvius Fortunatus, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-11600261.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Erennia Casta pia vixit annos LXXXXI hic sita est hic sita est\n
EDCS-11600261.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Erennia Casta, dutiful, lived 91 years. She is buried here. She is buried here.
EDCS-11600261.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, age, and burial place of Erennia Casta, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-11600263.text_edition: \n Caius Licinius Beryllus pius vixit annos\n
EDCS-11600263.translation: Gaius Licinius Beryllus, dutiful, lived [x] years.
EDCS-11600263.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Gaius Licinius Beryllus, likely funerary or commemorative.

EDCS-11600265.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Manlius Manlianus vixit annos LXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-11600265.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Manlius Manlianus lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-11600265.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, age, and burial place of Quintus Manlius Manlianus, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-11600291.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a person named Espedita, likely from a tombstone or similar marker.

EDCS-11600295.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a person named Sorica, likely from a tombstone or similar marker.

EDCS-11600296.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for a person named Gratiosus, likely from a tombstone or similar marker.

EDCS-11600297.text_edition: \n Haec memoria quondam M Nicasiae scolasticae quae vixit annis XX mensibus histt\n
EDCS-11600297.translation: This memorial to Nicasia, the scolastica, who lived 20 years and some months.
EDCS-11600297.summary: This inscription is a memorial for Nicasia, a 'scolastica' (possibly a teacher or educated woman), recording her name and lifespan.

EDCS-11700293.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Caelei Tharridae Faustiani vixit nnos XVIII semissem hic situs est\n
EDCS-11700293.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Caleius Tharrida Faustianus lived 18 and a half years; he is buried here.
EDCS-11700293.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Caleius Tharrida Faustianus, recording his name, lifespan, and burial.

EDCS-12500342.summary: This inscription commemorates a woman identified by the initials ARH, described as 'most distinguished'.

EDCS-12500357.text_edition: \n Vol Calassini kentenaria sic X Caprorenses kentenaria sic DCVII fieri kentenaria sic CCCXIIII R CCLIIII et erunt in conditorio in die XIIII Kalendas Martias centenaria DCCCCXXXI fieri in conditorio |(centenaria) |milleCLXXX XIII Kalendas Martias ad Octobrem |(centenarius) Maurus pondo libras XXXV |(centenarius) Ianuarius pondo libras LV |(centenarius) Felix pondo libras VIIICCXXVI |(centenarius) Vindemialis pondo libras CCC |(centenarius) Victor Mercurialis pondo libras |(duo milia)XCVII |(centenarius) Augustalis pondo libras |milleCCCXCVIII |(centenarius) Maioricus pondo libras |(duo milia)XCVII |(centenarius) Marianus pondo libras IIIIDCLXXVII |(centenarius) Primus pondo libras IIIIDCLX |(centenarius) Murc pondo libras IIICCCCXXV |(centenarius) Evasius pondo libras |milleDCCCLXXII |(centenarius) Restutus pondo libras IIIIXXII |(centenarius) Victorinus pondo libras |(mille) DCCXCIIII fieri numero CCCL pondo libras LXXVIII Vol Calassini kentenaria sic LXXVI\n
EDCS-12500357.translation: Vol(umen) Calassini: 100,000 units. Caprorenses: 607 units. To be made: 314 units. 254. And there will be in the tomb on the 14th day before the Kalends of March, 931 units. To be made in the tomb: 1180 units. On the 18th day before the Kalends of March. At October: Maurus, 35 pounds. Ianuarius, 55 pounds. Felix, 2820 pounds. Vindemialis, 300 pounds. Victor Mercurialis, 2097 pounds. Augustalis, 1398 pounds. Maioricus, 2097 pounds. Marianus, 4677 pounds. Primus, 4660 pounds. Murc, 2425 pounds. Evasius, 1872 pounds. Restutus, 482 pounds. Victorinus, 1794 pounds. To be made, 350 units, 78 pounds. Vol(umen) Calassini, 76 units.
EDCS-12500357.summary: This complex inscription details quantities of goods (likely oil based on later entries) under the heading of Vol(umen) Calassini and specific contributors, with associated weights and dates related to storage and distribution.

EDCS-12500358.text_edition: \n VII Idus Martias ad Octobrem |(centenarius) Felix XXXIII pondo libras LXIIII |(summa) XXXIII pondo libras LXIIII inpletur Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero XXI |(centenaria) XXXIIII fieri in conditorio Zeugitano amphorae gemellariae numero CLXVII ascopae a Byzace(na numero VI |(summa) CCCCXIII pondo libras XX\n
EDCS-12500358.translation: The 7th Ides of March. At October: Felix, 33 units, 64 pounds. Total: 33 units, 64 pounds. Filled by Vol(umen) ascopae from Byzacena: 21 units, 34 units. To be made in the Zeugitan tomb: 167 gemellariae amphorae and 6 ascopae from Byzacena. Total: 413 units, 20 pounds.
EDCS-12500358.summary: This inscription records deliveries of goods (likely oil) on specific dates, detailing quantities from different sources and for different containers, intended for storage.

EDCS-12500359.text_edition: \n VII Kalendas Maias ad Octobrem |(centenarius) Quintus |(centenaria) V pondo libras LX |(centenarius) Victorianus |(centenaria) XX pondo libras LXVII |(centenarius) Bilossus ab Occhi sic |(centenaria) V pondo |(centenarius) Tzelica |(centenaria) X pondo libras XX |(summa) XXXVIII pondo libras XLVII inpletur Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero XXII |(centenaria) XXXVIII pondo libras LXXV fieri in conditorio Zeugitano amphorae gemellariae numero CLXVII et kentenaria sic LXII fisci et Tebelbucitan QZ ascopae a Byzacena numero CCCCXLVI et Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero |(duo milia)CLVI olei Romae canon ascopae numero DCCLVII kentenaria sic |milleCCXXXII |(summa) VIIDCCCLII pondo libras LXXV\n
EDCS-12500359.translation: The 7th day before the Kalends of May. At October: Quintus, 5 units, 60 pounds. Victorianus, 20 units, 67 pounds. Bilossus from Occhi, 5 units. Tzelica, 10 units, 20 pounds. Total: 38 units, 47 pounds. Filled by Vol(umen) ascopae from Byzacena: 22 units, 38 units, 75 pounds. To be made in the Zeugitan tomb: 167 gemellariae amphorae and 62 units. Fisci and from Tebelbucitan QZ, 446 ascopae from Byzacena and 2156 units. Oil from Rome, 757 ascopae, 1232 units. Total: 7852 pounds, 75 pounds.
EDCS-12500359.summary: This inscription details the distribution and quantities of oil from various sources (including Byzacena, Occhi, Tebelbucitan, and Rome) on a specific date and for storage, mentioning different types of containers.

EDCS-12500360.text_edition: \n VII Idus Apriles ad Octobrem |(centenarius) Flabianus sic |(centenaria) X Felix ab Ucubi |(centenaria) VII pondo libras XL |(centenarius) Peregrinus |(centenaria) XIIII pondo libras XC |(centenarius) Felix ab C |(centenaria) X pondo libras XCII |(centenarius) Doianus |(centenaria) pondo libras XXXV |(summa) CLIIII pondo libras LVII inpletur Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero XXI et olei Romae canon ascopae numero CLXI et Macrinenses ascopae a Byzacena numero VI |(centenaria) CLVI fieri in conditorio Zeugitano amphorae gemellariae numero CLXVII et kentenaria sic L fisci et de numero Tebelbucitan QZ ascopae a Byzacena numero CCC et kentenaria sic L de numero Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero |milleDXIIII et olei Romae canon ascopae numero CCLXXII et kentenaria sic |milleXCI |(summa) VCCLXV pondo libras L\n
EDCS-12500360.translation: The 7th Ides of April. At October: Flabianus, 10 units. Felix from Ucubi, 7 units, 40 pounds. Peregrinus, 14 units, 90 pounds. Felix from C, 10 units, 92 pounds. Doianus, units, 35 pounds. Total: 154 units, 57 pounds. Filled by Vol(umen) ascopae from Byzacena: 21 units. And oil from Rome, 161 ascopae. And Macrinenses, 6 ascopae from Byzacena, 156 units. To be made in the Zeugitan tomb: 167 gemellariae amphorae and 50 units. Fisci and from Tebelbucitan QZ, 300 units and 50 units. From Vol(umen) ascopae from Byzacena, 1504 units. And oil from Rome, 272 ascopae, 1091 units. Total: 2265 units, 50 pounds.
EDCS-12500360.summary: This inscription records deliveries of oil and other goods from various regions (Byzacena, Ucubi, Rome, Macrinenses) on a specific date, detailing quantities, weights, and intended storage locations.

EDCS-12500361.text_edition: \n VI Idus Apriles ad Octobrem |(centenarius) Donatilus |(centenaria) XII pondo libras L |(centenarius) Victorianus |(centenaria) VII pondo libras XV |(centenarius) Vanosus |(centenaria) XIIII pondo libras XIIII |(centenarius) Fir |(centenaria) XLVII pondo libras LXV |(centenarius) Kalius |(centenaria) XXIIII |(centenarius) Arus |(centenaria) XXIII pondo libras LIII |(centenarius) Victor |(centenaria) XLVI pondo libras LX |(centenarius) Romanus |(centenaria) LVII pondo libras X |(summa) CCXXXII pondo libras LXVII inpletur Macrinenses ascopae a Byzacena numero XLVIIII et kentenaria sic CXX AFR |(summa) CCXXXV fieri in conditorio Zeugitano amphorae gemellariae numero CLXVII et kentenaria sic L fisci et Tebelbucitan QZ ascopae a Byzacena numero CCC et kentenaria sic L et Macrinenses ascopae a Byzacena numero VI et kentenaria sic CXX olei Romae canon ascopae numero CCCXCI et kentenaria sic |(mille)XCI |(summa) VCCCCI pondo libras XXXV\n
EDCS-12500361.translation: On the sixth day before the Ides of April, to Octobrem, Donatilus the centenarian, with 12 centenaria weighing 50 pounds; Victorianus the centenarian, with 7 centenaria weighing 15 pounds; Vanosus the centenarian, with 14 centenaria weighing 14 pounds; Fir the centenarian, with 47 centenaria weighing 65 pounds; Kalius the centenarian, with 24; Arus the centenarian, with 23 centenaria weighing 53 pounds; Victor the centenarian, with 46 centenaria weighing 60 pounds; Romanus the centenarian, with 57 centenaria weighing 10 pounds; total 232 centenaria, 67 pounds. Filled by the Macrinenses, 49 ascopae from Byzacena, and 120 centenaria. Total 235. Made in the Zeugitan storehouse, 167 twin amphorae and 62 centenaria for the treasury. And Tebelbucitan, 300 ascopae from Byzacena and 50 centenaria. And Macrinenses, 6 ascopae from Byzacena and 120 centenaria. Oil for Rome, 391 ascopae, and 1091 centenaria. Total 4001 pounds, 35 pounds.
EDCS-12500361.summary: This inscription is a record of oil quantities measured and stored, dated to April, likely related to an administrative or logistical record from Roman North Africa.

EDCS-12500362.text_edition: \n Pridie Nonas Maias ad Octobrem |(centenarius) inus |(centenaria) VIIII pondo libras XCIIII |(centenarius) |(centenaria) XIII pondo libras XLII |(centenarius) |(centenaria) VII pondo libras LXVI |(centenarius) nus |(centenaria) IIII pondo libras LXXXVII |(centenarius) Victor a Serue |(centenaria) V pondo libras XVIII |(centenarius) Felix |(centenaria) V pondo libras XIIII |(centenarius) Inventus |(centenaria) XXXV pondo libras LXII |(centenarius) Victor a Nobis sic |(centenaria) XXVII pondo libras XVIII |(summa) CXI pondo libras XI inpletur Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero LXVIII |(centenaria) CXI pondo libras X fieri in conditorio Zeugitano \xcf\x88 numero CLXXXIIII et amphorae gemellariae numero CLXVII kentenaria LXII fisci et Tebelbucitan--- ascopae a Byzacena numero CCCCXLVI et kentenaria sic LXV Macrinenses ascopae a Byzacena LV et kentenaria sic CCLXXXV Vol ascopae a Byzacena numero |(duo milia)CCLXXV olei Romae canon ascopae numero DCCLVII kentenaria sic |milleCCXXXII |(summa) VIIIDCCCII pondo libras LXXV\n
EDCS-12500362.translation: On the day before the Nones of May, to Octobrem, [centenarius] with 9 centenaria weighing 94 pounds; [centenarius] with 13 centenaria weighing 42 pounds; [centenarius] with 7 centenaria weighing 66 pounds; [centenarius] with 4 centenaria weighing 87 pounds; Victor from Serue, with 5 centenaria weighing 18 pounds; Felix, with 5 centenaria weighing 14 pounds; Inventus, with 35 centenaria weighing 62 pounds; Victor from Nobis, with 27 centenaria weighing 18 pounds; total 111 centenaria, 11 pounds. Filled by Vol, 68 ascopae from Byzacena, 111 centenaria, 10 pounds. Made in the Zeugitan storehouse, 184 [units] and 167 twin amphorae, 62 centenaria for the treasury. And Tebelbucitan, 446 ascopae from Byzacena and 65 centenaria. Macrinenses, 55 ascopae from Byzacena and 285 centenaria. Vol, 2275 ascopae from Byzacena. Oil for Rome, 757 ascopae, 1232 centenaria. Total 8882 pounds, 75 pounds.
EDCS-12500362.summary: This inscription details quantities of oil measured and stored, dated to May, likely an administrative or logistical record from Roman North Africa.

EDCS-12600043.text_edition: \n Invicto numini deae Caelestis pro salute et aeternitate imperii domini nostri Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Augustae matris Augusti et castrorum et senatus et patriae totiusque domus divinae\n
EDCS-12600043.translation: To the unconquered divine power of the goddess Caelestis, for the safety and eternity of the empire of our lord Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, and of Julia Augusta, mother of the Augustus and of the camps and of the Senate and of the whole fatherland and of the whole divine house.
EDCS-12600043.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Caelestis for the well-being and endurance of the Roman Empire and its imperial family, likely from the reign of Emperor Severus Alexander.

EDCS-12600056.text_edition: \n XI Kalendas Augustas navicula Felicis Xeri I Byzacenae VI Kalendas Augustas navicula Byzacenae CXLIIII reprobo IIII\n
EDCS-12600056.translation: On the eleventh day before the Kalends of August, Felix's small boat from Xeri, 1 from Byzacena. On the sixth day before the Kalends of August, a small boat from Byzacena, 144, reprobate 4.
EDCS-12600056.summary: This inscription records the arrival or shipment of small boats, possibly related to trade or transport, dated to July.

EDCS-12700167.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-12700167.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aelius Publius veteranus militavit ala Britannica bis torquata pius vixit annos LV Numpidia sic fecit viro pientissimo et carissimo marito eius sit tibi terra levis VSAL OQB\n
EDCS-12700167.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelius Publius, a veteran who served in the winged cavalry regiment Britannica, pious, lived 55 years. Numpidia dedicated this to her most pious and dearest husband. May the earth lie lightly upon you. V(otum) S(olvit) A(nimo) L(ibens) O(b) Q(od) B(e) F(ecisti).
EDCS-12700167.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aelius Publius, a veteran of the ala Britannica, erected by his wife Numpidia, indicating his service and lifespan.

EDCS-12700228.text_edition: \n Numini sancto inferno deo votum monitus solvit Cissus\n
EDCS-12700228.translation: To the holy divine power of the underworld god, Cissus fulfilled his vow after being prompted.
EDCS-12700228.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Cissus fulfilling a vow to an underworld deity.

EDCS-12700229.text_edition: \n Diis Mauris Augustis sacrum pro salute Augusti Caesaris Marci Aureli Commodi Antonini toris procurator marmorum Numidicorum io\n
EDCS-12700229.translation: Sacred to the divine Lords of the Underworld. In fulfillment of a vow for the safety of Augustus Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus, procurator of the Numidian marble quarries.
EDCS-12700229.summary: This is a sacred inscription dedicated to the underworld gods for the safety of Emperor Commodus, mentioning a procurator responsible for Numidian marble.

EDCS-12800189.text_edition: \n Quintus Mirusius votum fecit Saturno votum solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-12800189.translation: Quintus Mirusius made a vow to Saturn and willingly fulfilled it.
EDCS-12800189.summary: This is a votive inscription where Quintus Mirusius declares he made and fulfilled a vow to the god Saturn.

EDCS-12800190.text_edition: \n Fortunatus Heleni filius Saturno votum solvit libens animo pro Severo filio suo\n
EDCS-12800190.translation: Fortunatus, son of Helenus, willingly fulfilled his vow to Saturn for his son Severus.
EDCS-12800190.summary: This is a votive inscription where Fortunatus fulfilled a vow to Saturn for the sake of his son Severus.

EDCS-12800321.text_edition: \n ------ legato Kar]thaginis praetori tribuno plebis quaestori provinciae Baeticae viocuro universae curiae impetrato ab ordine loco pecunia sua patrono et alumno coloniae posuerunt\n
EDCS-12800321.translation: [...], legate of Carthage, praetor, tribune of the plebs, quaestor of the province of Baetica, curator of the entire curia, having obtained from the order a place with his own money, as patron and foster-child of the colony, they placed [this].
EDCS-12800321.summary: This inscription, with significant lacunae, likely records the dedication of a public space or monument by officials and citizens of a colony, honoring a benefactor.

EDCS-12800333.text_edition: \n Bonae Fortunae Dis monumentum Balsilechi Subath qui vixit annorum sic CX et supra\n
EDCS-12800333.translation: A monument to Good Fortune by Balsilechus of Subath, who lived for 110 years and more.
EDCS-12800333.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the goddess Bona Fortuna by Balsilechus, who lived to an advanced age.

EDCS-12800687.text_edition: \n Caius Acrasius Publi filius militavit legione III Augusta vixsit sic nnis XXXV O militavit annis XV heres Helvius rius Quirina H\n
EDCS-12800687.translation: Caius Acrasius, son of Publius, served in the III Augusta legion, lived for 35 years, and served for 15 years. His heir was Helvius [---]rius of the Quirina tribe.
EDCS-12800687.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Acrasius, a soldier in the III Augusta legion, recording his lifespan and service years.

EDCS-12800688.text_edition: \n Marcus Cornelius Marci filius Maecia Longus Neapole miles legionis III Augustae centuria Sextili vixit nnos LIX militavit nnos XXV hic situs est heredes posuerunt\n
EDCS-12800688.translation: Marcus Cornelius Longus, son of Marcus, of the Maecia tribe, a soldier in the III Augusta legion, in the century of Sextilius, lived for 59 years and served for 25 years. His heirs placed him here.
EDCS-12800688.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and military service of Marcus Cornelius Longus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-12800689.text_edition: \n Clodius Arnensis Qu miles legionis III AugustaeIFMC \n
EDCS-12800689.translation: Clodius Arnensis, a soldier of the III Augusta legion...
EDCS-12800689.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription identifying Clodius Arnensis as a soldier of the III Augusta legion.

EDCS-12800690.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Augusti libertus Crestus Samianus \n
EDCS-12800690.translation: Caius Iulius Crestus Samianus, freedman of Augustus.
EDCS-12800690.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Caius Iulius Crestus Samianus, a freedman.

EDCS-12800691.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus sacrum Tiberius Claudius Felix Cilicinenus vixit nnos LI menses hic situs est\n
EDCS-12800691.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Tiberius Claudius Felix of Cilicia lived for 51 years and [some] months. He is buried here.
EDCS-12800691.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius Felix, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-13000608.text_edition: \n Iovi Augusto sacrum dedicante Cnaeo Suellio Flacco legato Augusti pro praetore Quintus Mantius Quinti filius Camilia Alba Pompeia legio III Augusta de suo fecit\n
EDCS-13000608.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Dedicated by Gnaeus Suellius Flaccus, governor of the province, Quintus Mantius, son of Quintus, of the Camilia tribe, and Alba Pompeia of the III Augusta legion, made this from his own resources.
EDCS-13000608.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by soldiers of the III Augusta legion under the command of the governor, Gnaeus Suellius Flaccus.

EDCS-13000622.translation: To the Divine Pius Severus, father of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, high priest, with tribunician power for the 17th time, consul for the 4th time, proconsul, father of the country.
EDCS-13000622.summary: This is a posthumous dedication honoring Emperor Antoninus Pius (father of Marcus Aurelius), listing his extensive titles and honors.

EDCS-13000625.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-13000625.text_edition: \n Ex testamento Cai Corneli Egriliani praefecti legionis XIIII geminae quo testamento ex HS CCL milibus nummum arcum cum statuis Auggustorum nnostrorum item tetrastylis duobus cum statuis et Minervae quae in foro fieri praecepit praeter alia HS CCL milia nummum quae rei publicae ita ut certis diebus gymnasia populo publice in thermis praeberentur legavit et ad Kapitolinum argenti libras CLXX id est lances IIII auri libras XIIII id est phialas III scyphos II haece omnia secundum voluntatem eius in contione populi Corneli Fortunatus et Quinta fratres et heredes eius adsignaverunt et opus perfecerunt\n
EDCS-13000625.translation: From the will of Caius Cornelius Egrilianus, prefect of the XIIII Gemina legion, who bequeathed 250,000 sesterces for the construction of an arch with statues of our Augusti, and two tetrastyle buildings with statues and a statue of Minerva, which he ordered to be built in the forum, in addition to other bequests of 250,000 sesterces for public games and access to the baths for the people on specific days. He also left 170 pounds of silver, namely 4 large dishes, and 14 pounds of gold, namely 3 shallow bowls and 2 cups, for the Capitol. All these things, in accordance with his wishes, Cornelius Fortunatus and Quinta, his brothers and heirs, assigned and completed in the public assembly.
EDCS-13000625.summary: This inscription details the fulfillment of the will of Caius Cornelius Egrilianus, a legionary prefect, who left substantial funds for public works, games, and offerings in Rome.

EDCS-13000642.translation: ...of the [3rd] legion [Claudiae], lived 10[3?] years, served 5 years, is buried here. Lucius Annaeus...
EDCS-13000642.summary: This is a fragment of a tombstone for Lucius Annaeus, a soldier of the III Claudian legion, recording his lifespan and service years.

EDCS-13000643.translation: Flavius Ianuarius, assistant in the office of the cornicularii, made this for Homullius Saturninus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, his fellow soldier.
EDCS-13000643.summary: A memorial stone erected by Flavius Ianuarius, an assistant in the cornicularii office, for his fellow soldier Homullius Saturninus of the Third Legion Augusta.

EDCS-13000644.translation: Quintus Iulius Dioratus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, century of Lucius Iulius, lived 30 years, served 5 years, is buried here. Titus Flavius Martialis, of the century of Nasidius, his heir, erected this monument.
EDCS-13000644.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Iulius Dioratus, a soldier of the Third Legion Augusta, erected by his heir Titus Flavius Martialis.

EDCS-13000646.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Repentinus, an Augustan scribe, lived 25 years. His colleagues from the scribal guild made this.
EDCS-13000646.summary: A funerary inscription for Repentinus, a scribe, erected by his colleagues from the scribal guild.

EDCS-13000654.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Audasius Succedens, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, who held the offices of duovir, prefect for dispensing justice, and quaestor, lived 70 years. Audasius Pomptinus Primitius and Ianuaria, his freedmen and heirs, made this for their most excellent and devoted patron. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000654.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Audasius Succedens, a local magistrate, erected by his freedmen and heirs.

EDCS-13000656.text_edition: \n Publi Saturnini Saturniani flaminis perpetui filio nepoti Cai Iuli Romuleani equitis Romani pontificis qui primus a condita civitate sua ob honorem flamoni annui munus Idibus omnibus senis paribus curiae suae dedit universae curiae et Augustales pecunia sua locus datus ex decreto ordinis\n
EDCS-13000656.translation: To the son of Publius Saturninus Saturnianus, the perpetual priest, and grandson of Gaius Iulius Romuleanus, a Roman knight and pontiff, who, from the founding of his city, for the honor of the annual priesthood, gave on all the middle days of the month a gift of six each to his senate; and the Augustales, the place was given by his own money, by decree of the order.
EDCS-13000656.summary: A dedication recording the privileges and benefactions of a local dignitary, Publius Saturninus Saturnianus, and his family.

EDCS-13000665.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Felicitas lived 3 years. She is buried here. Hilarus, freedman of the emperors, made this for his native slave girl.
EDCS-13000665.summary: A funerary inscription for Felicitas, a young girl, erected by Hilarus, an imperial freedman.

EDCS-13000667.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Victoria, of Augustus, lived 50 years. She is buried here. Flavius Primus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-13000667.summary: A funerary inscription for Victoria, erected by her husband Flavius Primus.

EDCS-13000670.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Aelius Viator. He is buried here. Publius Aelius Felix made this for his devoted father.
EDCS-13000670.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Viator, erected by his son Publius Aelius Felix.

EDCS-13000674.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Apuleius Gudullus lived 65 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000674.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Apuleius Gudullus.

EDCS-13000675.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Apuleius Miggin lived 26 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000675.summary: A funerary inscription for Apuleius Miggin.

EDCS-13000676.translation: To the departed spirits. Attia Hilariatas lived 27 years, 5 months, 6 days. Iulius Minervalis made this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-13000676.summary: A funerary epitaph for Attia Hilariatas, recording her age at death and set up by her husband, Iulius Minervalis.

EDCS-13000677.translation: To the departed spirits. Audasius Primitivus and Audasia Ianuaria made this for their most excellent and devoted daughter Audasia Fortunata, who deserved well of us, and lived 16 years, 3 months. She lies here.
EDCS-13000677.summary: A funerary epitaph for Audasia Fortunata, set up by her parents Audasius Primitivus and Audasia Ianuaria, recording her age and death.

EDCS-13000678.translation: To the departed spirits. Audasia Charitu lived 30 years. Pomptinus, her husband, made this for his most devoted wife. She lies here.
EDCS-13000678.summary: A funerary epitaph for Audasia Charitu, recording her age at death and set up by her husband Pomptinus.

EDCS-13000680.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelius Fortunatus placed this for his dearest father Aurelius Victorinus, who deserved well, and for his sweetest mother Aurelia Cara, who deserved well. Mother lived 20 years, Father lived 80 years.
EDCS-13000680.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Victorinus and Aurelia Cara, set up by their son Aurelius Fortunatus, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-13000681.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Caecilius Ianuarius lived 62 years, 11 months, 25 days. Marcus Caecilius Ianuarius made this for his dearest father.
EDCS-13000681.summary: A funerary epitaph for a father, set up by his son Marcus Caecilius Ianuarius, recording the father's age at death.

EDCS-13000682.translation: To the departed spirits. Calpurnia Caesita lived 25 years. She lies here. Calpurnius Felix and Hirria Rogata, her parents, made this for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-13000682.summary: A funerary epitaph for Calpurnia Caesita, recording her age at death and set up by her parents Calpurnius Felix and Hirria Rogata.

EDCS-13000686.translation: To the departed spirits. For Claudia Aphrodisia.
EDCS-13000686.summary: A funerary epitaph fragment for Claudia Aphrodisia.

EDCS-13000690.translation: Cornelia Afrodisia lived 50 years. Iulius Probus made this for his most devoted wife. She lies here.
EDCS-13000690.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelia Afrodisia, recording her age at death and set up by her husband Iulius Probus.

EDCS-13000691.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornificia Fortunata lived [---].
EDCS-13000691.summary: A funerary epitaph fragment for Cornificia Fortunata, recording her name and part of her lifespan.

EDCS-13000692.translation: To the departed spirits. Domitia Primitiva lived 32 years. She lies here. Gaius Domitius Pudens made this for his dearest and most faithful freedwoman.
EDCS-13000692.summary: A funerary epitaph for Domitia Primitiva, recording her age at death and set up by her patron Gaius Domitius Pudens.

EDCS-13000693.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Faonius Marcellus, son of Caius, lived [unknown years]. He made this monument in his own lifetime.
EDCS-13000693.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Faonius Marcellus, who dedicated his tomb during his life, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000694.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Agilis lived eighty years. Flavius, his son, made this for his dutiful father.
EDCS-13000694.summary: This funerary epitaph records Titus Flavius Agilis, who lived to be eighty, with the monument erected by his son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000696.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Ianuarius lived twenty years. His heirs made this; he is buried here.
EDCS-13000696.summary: This funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Ianuarius, who died at twenty, was made by his heirs, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000699.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavian[a] lived seventeen years. Demetrianus made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-13000699.summary: This funerary epitaph records Flavian[a], who died at seventeen, with the monument erected by her husband Demetrianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000700.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Folius Felix lived twelve years. Martialis made this for his dutiful son.
EDCS-13000700.summary: This funerary epitaph records Quintus Folius Felix, who died at twelve, with the monument erected by his father Martialis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000703.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fusca lived ten months and five days; she is buried here. Gaius Caecilius Cinna[?] made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-13000703.summary: This funerary epitaph records Fusca, who died at ten months and five days, with the monument erected by her husband Gaius Caecilius Cinna[?], likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000706.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ianuarius lived thirty-six years. His father(?) made this.
EDCS-13000706.summary: This funerary epitaph records Ianuarius, who lived to be thirty-six, with the monument erected by his father(?), likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000707.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ianuarius Halconius(?) the dutiful lived seventy years.
EDCS-13000707.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Ianuarius Halconius(?), who lived to be seventy, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000711.translation: Julius Gududio lived eight years; he is buried here. Julia Saturnina made this for her sweetest son.
EDCS-13000711.summary: This funerary epitaph records Julius Gududio, who died at eight, with the monument erected by his mother Julia Saturnina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000713.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Julius Minervalis lived seventy years, seven months, and seven days; he is buried here. Tullius Pistus made this.
EDCS-13000713.summary: This funerary epitaph records Quintus Julius Minervalis, who lived to seventy years, seven months, and seven days, with the monument erected by Tullius Pistus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000714.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Gaius Julius Niger lived 65 years. Caecilia Candida made this for her most beloved husband.
EDCS-13000714.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Gaius Julius Niger at age 65 and made by his wife, Caecilia Candida.

EDCS-13000716.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Speratus made this for his most beloved daughter, who lived 2 years, 8 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-13000716.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Speratus, detailing her lifespan and erected by her parent.

EDCS-13000718.translation: Gaius Julius Victorinus lived 60 years; he lies here. Julius Vitalis and Arnobius made this for their dearest father.
EDCS-13000718.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Victorinus, who lived to be 60, and was commemorated by his sons, Julius Vitalis and Arnobius.

EDCS-13000720.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Iulia Eutyche lived 35 years, 10 months, and 12 days; she lies here. Amantius made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-13000720.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Eutyche, detailing her lifespan and erected by her husband, Amantius.

EDCS-13000721.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Iulia Fortunata lived piously for 60 years, chaste, good, inviolate. This rare gift from a husband, Fortunata, is for you. Worthy of my name Hardalius, as you remain alive and my children's children thrive while you are safe.
EDCS-13000721.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Fortunata, who lived 60 years, a dedication from her husband Hardalius, praising her virtues and longevity.

EDCS-13000722.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Iulia Fortunata lived 40 years. Maximus made this for his pious mother.
EDCS-13000722.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Fortunata, who lived 40 years, erected by her son, Maximus.

EDCS-13000724.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Iulia Verecunda lived 2 years; she lies here.
EDCS-13000724.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Iulia Verecunda, who died at age 2.

EDCS-13000729.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Matronica lived 50 years. Ulpius (?) Rugosus lived 50 years, 6 months, and 2 days. Lupus made this for his parents.
EDCS-13000729.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating two individuals, Matronica and Ulpius Rugosus, and dedicated by their son Lupus.

EDCS-13000731.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Lucius Minucius Saturus lived 127 years. Lucius Minucius Felix made this for his excellent father.
EDCS-13000731.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Minucius Saturus, who incredibly lived to 127 years, erected by his son, Lucius Minucius Felix.

EDCS-13000734.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Occia Auge lived 37 years; she lies here. Her husband Pomponius Gaetulus made this.
EDCS-13000734.summary: A funerary epitaph for Occia Auge, who lived 37 years, erected by her husband, Pomponius Gaetulus.

EDCS-13000736.translation: Gabia Fausta lived piously for 19 years.
EDCS-13000736.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gabia Fausta, who died at the age of 19.

EDCS-13000737.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Paetius Abascantius, pious, lived 25 years, 11 months, and 22 days. He is buried here. His father, Publius Paetius Abascantius, made this.
EDCS-13000737.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Paetius Abascantius, who lived to 25 years, 11 months, and 22 days, and was erected by his father.

EDCS-13000738.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Perellia Fortunula lived 12 years. Her parents made this.
EDCS-13000738.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Perellia Fortunula, who died at the age of 12, and was erected by her parents.

EDCS-13000739.translation: Publius Petronius Festus, son of Decimus, pious, lived 7 years, 5 months, and half an hour. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000739.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Petronius Festus, who lived to 7 years, 5 months, and half an hour.

EDCS-13000741.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Plotius Maximus lived 65 years. Labenia Glauce made this for her pious husband. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000741.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Plotius Maximus, who lived to 65 years, erected by his wife Labenia Glauce.

EDCS-13000746.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Pomponius Adiutor lived 62 years, 1 month, and 17 days.
EDCS-13000746.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Marcus Pomponius Adiutor, who died at the age of 62 years, 1 month, and 17 days.

EDCS-13000747.translation: Marcus Pomponius Albinus lived 63 years, and 16 days.
EDCS-13000747.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Marcus Pomponius Albinus, who died at the age of 63 years and 16 days.

EDCS-13000750.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. O Pompōsāi, lived 16 years. Hermophilus made this for his dearest daughter. She is buried here.
EDCS-13000750.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pompōsāi, who lived to 16 years, erected by her father Hermophilus.

EDCS-13000751.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [Ru]frius(?) [---] A/[---] [P]ompe[ia](?) Ingenua(?) made this for her dearest husband. He is buried here.
EDCS-13000751.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unidentified husband, erected by his wife Ingenua(?), with significant lacunae.

EDCS-13000754.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Sempronius Amaranthus lived 60 years. His son made this for his pious father.
EDCS-13000754.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Sempronius Amaranthus, who lived to 60 years, erected by his son.

EDCS-13000755.translation: To the departed spirits. Sergia Rustica lived 36 years, 7 months. Claudius Titius Crescens [erected this].
EDCS-13000755.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sergia Rustica, who died at age 36 years and 7 months, likely set up by Claudius Titius Crescens.

EDCS-13000757.translation: To the departed spirits. Speratus lived 34 years.
EDCS-13000757.summary: A funerary epitaph for Speratus, who died at age 34.

EDCS-13000759.translation: To the departed spirits. Tadius Cotronius lived 3 years; his father made this. To the departed spirits. Gaius Tadius Victorinus lived [an unknown age].
EDCS-13000759.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Tadius Cotronius at age 3, set up by his father, followed by a partial epitaph for Gaius Tadius Victorinus.

EDCS-13000760.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Tanonnius Felix, son of Publius, lived 27 years. Dearest son, he is buried here.
EDCS-13000760.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Tanonnius Felix, who died at age 27, likely erected by his children.

EDCS-13000761.translation: To the departed spirits. Valerius Sisa made [this] for himself and his parents while alive. To the departed spirits. Homullia Secundilla lived 40 years. Sisa made [this] for his mother. To the departed spirits. Valerius Natalis lived 75 years. Sisa made [this] for his father. To the departed spirits. Valerius Eupraxius and Iulia Tertia lived [unknown ages]. Sisa made [this]. To the departed spirits. Banasius lived [unknown age]. And Pretiosa lived [unknown age].
EDCS-13000761.summary: A funerary monument with multiple inscriptions, including self-dedications and dedications to family members by Valerius Sisa, recording various individuals' ages at death.

EDCS-13000763.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Vettius Rufus, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, pious, lived 34 years. Marcus Vettius Rufus (his) father and Lucius Vettius Tenax, (his) brother, pious, took care to have this made.
EDCS-13000763.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Vettius Rufus, who died at age 34, erected by his father and brother.

EDCS-13000767.translation: To the departed spirits. Vindemia lived 35 years. Ispiculus made [this] for his dearest wife; she is buried here.
EDCS-13000767.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vindemia, who died at age 35, erected by her husband Ispiculus.

EDCS-13000768.translation: To the departed spirits. Ulpius Liberalis lived 15 years, 11 months, 7 days. He is buried here. Marcus Ulpius Vibianus (father) and Aurelia Victoria (mother) made [this] for their incomparable son.
EDCS-13000768.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpius Liberalis, who died at age 15 years, 11 months, and 7 days, erected by his parents.

EDCS-13000769.translation: To the departed spirits. Ulpia Quinta lived 40 years. Hortensius Geminius made [this] for his dearest wife.
EDCS-13000769.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpia Quinta, who died at age 40, erected by her husband Hortensius Geminius.

EDCS-13000772.translation: To the departed spirits. [She] was good with good character, urbane and holy, with her Gaius, a worthy wife. I adore your memory. Few are the epitaphs to seek yours. To you, our good and cheerful one. We go to you for many years. We return to you, dear one, unless your good reputation and chaste [innocence]... [illegible]... [illegible]... [illegible]... were noble [illegible]... [illegible] frugal [illegible] NOT enough [illegible] your [illegible] full [illegible] vows [illegible] your [illegible] Oh dear [illegible] will learn and these [illegible] of verses I read and greet you, my sweetest Brea, who lived [illegible] pregnant and returned [her] life to nature. [illegible] [illegible] Brea, farewell, woman. May you fare well, and this sacred [offering]. [For] rare and modest [virtue].
EDCS-13000772.summary: A complex and partially preserved funerary text praising the deceased Brea, noting her virtue, her relationship with Gaius, and her death in childbirth, possibly erected by her family.

EDCS-13000800.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Margarita, pious, lived 75 years, is buried here. Gudullus, assistant in the archive of our Emperor, [made this] for his most rare mother-in-law.
EDCS-13000800.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Margarita, who lived 75 years, with her son-in-law Gudullus, an archive assistant, as the dedicator, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000801.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Attius Mattius lived 47 years. His sons made [this].
EDCS-13000801.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life of Lucius Attius Mattius, who lived 47 years, and dedicated by his sons, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000803.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Perellius Felix lived [? number] years. Caecilia [Zaba?] [made this] for her husband.
EDCS-13000803.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Perellius Felix, whose age at death is partially restored, and dedicated by his wife Caecilia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000805.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Septimius Iulianus lived 90 years. Caecilia Paulina made [this] for her most beloved husband.
EDCS-13000805.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Quintus Septimius Iulianus lived 90 years and was dedicated by his wife Caecilia Paulina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000867.translation: Decimus Seius Iuvenis, son of Decimus, of the Quirina tribe, from Burdigala, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, in the century of Nepos, lived 46 years, served 23 years, is buried here. Gisatia Seiana made [this] for her husband, who deserved well of her.
EDCS-13000867.summary: This is the funerary epitaph of Decimus Seius Iuvenis, a soldier from Burdigala, who lived 46 years and served for 23 years, dedicated by his wife Gisatia Seiana, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000869.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Domitius Saturninus lived 25 years and 13 days. Domitia Crescentina arranged for [this] to be made for her dearest brother.
EDCS-13000869.summary: A funerary epitaph for Domitius Saturninus, who lived 25 years and 13 days, dedicated by his sister Domitia Crescentina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000870.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius M. Rogatus lived 95 years. His sons made [this].
EDCS-13000870.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Lucius M. Rogatus, who lived 95 years, and was made by his sons, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000933.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Aemilius Severinus qui et Phillyrio vixit annos LXVI plus minus et pro amore Romano quievit ab hoc Capelliano captus memor amicitiae pietatis Victoricus qui et Verota\n
EDCS-13000933.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Aemilius Severinus, also known as Phillyrio, lived 66 years more or less, and rested from this life, captured by Roman affection. Victoricus, also known as Verota, [made this] in memory of his friendship and devotion.
EDCS-13000933.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Lucius Aemilius Severinus, also known as Phillyrio, lived 66 years, and was dedicated by Victoricus (also known as Verota) in memory of their friendship and devotion, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000934.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelia Nina lived 85 years, is buried here. Aeli S. and P. made [this].
EDCS-13000934.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Nina, who lived 85 years, dedicated by Aeli S. and P., likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000935.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Apuleius Martis lived 60 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13000935.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the life of Gaius Apuleius Martis, who lived 60 years, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13000939.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Numerius Petronius Successus, aged 85, is buried here.
EDCS-13000939.summary: A funerary epitaph for Numerius Petronius Successus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13000941.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tutia Vitalis, daughter of Publius, lived 67 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13000941.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tutia Vitalis, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13000994.text_edition: \n Ex auctoritate Imperatoris Nervae Traiani Caesaris Augusti Germanici Dacici Lucius Minicius Natalis legatus Augusti pro praetore inter Madaurenses et Musulamios\n
EDCS-13000994.translation: By the authority of the Emperor Nerva Trajan Caesar Augustus, conqueror of Germany and Dacia, Lucius Minicius Natalis, imperial legate with praetorian power, [intervened] between the people of Madaura and Musulamia.
EDCS-13000994.summary: An inscription recording a legal or administrative decision made by Lucius Minicius Natalis under Emperor Trajan, concerning a dispute between two local communities.

EDCS-13001069.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Scribonius Celsinus, enrolled in the Quirina tribe, dutiful, lived 49 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13001069.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Scribonius Celsinus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13001088.text_edition: \n no nex sacro praecepto domini nostri Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et consensum sic utrarumque partium decernente Claudio Iuliano proconsule clarissimo viro\n
EDCS-13001088.translation: ...on account of a divine command not to profane, with the consent of both parties, pronounced by Claudius Julianus, most illustrious proconsul, under our lord Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-13001088.summary: A fragment of an inscription recording a legal decision made by the proconsul Claudius Julianus during the reign of Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-13001089.translation: To the most holy Mother of the Gods, the Great Idaias, for the health and safety of our lord Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, and Augusta Julia Mamaea, and their whole divine family, Publius Caecilius Felix, priest from the order of pontiffs, proclaimed a kriybolium and taurobolium.
EDCS-13001089.summary: A dedication to the Magna Mater by Publius Caecilius Felix, a priest, for the welfare of Emperor Severus Alexander and his mother Julia Mamaea, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13002447.translation: Marcus Canteius, soldier of the Legion III Augusta, under Centurion Perpetuus, lived 30 years, served 5 years, is buried here.
EDCS-13002447.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Canteius, a soldier of the Legion III Augusta, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002449.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Stercula lived 5 years, 2 months, and 7 days. Liberalis, assistant to the scribe of our lord Augustus, and Felicia, made [this] for their dearest daughter, is buried here.
EDCS-13002449.summary: A funerary epitaph for Stercula, a child, with funerary inscriptions by her father and possibly mother, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002450.translation: Marcus Julius Maternus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, in the service of Augustus, soldier of the Legion III Augusta, under Centurion Crispinus, lived 27 years, served 5 years, is buried here. Sextus Valerius Atticinus, standard-bearer of the cavalry of the Legion III Augusta.
EDCS-13002450.summary: A combined funerary epitaph for Marcus Julius Maternus and an adjacent inscription for Sextus Valerius Atticinus, both soldiers of the Legion III Augusta, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002451.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marco Aurelio Ursiono, freedman of the junior Augustus, lived 90 years, 3 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-13002451.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marco Aurelio Ursiono, a freedman of an emperor, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13002452.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Celsina, also known as Lampadaria, who lived 6 years and 8 months, rests here. Iulia made this for her most sweet daughter.
EDCS-13002452.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Iulia Celsina, set up by her mother, Iulia Celsina.

EDCS-13002453.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Secura, the dutiful, lived 64 years, rests here.
EDCS-13002453.summary: A funerary epitaph for a woman named Secura, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002485.text_edition: \n Ex auctoritate Imperatoris Caesaris Traiani Augusti Germanici Dacici Lucius Munatius Gallus legatus pro praetore finibus Musulamiorum ectis vetustatis tam abolevit\n
EDCS-13002485.translation: By the authority of Emperor Caesar Trajan Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, Lucius Munatius Gallus, governor with praetorian power, removed the boundaries of the Musulamii which had been erased by age.
EDCS-13002485.summary: A boundary stone dating to the reign of Trajan (98-117 AD), recording the action of the governor Lucius Munatius Gallus regarding the Musulamii tribe.

EDCS-13002527.translation: Publius Messius Melissus, son of Publius, of the voting tribe Quirina, from Augusta, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, in the century of Lucilius, lived 30 years, served 4 years, rests here. Decimus Clodius, armourer, of the same century, erected this monument.
EDCS-13002527.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Messius Melissus, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, with his monument erected by a fellow soldier.

EDCS-13002528.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Vitalis, a soldier from the ranks of the Martenses, from Gaul, lived 55 years.
EDCS-13002528.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Vitalis, a soldier who served in a unit from Gaul, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-13002530.translation: Centurion, armourer of the second Adiutrix legion, promoted into the III Augusta legion, 26 years of service, who lived 46 years, 2 months, and 20 days, rests here. Aurelia Dio[--] made this for her husband [--].
EDCS-13002530.summary: A funerary epitaph for a centurion and armourer, who served in two legions and had 26 years of service, erected by his wife Aurelia Dio[--].

EDCS-13002538.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fortunatus, assistant to our Emperor from the office of records, lived 32 years, 1 month, and 17 days, rests here. [---]ATE[---] and Venustula, his wife, made this for their dearest husband. Vegethi.
EDCS-13002538.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fortunatus, an imperial assistant, erected by his wife Venustula and another individual, Vegethi.

EDCS-13002539.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Maxima, servant of our Emperor, lived 5 years, rests here. Victor made this for his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-13002539.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Maxima, a servant of the emperor, erected by her father Victor.

EDCS-13002540.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sallustia Amnubiti(?), freedwoman of our Emperors, lived 18 years, 3 months, and 5 days. Ianua, freedwoman of our Emperors, her mother, made this for her most rare daughter.
EDCS-13002540.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sallustia Amnubiti(?), a young freedwoman, erected by her mother Ianua, who was also a freedwoman of the emperors.

EDCS-13002541.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cornelius Vincentius, foster-child of the arena, lived [---] years, 3 months, and 10 days. Baberius, father, made this for his dearest son.
EDCS-13002541.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelius Vincentius, a gladiator ('foster-child of the arena'), erected by his father.

EDCS-13002545.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Aelius Salvianus lived 60 years and is buried here. His sons Aelii Saturninus, Messianus, Octavianus, and Sedatus made this for their father.
EDCS-13002545.summary: A funerary monument records the life and death of Quintus Aelius Salvianus, with dedication by his sons.

EDCS-13002546.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilius Processianus lived 13 years, 2 months, and 16 days and is buried here. His grandfather Manilius Restutus and Aemilius Felicisimus made this for their dearest son.
EDCS-13002546.summary: A funerary monument for a young boy, Aemilius Processianus, with dedication by his grandfather and another relation.

EDCS-13002547.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [A]emilia Donativa lived 1 year, 4 months, and 13 days and is buried here, who lived sweeter than a rose. Her father Turbo and mother Designata made this for their daughter.
EDCS-13002547.summary: A funerary monument for an infant girl, Aemilia Donativa, with dedication by her parents.

EDCS-13002548.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Processa lived 59 years. Her husband Manilius Restutus and her sons Aemilii made this for their dearest mother.
EDCS-13002548.summary: A funerary monument for Aemilia Processa, with dedication by her husband and sons.

EDCS-13002553.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Apponius Hilarus Tonantius lived 37 years, 4 months, and 9 days and is buried here. Licinia Montana made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-13002553.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Apponius Hilarus Tonantius, with dedication by his wife.

EDCS-13002555.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Atellius Terminalis, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, who lived 80 years and is buried here; I arranged for this to be made while still alive.
EDCS-13002555.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Atellius Terminalis, who commissioned his own tomb.

EDCS-13002559.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caelia Primula lived 7 months and 8 days and is buried here. Lucius Caelius Primulus and Iulia Dativa made this for their dearest daughter.
EDCS-13002559.summary: A funerary monument for an infant girl, Caelia Primula, with dedication by her parents.

EDCS-13002560.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caelia Victoria lived 3 years and 4 months. Caelius Maximus, her father, made this for his daughter. She is buried here.
EDCS-13002560.summary: A funerary monument for Caelia Victoria, a young girl, with dedication by her father.

EDCS-13002561.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudius Abigeus, freedman, lived 8 years, 6 months, and 20 days. Claudius Orion made this for his dearest foster-son. He is buried here.
EDCS-13002561.summary: A funerary monument for a young freedman, Claudius Abigeus, with dedication by his foster-father.

EDCS-13002563.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cornelius Dativus lived [ ] years and is buried here. [For his most exceptional father] Cornelius Lupercus, as a consolation [after his death].
EDCS-13002563.summary: A funerary monument for Cornelius Dativus, with a partial dedication for his father, Cornelius Lupercus.

EDCS-13002568.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Gaius F. Felix lived 50 years.
EDCS-13002568.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Gaius F. Felix.

EDCS-13002572.translation: Fortunatus lived 55 years. Petronia Quarta had this made for her dearest husband and he is buried here.
EDCS-13002572.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Fortunatus and erected by his wife Petronia Quarta.

EDCS-13002575.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Gavilia Felicula lived 15 years. Her father Flavius Ianuarius made the tomb.
EDCS-13002575.summary: A funerary inscription for Gavilia Felicula, detailing her age and that her father commissioned the tomb.

EDCS-13002576.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Gentia Prima lived 50 years and 2 months. She is buried here.
EDCS-13002576.summary: A funerary inscription stating the lifespan and burial place of Gentia Prima.

EDCS-13002577.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Gaius Julius Donatianus lived 32 years, 4 months, and 6 days. His brother Gaius Julius Felix made this for him, a most devoted brother.
EDCS-13002577.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Donatianus, detailing his lifespan and commissioned by his devoted brother.
