EDCS-14900361.translation: To the departed spirits. Sacred. Cornelia Romana, daughter of Marcus, lived 25 years. Messius Saturninus placed this for his excellent wife. She is buried here.
EDCS-14900361.summary: A tombstone for Cornelia Romana, erected by her husband Messius Saturninus, recording her age at death.

EDCS-14900362.translation: To the departed spirits. Domitia Veneria lived 95 years and is buried here. Fortunata erected this for her pious mother.
EDCS-14900362.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Domitia Veneria, dedicated by her daughter Fortunata.

EDCS-14900364.translation: To the departed spirits. Duronius Neptunalis lived 25 years and is buried here. To the departed spirits. Duronius Successor lived 13 years and is buried here.
EDCS-14900364.summary: A tombstone recording the deaths of two individuals, Duronius Neptunalis and Duronius Successor, possibly father and son.

EDCS-14900365.translation: To the departed spirits. Aetria Victoria lived 44 years. Her devoted parents erected this; she is buried here.
EDCS-14900365.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Aetria Victoria, erected by her parents.

EDCS-14900366.translation: To the departed spirits. Euplius lived 37 years and 7 days and is buried here. Valeria Concordia erected this for her dearest father.
EDCS-14900366.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Euplius, erected by his daughter Valeria Concordia.

EDCS-14900369.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavia Primitiva Crescentina lived 1 year [...].
EDCS-14900369.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Flavia Primitiva Crescentina, who died in her first year.

EDCS-14900370.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavia Secura, who lived 18 years, is buried here.
EDCS-14900370.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Flavia Secura, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-14900371.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Gabinius Ingeneuus lived [...] years.
EDCS-14900371.summary: A tombstone fragment recording the death of Gaius Gabinius Ingeneuus.

EDCS-14900372.translation: Gargilia Saturnina the pious lived 80 years and is buried here. Postumiae Pacata Sperata Quieta erected this for their pious mother.
EDCS-14900372.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Gargilia Saturnina, erected by her daughters.

EDCS-14900373.translation: To the departed spirits. Geminius Fortunatianus lived 22 years, 7 months, and 14 days. His devoted father erected this. To the departed spirits. Pinar[ia He]donin[a vi]/xit a[nnos] / XXXX[3] / man[3] / p[.
EDCS-14900373.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Geminius Fortunatianus, erected by his father, alongside a fragment likely for a woman named Pinar[ia].

EDCS-14900374.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Geminius Laetus, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 66 years and is buried here.
EDCS-14900374.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Publius Geminius Laetus, son of Publius.

EDCS-14900375.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Herennius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived 25 years, 4 months, and 20 days; he lies here. Quintus Herennius, his father, placed this for his devoted son.
EDCS-14900375.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Herennius, set up by his father, Quintus Herennius, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14900376.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Herennia Celsiana, devoted, lived 40 years and 6 months; she lies here.
EDCS-14900376.summary: A funerary epitaph for Herennia Celsiana, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14900377.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Felix lived 60 years and 3 months. Augendus, his son, placed this for his dearest father.
EDCS-14900377.summary: A funerary epitaph for Julius Felix, set up by his son Augendus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15000134.text_edition: \n Centenarum sic Mufelthiana Marci Caecili Bumupal ffecerunt semper Dunbanem Bucubuom Salonema\n
EDCS-15000134.translation: The Centenari, Mufelthiana and Marci Caecili, made it always for Dunbanem, Bucubuom, Salonema.
EDCS-15000134.summary: An inscription recording a dedication made by the 'Centenari,' possibly a local group or administrative unit, to individuals named Dunbanem, Bucubuom, and Salonema, with uncertain dating.

EDCS-15100007.text_edition: \n Lucio Iulio Luci filio Papiria Victori |(centurioni) cohortis I urbanae Severianae laterculensi ostiario scriniario praeffectorum praetorio eemminentissimorum vvirorum innocentissimo viro ob nimia merita IIORORVMI admirabilem benevolentiam erga singulos universosque optimo civi Maracitani sua pecunia fecerunt dedicaverunt\n
EDCS-15100007.translation: Lucius Iulius Victor, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, centurion of the first urban cohort Severiana, doorkeeper and scribe of the most eminent prefects of the praetorium, an innocent man, for his admirable benevolence towards individuals and the community as a whole, the best citizen, the people of Maracita ('Maracitani') made and dedicated this with their own money.
EDCS-15100007.summary: A dedication of a monument by the citizens of Maracita to Lucius Iulius Victor, a centurion and official, honoring his merits and benevolence, likely dating to the Severan period.

EDCS-15100016.text_edition: \n ? Aemilio Quinti filio Papiria Pudenti centurioni legionis III Augustae item legionis II Augustae item iterum legionis II Augustae item legionis XI Claudiae adlecto in comitatu Imperatoris Commodi Augusti Pii Felicis IIviro q(uin)q(uennali) coloniae Thaenitanae fratri Quinti Aemili Laeti praefecti praetorio ob singularem innocentiam et in promerendis singulis universisque civibus examina tam adfectionem ordo Thaenensium statuam equestrem ponendam de publico decreto decreto decurionum pecunia publica\n
EDCS-15100016.translation: To Aemilius Pudentus, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, centurion of the III Augustan legion, also of the II Augustan legion, again of the II Augustan legion, also of the XI Claudian legion, admitted into the retinue of the Emperor Commodus Augustus Pius Felix, quinquennial duovir of the colony of Thaena, for his brother Quintus Aemilius Laetus, prefect of the praetorium, because of singular innocence and his careful attention to individuals and the citizens as a whole, the council of the Thaenitani, by public decree of the decurions, with public money, [ordered to erect] an equestrian statue.
EDCS-15100016.summary: A decree by the council of Thaena to erect an equestrian statue to Aemilius Pudentus, a military officer and local magistrate, in honor of his brother Quintus Aemilius Laetus, a prefect of the praetorium, likely dating to the reign of Commodus.

EDCS-15100322.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius Licinianus lived 19 years and 8 months; he lies here. Julius Secundianus, his devoted father, placed this. Sacred to the departed spirits. Manilia Saturnina lived 2 years and 10 months; she lies here. Julius Secundianus, her devoted grandfather, placed this. Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelia Iulia Crescentia lived 22 years; she lies here. Julius Secundianus, her dearest husband, placed this.
EDCS-15100322.summary: A complex funerary monument with three epitaphs, recording the deaths of Julius Licinianus, Manilia Saturnina, and Cornelia Iulia Crescentia, with two of them set up by Julius Secundianus in his roles as father and husband, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100324.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Iulius Saturninus, devoted, lived 35 years; he lies here. Quintus Iulius Fortunatus placed this for his devoted brother, a good man.
EDCS-15100324.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Iulius Saturninus, set up by his brother Quintus Iulius Fortunatus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100325.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iulius [illegible] lived [illegible]... lived 85 years; he lies here.
EDCS-15100325.summary: A damaged funerary epitaph recording the death of a Caius Iulius, with only his age at death partially preserved, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100326.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julius [illegible]nus lived 32 years and [illegible] months; he lies here. Iulius Liberalis placed this for his brother.
EDCS-15100326.summary: A funerary epitaph for an unnamed Julius, set up by his brother Iulius Liberalis, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100327.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Nampamina lived 80 years. Iulius Antiochenus set this up. Here she lies. [Sacred to the departed spirits] Iulia [lived]...
EDCS-15100327.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Nampamina, erected by Iulius Antiochenus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15100330.translation: Iunia Fructuosa, daughter of Marcus, lived 22 years and 11 months. Here she lies. Pleminius Donatus set this up for his dearest wife.
EDCS-15100330.summary: A funerary monument erected by Pleminius Donatus for his wife, Iunia Fructuosa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100331.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Memia Quietula lived [X] years. Here she lies. Liberalis for himself and the same Quietula for herself while alive set this up. Sacred to the departed spirits. Liberalis lived [X] years. Here he lies. He himself set this up for himself while alive.
EDCS-15100331.summary: A double funerary inscription, possibly for a couple, recording the lives of Memia Quietula and Liberalis, who prepared their tombs in advance.

EDCS-15100332.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinius Donatus lived 60 years. Here he lies. Licinia Tertiola set up this tomb for her father, out of dutiful affection.
EDCS-15100332.summary: A funerary monument set up by Licinia Tertiola for her father, Licinius Donatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100333.translation: To the departed spirits. Ligarius,(?) son of Ligarius,(?) Ligarianus lived 30 years. Ligarius Liberalis set this up for his devoted brother. Here he lies.
EDCS-15100333.summary: A funerary inscription for Ligarius Liberalis, erected by his brother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100334.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sextus Magnius Verus lived 53 years. Here he lies. Quintus Magnius Hilarius(?) set this up for his devoted father.
EDCS-15100334.summary: A funerary monument erected by Q. Magnius Hilarius(?) for his father, Sex. Magnius Verus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100335.translation: [Manilius Longus, son of Lucius, lived 54 years. Here he lies.] Quintus Manilius Maximus set this up for [his] best and devoted [father/son/brother].
EDCS-15100335.summary: A funerary inscription, partially restored, for Manilius Longus, with a dedication likely by Quintus Manilius Maximus.

EDCS-15100336.translation: Quintus Manilius Maximus, possibly a perpetual flamen, lived 52 years and 5 months. Here he lies. Iulia Stolis set this up for her devoted husband.
EDCS-15100336.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Manilius Maximus, possibly a local religious official, erected by his wife Iulia Stolis.

EDCS-15100337.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Manilia Rufina lived 45 years. Vinnius Silo set this up for his most devoted wife. Here she lies.
EDCS-15100337.summary: A funerary monument erected by Vinnius Silo for his wife, Manilia Rufina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100338.translation: [This tomb is for] Mantia Lucisca, daughter of Publius, [his] excellent grandmother. She lived 80 years.
EDCS-15100338.summary: A funerary inscription for Mantia Lucisca, honoring her as an excellent grandmother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100339.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Marius Pasi[3] lived 75 years. Publius Marius Saturninus, his son, placed this for his father.
EDCS-15100339.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Publius Marius Pasi[3] and erected by his son, Publius Marius Saturninus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100340.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcia Fortuna lived 90 years and is buried here. Marci Fortunatus Secundinus Ampliatus Honoratus, her sons, placed this for their dutiful mother.
EDCS-15100340.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Marcia Fortuna, who lived 90 years, and was erected by her sons, Marci Fortunatus Secundinus Ampliatus Honoratus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100341.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Margarita lived 20 years and is buried here. Venustus placed this for his wife.
EDCS-15100341.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Margarita, who lived 20 years, and was erected by her husband, Venustus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100342.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Marius Felix lived 67 years and is buried here. Perellia Saturnina placed this for her husband.
EDCS-15100342.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Quintus Marius Felix, who lived 67 years, and was erected by his wife, Perellia Saturnina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100345.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Memmia Saturnina [---]. Sacred to the departed spirits. Q(uintus) Memmius Saturninus lived 83 years and is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Donata lived 90 years and is buried here. Memmius Aemilianus placed this for his dutiful mother.
EDCS-15100345.summary: This inscription appears to be a composite epitaph, recording the deaths of Memmia Saturnina, Quintus Memmius Saturninus, and Aemilia Donata, with Memmius Aemilianus erecting a monument for his mother, Aemilia Donata; it likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100346.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Mevia Felicitas lived 40 years. Quintus Calpurnius Fortunatus placed this for his dearest wife, a most unworthy spouse who too quickly departed this life; miserable one, you ought to have lived a hundred years. For you were more beautiful than others, and possessed eloquence and modesty beyond compare, and I will not say that such qualities existed or will exist to be surpassed. But since the spirits exist, may the earth lie lightly upon you. She is buried here.
EDCS-15100346.summary: This is a tombstone for Mevia Felicitas, who died at 40, erected by her husband Quintus Calpurnius Fortunatus, who laments her early death and praises her beauty, eloquence, and modesty, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100347.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felicitas lived 4 years and 4 months. Her dutiful mother placed this.
EDCS-15100347.summary: This is a tombstone for Felicitas, who died at the age of 4 years and 4 months, erected by her mother, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100348.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Sentius Fortunatus lived 90 years. Gaius Sentius Rogatianus, his son, placed this for his dutiful father. He is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Minucius Valentinus lived 88 years. His dutiful sons placed this for their father. He is buried here.
EDCS-15100348.summary: This inscription is a composite tombstone recording two burials: Gaius Sentius Fortunatus, who lived 90 years and was honored by his son Gaius Sentius Rogatianus, and Gaius Minucius Valentinus, who lived 88 years and was honored by his sons; it likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100349.translation: [---]tia, daughter of Sextus [---]. Sacred to the departed spirits. Modia Maxima, daughter of Caius, dutiful, lived 24 years and is buried here. Caius Modius Maximus placed this for his excellent daughter. Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Modius Maximus, son of Gaius, lived 24 years and 2 months and is buried here. Caius Modius Maximus placed this for his son.
EDCS-15100349.summary: This inscription records multiple burials, including Modia Maxima, who died at 24 and was honored by her father Caius Modius Maximus, and Gaius Modius Maximus, who also died at 24 and was honored by his father also named Caius Modius Maximus; it likely dates to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100352.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nonia Suriscina, daughter of Marcu[s], is buried here.
EDCS-15100352.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Nonia Suriscina, daughter of Marcu[s], likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-15100353.translation: To the divine spirits. Quintus Orbius Salutaris Connari lived for 28 years, one month, and 26 days. He lies buried here. His adopted son placed this.
EDCS-15100353.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Orbius Salutaris Connari, who lived for 28 years, one month, and 26 days, with the monument erected by his adopted son.

EDCS-15100355.translation: To the spirits, here Passiena Casta rests in peaceful shades. She lived 45 years, pious, generous, kind. Her father and brothers erect this tomb for her.
EDCS-15100355.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Passiena Casta, who lived 45 years, erected by her father and brothers.

EDCS-15100356.translation: To the divine spirits. Petronia Casta lived for 75 years. She lies buried here. Kastus, her son, placed this for his mother.
EDCS-15100356.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Petronia Casta, who lived 75 years, erected by her son Kastus.

EDCS-15100357.translation: To the divine spirits. Plotius Alexander lived...
EDCS-15100357.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph for Plotius Alexander.

EDCS-15100358.translation: To the divine spirits. Pompeia Fortunata lived for 85 years. Her devoted sons placed this. To the divine spirits. Publius Sextilius Donatus lived for 60 years, a devoted wife. She placed this for her husband.
EDCS-15100358.summary: This inscription is a double epitaph, recording Pompeia Fortunata, who lived 85 years and was commemorated by her sons, and Publius Sextilius Donatus, who lived 60 years and was commemorated by his wife.

EDCS-15100359.translation: To the spirits. Pomponia Quarta, daughter of Caius, pious, lived for 18 years.
EDCS-15100359.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pomponia Quarta, daughter of Caius, who lived 18 years.

EDCS-15100360.translation: To the divine spirits. Pomponia Valeria lived for 15 years...
EDCS-15100360.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph for Pomponia Valeria, who lived 15 years.

EDCS-15100362.translation: To the divine spirits. Publius Praecilius Pollio, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived for [3] years. He lies buried here. His brothers, Praecilius Fidus and Vitalis, saw to its construction.
EDCS-15100362.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Praecilius Pollio, son of Marcus, who lived an unspecified number of years and was commemorated by his brothers, Fidus and Vitalis.

EDCS-15100363.translation: To the divine spirits. Refrius Successus lived for 47 years. He lies buried here. Refrius Nundinarius placed this for his devoted brother.
EDCS-15100363.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Refrius Successus, who lived 47 years, erected by his brother Refrius Nundinarius.

EDCS-15100364.translation: To the divine spirits. Gaius Remmius Felix, son of Caius, a rope maker, pious, lived for 21 years.
EDCS-15100364.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Remmius Felix, son of Caius, a rope maker, who lived 21 years.

EDCS-15100365.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Remmia Tertullina Mustelica lived 23 years. Gaius Titius Clemens, a dutiful man, erected this for his dutiful wife, who rests here.
EDCS-15100365.summary: A funerary epitaph for Remmia Tertullina Mustelica, erected by her husband, Gaius Titius Clemens.

EDCS-15100366.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Remmia Secunda, daughter of Caius, who lived 28 years.
EDCS-15100366.summary: A funerary epitaph for Remmia Secunda, daughter of Caius, with an incomplete line indicating the mother's name.

EDCS-15100367.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Rutilius Vitalis, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, lived 38 years and rests here. Publius Rutilius Fortunatus erected this for his excellent brother.
EDCS-15100367.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Rutilius Vitalis, erected by his brother, Publius Rutilius Fortunatus.

EDCS-15100368.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Sempronius Lucifer, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, lived a dutiful 80 years. His heirs, Quintus Sempronius Antenor and Sempronia Mallina, erected this for their patron.
EDCS-15100368.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Sempronius Lucifer, erected by his heirs, Quintus Sempronius Antenor and Sempronia Mallina.

EDCS-15100369.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sempronia Mallina lived 80 years.
EDCS-15100369.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sempronia Mallina, with an incomplete line.

EDCS-15100370.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextia Fortunata lived...
EDCS-15100370.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextia Fortunata, with incomplete life span and name restoration.

EDCS-15100371.translation: Tannonius Marcellus lived 70 years. Tannonius Sterceius erected this for his dutiful father, who rests here.
EDCS-15100371.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tannonius Marcellus, erected by his son, Tannonius Sterceius.

EDCS-15100373.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Titinius Fuscus lived 80 years and rests here. Gaius Titinius Fuscus erected this for his father. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Agria Extricata lived 70 years and rests here. Gaius Titinius made this for his mother. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Victoria lived 50 years. Gaius Titinius Fuscus erected this for his wife.
EDCS-15100373.summary: A funerary monument with three epitaphs: Marcus Titinius Fuscus (erected by son Gaius), Agria Extricata (erected by son Gaius), and Iulia Victoria (erected by husband Gaius Titinius Fuscus).

EDCS-15100374.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Trebius Ianuarius lived 15 years and rests here. Annia Maxima erected this for her dutiful son. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Trebius Maximus lived 45 years and rests here. His dutiful wife erected this.
EDCS-15100374.summary: A funerary monument with two epitaphs: Gaius Trebius Ianuarius (erected by mother Annia Maxima) and Gaius Trebius Maximus (erected by his wife).

EDCS-15100375.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Valerius Institor lived 55 years and rests here.
EDCS-15100375.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Institor.

EDCS-15100376.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valeria Dubitata lived 5 years and 20 days; her father placed [this] for his daughter.
EDCS-15100376.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Valeria Dubitata, who died at the age of 5 years and 20 days, and was commemorated by her father.

EDCS-15100377.translation: Here lies Varius, known as Frontonianus, whom his delightful wife Cornelia Galla placed here, restoring the sweet comforts of a former life; she set up marble likenesses of his face, eyes, and spirit, so that she could satisfy her love for him more deeply through his image. This will be her solace, for as long as she lives, her husband will be in her whole heart, and it is no wonder, for what woman has such qualities? [---] he was pious, [---] indulgent, [---] and happy, [---] but [---] snatched away [---] for he [---] and not [---] in the year [---].
EDCS-15100377.summary: This is a lengthy funerary epitaph for Varius Frontonianus, commemorating his character and the deep love of his wife Cornelia Galla, who erected the monument.

EDCS-15100378.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Venustus, pious, lived 80 years; he lies here. [---] placed for his most pious father.
EDCS-15100378.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Venustus, who died at the age of 80, and whose monument was erected by his son.

EDCS-15100381.translation: Caia Ve/tutia, a respectable matron, lived [---] years.
EDCS-15100381.summary: This is a funerary epitaph fragment for Caia Vetutia, a matron, recording her name and an incomplete lifespan.

EDCS-15100382.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Volussena Ianuar(i)a lived 38 years; she lies here. Lucius Granius Victor placed [this] for his most excellent wife. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Volussena Gududia lived 96 years; she lies here. Lucius Granius Victor placed [this] for his daughter.
EDCS-15100382.summary: This funerary monument records two women, Volussena Ianuar(i)a and Volussena Gududia, both honored by Lucius Granius Victor, one as his wife and the other as his daughter.

EDCS-15100411.translation: [---] Rufus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, lived 94 years; he lies here. Marcus Nunnius Secundinus placed [this] for his dear uncle.
EDCS-15100411.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rufus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, who lived to 94, and was commemorated by his nephew, Marcus Nunnius Secundinus.

EDCS-15100412.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Caelius Felix, priest, lived 60 years. Quintus Caelius Felix, his son, placed [this] for his pious father.
EDCS-15100412.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Caelius Felix, a priest who died at 60, erected by his son of the same name.

EDCS-15100415.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Atilia Quartilla lived 35 years; she lies here. Cornelius Saxo placed [this] for his dearest wife.
EDCS-15100415.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Atilia Quartilla, who died at 35, and was commemorated by her husband, Cornelius Saxo.

EDCS-15100416.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caecilia Concordia, dutiful, lived 32 years; she lies here.
EDCS-15100416.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia Concordia, who died at the age of 32.

EDCS-15100424.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Secundina, a chaste virgin, lived 19 years and 7 months; she lies here. Whoever reads this, may you know that [---] made this.
EDCS-15100424.summary: This funerary epitaph records Flavia Secundina, a virgin who died at 19 years and 7 months, with a final admonition to the reader.

EDCS-15100427.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Nonia Prima lived 23 years. Julius Donatus erected this for his devoted wife.
EDCS-15100427.summary: A funerary epitaph for Nonia Prima, set up by her husband, Julius Donatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100428.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Postumia Mustacia, freedwoman of Caius, lived 41 years. Pullimer erected this for his most devoted wife here.
EDCS-15100428.summary: A funerary epitaph for Postumia Mustacia, erected by her husband, Pullimer, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100436.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fructuosa lived 24 years and 5 months. Manilius Messallinus erected this for his devoted daughter here.
EDCS-15100436.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fructuosa, erected by her father, Manilius Messallinus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100439.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Plotia, a chaste and modest woman, lived 54 years. Julius Generosus erected this for his most remarkable wife here.
EDCS-15100439.summary: A funerary epitaph for Plotia, erected by her husband, Julius Generosus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100440.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Fortunata lived 43 years. Agrius Rufinus made this for his dearest mother here. She rests here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Agrius Quartus lived 57 years. He erected this for his dearest husband here. He rests here.
EDCS-15100440.summary: A double funerary epitaph, recording the burials of Aemilia Fortunata, erected by her son Agrius Rufinus, and Marcus Agrius Quartus, erected by his husband, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100441.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Gabinius Gentilis lived 65 years and, while alive, arranged for this tomb for himself and his wife Ianuaria. He rests here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pompeia Ianuaria lived 60 years. She rests here. Gabinia Adauctula arranged for the inscription for her exceedingly devoted parents.
EDCS-15100441.summary: A double funerary epitaph recording self-funded burials for Gaius Gabinius Gentilis and Pompeia Ianuaria, with an additional note about parents by Gabinia Adauctula, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100443.translation: Of Lucius Calpurnius, son of Marcus, of the Tromentina tribe, from Aquae Statiellae. He lived 35 years, served 13 years as a soldier in the III Augusta legion. Philmusus Eutychus Amaranthus, his freedman and heir, rests buried here.
EDCS-15100443.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Calpurnius, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, erected by his freedman and heir, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100444.translation: Publius Vettius, son of Publius, of the Velina tribe, a soldier in the III Augusta legion, a trumpeter, rests here.
EDCS-15100444.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Vettius, a trumpeter in the III Augusta legion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100445.translation: Titus Pompeius Macrinus, son of Titus, veteran of the III Augusta legion, lived 85 years. He rests here.
EDCS-15100445.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Pompeius Macrinus, a veteran of the III Augusta legion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100446.translation: [...]lia Amata, high priestess, lived 100 years. She rests here. II Felix III were there.
EDCS-15100446.summary: A funerary epitaph for [...]lia Amata, a high priestess, mentioning two other individuals and possibly a number of burials, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15100450.translation: Antonia Amia, devout, lived 90 years; here she lies.
EDCS-15100450.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, piety, lifespan, and burial place of Antonia Amia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300049.translation: Pompeia Kariota lived 21 years; here she lies.
EDCS-15300049.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, lifespan, and burial place of Pompeia Kariota, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300051.translation: Berec Balyuri lived [---] years; here he lies.
EDCS-15300051.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and burial place of Berec Balyuri, with a partially lost lifespan, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300052.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name and lifespan of Marcus Caelius Tuscus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300053.translation: Gaius Calpurnius Caprarius lived 50 years; here he lies.
EDCS-15300053.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, lifespan, and burial place of Gaius Calpurnius Caprarius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300054.translation: Camuria Secunda lived 100 years; here she lies.
EDCS-15300054.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, lifespan, and burial place of Camuria Secunda, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300055.translation: Lucius Carmeius Rogatus lived 12 years; here he lies.
EDCS-15300055.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, lifespan, and burial place of Lucius Carmeius Rogatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300060.translation: Publius Eppius Felix lived 25 years; here he lies.
EDCS-15300060.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, lifespan, and burial place of Publius Eppius Felix, with a partial restoration, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300077.translation: Gaius Manilius Titulus lived 90 years; here he lies.
EDCS-15300077.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, lifespan, and burial place of Gaius Manilius Titulus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300082.translation: Sextus Munniatius Communus, son of Primigenius, lived [---] 8 years; here he lies.
EDCS-15300082.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, parentage, partially restored lifespan, and burial place of Sextus Munniatius Communus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300084.translation: Munniatia, daughter of Sextus, lived 25 years; she rests here.
EDCS-15300084.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Munniatia, a woman who died at age 25.

EDCS-15300090.translation: Cubelia, daughter of Caius, lived 20 years; she rests here.
EDCS-15300090.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Cubelia, a woman who died at age 20.

EDCS-15300160.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano optimo Augusto Germanico Parthico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XX imperatori XII consuli Vl Caecilius Faustinus proconsul dedicavit decreto decurionum pecunia publica\n
EDCS-15300160.translation: Caecilius Faustinus, proconsul, dedicated this at the decree of the town councillors with public money to Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Optimus Augustus Germanicus Parthicus, son of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 20th time, Imperator for the 12th time, Consul for the 6th time.
EDCS-15300160.summary: An official dedication to Emperor Trajan, likely from a provincial capital, commissioned by the proconsul Caecilius Faustinus on behalf of the local council.

EDCS-15400826.translation: Iulius Ingenus died in Gaul; his wife entrusts his body to the embrace of his homeland. Virula earned her place in Germany, serving as a speculator and cornicularius of the legion. The beginning of his life was its end.
EDCS-15400826.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulius Ingenus, a soldier who died in Gaul, and his wife Virula, with a philosophical reflection on life and death.

EDCS-15600039.text_edition: \n Memoria marturum sic Rogati Vitalis IESTES animas s perenn \n
EDCS-15600039.translation: In memory of the martyrs, Rogatus Vitalis, your souls live on forever.
EDCS-15600039.summary: A memorial inscription for martyrs, possibly referring to Rogatus Vitalis, invoking their eternal remembrance.

EDCS-15600040.summary: A short epitaph for a person named Maxima, indicating peaceful rest.

EDCS-15600042.summary: A simple epitaph stating the burial place of Sabina.

EDCS-15600922.text_edition: \n Caio Vettio Grato Attico Sabiniano clarissimo puero filio Cai Vetti Grati Sabiniani clarissimi viri tribuni militum legionis VII Claudiae quaestoris kandidati nepotis sic Cai Vetti Sabiniani Hospitis clarissimae memoriae viri proconsulis municipium Aurelium Cpatrono decreto decurionum pecunia publica\n
EDCS-15600922.translation: Dedicated by the Municipality of Aurelium, as patron, by decree of the town councillors with public money, to Gaius Vettius Gratus Atticus Sabinianus, a most distinguished boy and son of Gaius Vettius Gratus Sabinianus, a most distinguished man and tribune of the soldiers of the Legio VII Claudia, candidate for quaestor, grandson of Gaius Vettius Sabinianus Hospes, a man of most distinguished memory and proconsul.
EDCS-15600922.summary: A public monument erected by a municipality to honor a young boy, Gaius Vettius Gratus Atticus Sabinianus, the son and grandson of distinguished officials.

EDCS-15600925.translation: To Quintus Considius Repostus Areianus, son of Nymphamon, who held the office of aedile twice and duovir, for his outstanding administration as duovir and his singular love for the citizens, the entire curia. Dedicated on the 11th day before the Kalends of March, when Agricola and Clementianus were consuls.
EDCS-15600925.summary: An honorific inscription to Quintus Considius Repostus Areianus for his service as aedile and duovir, dedicated by the town council during the consulship of Agricola and Clementianus.

EDCS-15600970.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Abutius Fortunatus lived 70 years piously; he rests here.
EDCS-15600970.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Abutius Fortunatus, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-15600971.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Agbor lived piously for 30 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-15600971.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Agbor, who died at age 30.

EDCS-15600972.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Aelius Felix lived piously for 81 years; he is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Celsina lived piously for 71 years; she is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Aelius Fortunatus lived piously for 52 years; he is buried here. EDCS-15900341.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-15900341.text_edition: \n In hok sic tumulo iaket sic korpus bonae memoriae RV pectore carus qui per gladium indomitem sic okisum sic bixit sic in pake sic plus minus annos XXV rekessit sic de hok sic saekulo sic sub die VI mensis Dekember sic indictione XIII amen\n
EDCS-15900341.translation: In this tomb lies the body of good memory, dear to my heart, who died by the sword, lived and rested in peace for more or less 25 years, departed from this world on the 6th day of the month of December, in the 13th indiction. Amen.
EDCS-15900341.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the violent death and age of approximately 25 years of an individual, dated by indiction.

EDCS-16100357.text_edition: \n Hic corpus iacet pueris Nominandi o benedicte puer paucis te terra diebus infantem tenuit caeliquae sic in regna remisit propterea et natus ut caperes tanta renatus\n
EDCS-16100357.translation: Here lies the body of the child Nominandus, blessed child. The earth held you only a few days; heaven recalled you, reborn, so that you might grasp such things.
EDCS-16100357.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an infant named Nominandus, emphasizing his brief life and immediate ascension to heaven.

EDCS-16100561.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Crispina, daughter (?), Felicissima, lived 50 years and rests here.

EDCS-16100593.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Faustina lived piously for 1 month and 21 days; she is buried here.
EDCS-16100593.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Faustina, recording her age at death in months and days.

EDCS-16100597.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felicula lived piously for 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-16100597.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felicula, recording her age at death.

EDCS-16100600.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felix, son of Proculus Sefessu, lived piously for 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-16100600.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felix, son of Proculus Sefessu, recording his age at death.

EDCS-16100601.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felix, son of Rogatus Sofossu, lived piously for 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-16100601.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felix, son of Rogatus Sofossu, recording his age at death.

EDCS-16100603.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Felix Sefessu lived piously for 51 years; he lies here.
EDCS-16100603.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Felix Sefessu, who lived for 51 years.

EDCS-16100604.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [Name] Festinus lived piously for 28 years; he lies here.
EDCS-16100604.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of a man named Festinus, who lived for 28 years, though his first name is lost.

EDCS-16100607.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Tida lived piously for 57 years.
EDCS-16100607.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Flavia Tida, who lived for 57 years.

EDCS-16100608.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavia Fortunata lived piously for 43 years.
EDCS-16100608.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Flavia Fortunata, who lived for 43 years.

EDCS-16100609.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fortunata lived piously for 30 years; she lies here.
EDCS-16100609.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Fortunata, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-16100612.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fortunata Quieta lived piously for 41 years; she lies here.
EDCS-16100612.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Fortunata Quieta, who lived for 41 years.

EDCS-16100613.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fortunata Eluvi, daughter of Saturninus, lived piously for 85 years; she lies here.
EDCS-16100613.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Fortunata Eluvi, daughter of Saturninus, who lived for 85 years.

EDCS-16100618.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fortunatus Felix, son of Tiro, lived piously for 51 years; he lies here.
EDCS-16100618.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Fortunatus Felix, son of Tiro, who lived for 51 years.

EDCS-16100619.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fortunatus Rufus lived piously for 20 years; he lies here.
EDCS-16100619.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Fortunatus Rufus, who lived for 20 years.

EDCS-16100621.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Fortunius Magnus Bellus lived for about 67 years; he lies here.
EDCS-16100621.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Fortunius Magnus Bellus, who lived for approximately 67 years.

EDCS-16100625.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Fulvia Firma lived 76 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-16100625.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fulvia Firma, who lived to be 76 years old.

EDCS-16100628.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Furnia Salsula, dutiful, lived 20 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-16100628.summary: A funerary epitaph for Furnia Salsula, who died at the age of 20 and is described as dutiful.

EDCS-16200133.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Helena Mucia, dutiful, lived 21 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-16200133.summary: A funerary epitaph for Helena Mucia, who lived 21 years and is noted for her dutifulness.

EDCS-16200134.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Granius Fortunius lived 63 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-16200134.summary: A funerary epitaph for Granius Fortunius, who lived 63 years.

EDCS-16200136.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gargilius Solutor, dutiful, lived 51 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-16200136.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gargilius Solutor, who lived 51 years and is described as dutiful.

EDCS-16200137.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Galla, daughter of Arsacenis, dutiful, lived 80 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-16200137.summary: A funerary epitaph for Galla Arsacenis, who lived 80 years and is noted for her dutifulness.

EDCS-16200141.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Geminia Fortunata, dutiful, lived 71 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-16200141.summary: A funerary epitaph for Geminia Fortunata, who lived 71 years and is described as dutiful.

EDCS-16201257.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Marius Clodius, dutiful, lived 51 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-16201257.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Marius Clodius, who lived 51 years and is described as dutiful.

EDCS-16201259.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Mattius Pullaienus Bellicus, son of Marcus, of the Arnensis tribe, dutiful, lived 71 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-16201259.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Mattius Pullaienus Bellicus, who lived 71 years, was a son of Marcus, and belonged to the Arnensis tribe.

EDCS-16201260.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Mattius Pullaienus Bellicus, dutiful, lived 55 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-16201260.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Mattius Pullaienus Bellicus, who lived 55 years and is described as dutiful.

EDCS-16201261.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Lucceius Gududianus, dutiful, lived 12 years, here he rests. To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Augustalis, son of Lucceius, dutiful, lived 7 years, here he rests. To the Spirits of the Departed. Furia Faustina, dutiful, lived 85 years, here she rests. To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Lucceius Fortunatus, dutiful, lived 87 years, here he rests.
EDCS-16201261.summary: This inscription contains four separate epitaphs for members of the Lucceius family, recording their names, ages at death, and burial.

EDCS-16201262.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Iulius Messor, dutiful grandson, lived 2 years, here he rests. To the Spirits of the Departed. Tonneia Venusta, dutiful, lived 65 years, here she rests.
EDCS-16201262.summary: This inscription contains two epitaphs for Lucius Iulius Messor and Tonneia Venusta, recording their names and ages at death.

EDCS-16201264.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [Labennius] Faustinus, dutiful, lived 80 years, here he rests.
EDCS-16201264.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph for Labennius Faustinus, recording his name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-16201273.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Iulius Aratorianus, dutiful, lived 26 years, here he rests.
EDCS-16201273.summary: This is an epitaph for Quintus Iulius Aratorianus, recording his name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-16201276.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Iulius Castus, dutiful, lived 75 years, father, I prepared this tomb, I established it for myself, I completed five and seventy years, I survived my son who will always tend the hearth. Here he rests. To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Namphamina, dutiful, lived 61 years, here she rests.
EDCS-16201276.summary: This inscription contains two epitaphs: one for Quintus Iulius Castus, who prepared his own tomb, and another for Iulia Namphamina, recording their names and ages at death.

EDCS-16201281.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Likewise, Nartialus made this for his father Iulius Felix, the best father, who lived 85 years.
EDCS-16201281.summary: This is an epitaph dedicated by Nartialus to his father Iulius Felix, recording the father's name, relationship, and age at death.

EDCS-16201282.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. And likewise, Nartialis made this for his mother Iulia Prima, the best mother, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-16201282.summary: This is an epitaph dedicated by Nartialis to his mother Iulia Prima, recording the mother's name, relationship, and age at death.

EDCS-16201283.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Iulius Fortunatus, dutiful, lived 75 years, here he rests.
EDCS-16201283.summary: This is an epitaph for Gaius Iulius Fortunatus, recording his name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-16201284.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Fortunatus, son of Solutor, dutiful, lived 18 years, here he rests.
EDCS-16201284.summary: This is an epitaph for Iulius Fortunatus, son of Solutor, recording his name, parentage, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-16201287.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Iulius Namphamo, dutiful, lived 95 years, here he rests.
EDCS-16201287.summary: This is an epitaph for Gaius Iulius Namphamo, recording his name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-16201292.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Quietus, son of Caius, Saturninus, piously lived 19 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-16201292.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Quietus, who died at the age of 19, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201301.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Julius Varro piously lived 58 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-16201301.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Julius Varro, who died at the age of 58, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201309.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Italia piously lived 52 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-16201309.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulia Italia, who died at the age of 52, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201320.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Privata piously lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-16201320.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulia Privata, who died at the age of 75, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201322.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Regilla, daughter of Caius, piously lived 26 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-16201322.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulia Regilla, daughter of Caius, who died at the age of 26, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201329.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia 3via Pullaiena piously lived 3 months; she is buried here.
EDCS-16201329.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulia 3via Pullaiena, likely a child who died at the age of 3 months, found in a Roman Imperial period context.

EDCS-16201330.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iunia Secundula piously lived [...] years
EDCS-16201330.summary: This is an incomplete tombstone for Iunia Secundula, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording her age at death.

EDCS-16201331.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iunia Victoria piously lived 27 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-16201331.summary: This is a tombstone for Iunia Victoria, who died at the age of 27, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201346.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Luria Lucana piously lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-16201346.summary: This is a tombstone for Luria Lucana, who died at the age of 40, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201355.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Maenia Marcellina, daughter of Publius, piously lived 51 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-16201355.summary: This is a tombstone for Maenia Marcellina, daughter of Publius, who died at the age of 51, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201357.translation: To the departed spirits. Magnius Felix Extricatinus, son of Mustulus, a dutiful man, lived for 5 months. He is buried here.
EDCS-16201357.summary: A tombstone for Magnius Felix Extricatinus, who died at 5 months old, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201360.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Magnius Minnus Fortunatianus, a dutiful man, lived for 2 years and 6 months. He is buried here. To the departed spirits. Lucius Magnius Ingenuus Marcianus, a dutiful man, lived for [X] years and 10 months. He is buried here.
EDCS-16201360.summary: A tombstone recording the deaths of two individuals, Lucius Magnius Minnus Fortunatianus (died at 2 years, 6 months) and Lucius Magnius Ingenuus Marcianus (died at 10 months), dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201389.translation: To the departed spirits. Minicius Salvidenus, son of Primus, a dutiful man, lived for 50 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-16201389.summary: A tombstone for Minicius Salvidenus, who died at 50 years of age, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201391.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Modius Firmus, a dutiful man, lived for 62 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-16201391.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Modius Firmus, who died at 62 years of age, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201398.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Marius Candidus, a dutiful man, lived for 80 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-16201398.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Marius Candidus, who died at 80 years of age, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201856.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Sexsus sic Volcacius Marsus vixit annis CXV Antonia Urbana vixit annis LX Helvia Urbana vixit annis LXX\n
EDCS-16201856.translation: To the departed spirits. Sexsus sic Volcacius Marsus lived for 115 years. Antonia Urbana lived for 60 years. Helvia Urbana lived for 70 years.
EDCS-16201856.summary: A tombstone for three individuals named Sexsus sic Volcacius Marsus, Antonia Urbana, and Helvia Urbana, recording their ages at death, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201857.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aelius Honoratus pius vixit annis LXXXIII mensibus V Gratus Gaius Cuminus Donatus filii fecerunt\n
EDCS-16201857.translation: To the departed spirits. Aelius Honoratus, a dutiful man, lived for 83 years and 5 months. Gratus Gaius Cuminus Donatus, his sons, made this.
EDCS-16201857.summary: A tombstone for Aelius Honoratus, who died at 83 years and 5 months, erected by his sons, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201858.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fortunatus Donati filius vixit annis XXII mensibus sexs sic Donatus pater fecit\n
EDCS-16201858.translation: To the departed spirits. Fortunatus, son of Donatus, lived for 22 years and six months. Donatus, his father, made this.
EDCS-16201858.summary: A tombstone for Fortunatus, son of Donatus, who died at 22 years and 6 months, erected by his father, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16201859.text_edition: \n Minervae Victoriae Augustae sacrum pro salute Auggg(ustorum) Imppp(eratorum) Severi et Antonini et Getae et Iuliae Domnae Augustae coloni Gamoniensis sua pecunia fecerunt\n
EDCS-16201859.translation: Sacred to Minerva Victoria Augusta for the safety of the Emperors Augustus, Severus, Antoninus, Geta, and Augusta Julia Domna; the colonists of Gamundia made this with their own money.
EDCS-16201859.summary: A dedication to Minerva Victoria Augusta by the colonists of Gamundia, praying for the safety of Emperors Severus, Antoninus, Geta, and Julia Domna, dated to their reigns (early 3rd century CE).

EDCS-16201867.text_edition: \n Hygiae Augustae sacrum pro salute et victoriis Impperatorum Caessarum Luci Septimii Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti et Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti et Luci Septimii Getae nobilissimi Caesaris et Iuliae Domnae Augustae matris Aug(ustorum) et castrorum totiusque divinae domus eorum Lucius Baebius Secundus et Iulia Victorina uxor eius votum solverunt\n
EDCS-16201867.translation: Sacred to Hygieia Augusta for the safety and victories of the Emperors Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, and Caesar Lucius Septimius Geta Nobilissimus, and Julia Domna Augusta, mother of the Augusti and of the camp, and of their entire divine family. Lucius Baebius Secundus and his wife Iulia Victorina fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-16201867.summary: A votive dedication to Hygieia Augusta by Lucius Baebius Secundus and his wife Iulia Victorina, praying for the safety and victories of Emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, Geta, Julia Domna, and their family, dated to their reigns (early 3rd century CE).

EDCS-16201878.text_edition: \n Publio Attio Deciano Felici Matutino clarissimo puero civi et patrono \n
EDCS-16201878.translation: To Publius Attius Decianus Felix Matutinus, a most distinguished boy, citizen and patron.
EDCS-16201878.summary: This is a dedication to a young boy named Publius Attius Decianus Felix Matutinus, identified as a citizen and patron, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-16201883.text_edition: \n Splendonii Lucio Clodio Luci filio Quirina Titiano qui in IANI et IIviratu admodum floret IIOR cultoribusque ium ediderunt \n
EDCS-16201883.translation: To Lucius Clodius Titianus, son of Quirinus, son of Splendonius, who flourishes greatly in the office of IANVS and IIvirate, and his cult-followers, they dedicated.
EDCS-16201883.summary: This inscription records the dedication by cult followers of Lucius Clodius Titianus, who held the IIvirate office and was a flourishing figure, likely in a provincial town.

EDCS-16201905.text_edition: \n Pro salute Augusti Quintus Bennius Felix Palus Victor filius Aesculapio vota reddiderunt\n
EDCS-16201905.translation: For the safety of Augustus, Quintus Bennius Felix Palus Victor, son, paid vows to Aesculapius.
EDCS-16201905.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintus Bennius Felix Palus Victor, dedicating a promise to the god Aesculapius for the health of the Emperor (Augustus), likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-16201908.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Peticius Ianuarius vixit annis LXVII et Peticia Procula uxor eius vixit annis Publius Peticius Martialis filius parentibus praestantissimis fecit et dedicavit ex officina Cittini\n
EDCS-16201908.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Peticius Ianuarius lived for 67 years, and his wife Peticia Procula lived for [XX] years. Publius Peticius Martialis, their son, made and dedicated this for his most excellent parents, from the workshop of Cittinus.
EDCS-16201908.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Peticius Ianuarius and his wife Peticia Procula, set up by their son, indicating the date of death and provenance from a workshop, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-16201909.text_edition: \n Quirino deo Patri Genio municipii Augusto sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate consulis patris patriae proconsulis et Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae matris Augusti et castrorum et senatus et patriae totiusque domus divinae Lucius Turius Martialis et Quintus Cornelius Felix Emeritus aediles inlatis rei publicae summis honorariis aedilitatis suae amplius statuam aeream ex SS VIIIImilibus nummum et basim patriae dono dederunt et dedicaverunt ob quam dedicationem ludos scaenicos sic ediderunt et gymnasium biduo dederunt\n
EDCS-16201909.translation: Sacred to Quirinus the Father God, to the Genius of the municipality, and to Augustus. For the safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and to Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of Augustus, of the camp, of the senate, and of the whole fatherland, and of the entire divine house, Lucius Turius Martialis and Quintus Cornelius Felix Emeritus, aediles, having presented the highest honorary gifts of their aedileship to the state, donated and dedicated a bronze statue weighing 8000 pounds and a base to the fatherland, and for this dedication they presented theatrical games and a gymnasium for two days.
EDCS-16201909.summary: This lengthy inscription details the dedication of a bronze statue and base by aediles Lucius Turius Martialis and Quintus Cornelius Felix Emeritus, for the safety of Emperor Severus Alexander and Julia Mamaea, which included public games and a gymnasium, dating to the reign of Severus Alexander (222-235 CE).

EDCS-16201910.text_edition: \n Bonifatius Ianuarius Montius Eurialus Candurius Victor Eulogius Anfio Ninficus Fortunio Preiectus Anastasus Amor Nymficus\n
EDCS-16201910.translation: Bonifatius Ianuarius, Montius Eurialus, Candurius Victor, Eulogius Anfius, Nymficus, Fortunius, Praeiectus, Anastasius, Amor Nymficus.
EDCS-16201910.summary: This inscription is a list of names, possibly of officials, members of a group, or individuals associated with an event, without further context.

EDCS-16201927.text_edition: \n Caelesti sacrum Decimus Valerius Phoenix libens animo votum solvit\n
EDCS-16201927.translation: Sacred to Caelestis. Decimus Valerius Phoenix willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-16201927.summary: This is a votive inscription by Decimus Valerius Phoenix, dedicating a promise to the goddess Caelestis, likely from Roman North Africa.

EDCS-16201951.text_edition: \n Divo Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci patri Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Parthici Arabici maximi Britannici maximi pontificis maximi patris patriae ordo Mustitanus Pacatori deo decreto decurionum pecunia publica\n
EDCS-16201951.translation: To the deified Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax, father of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Arabicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, father of the fatherland, the council of Mustitana, to the god Pacator, by decree of the decurions, with public money.
EDCS-16201951.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the deified Emperor Septimius Severus by the council of Mustitana, using public funds, likely dating to the reign of his son Caracalla (211-217 CE).

EDCS-16201959.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris divi Severi Pii nepotis divi Magni Antonini Pii filii Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate VIIII consulis III patris patriae proconsulis Caius Timuleius Honoratus Felix Petronianus eques Romanus in urbe Roma Imperatoris domini nostri Alexandri Augusti sacerdos bidentalis iussus fecit\n
EDCS-16201959.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 9th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, descendant of the deified Severus Pius, son of the deified Magnus Antoninus Pius, Caius Timuleius Honoratus Felix Petronianus, a Roman knight, priest of our lord Emperor Alexander Augustus, performed this iussus (command).
EDCS-16201959.summary: This inscription records a religious act performed by Caius Timuleius Honoratus Felix Petronianus, a Roman knight and priest, for the safety of Emperor Severus Alexander, detailing his prestigious lineage and titles, likely dating to Severus Alexander's reign (222-235 CE).

EDCS-16300229.text_edition: \n [] [] [] [] municipium Septimium Aurelium Severianum Antoninianum frugiferum concordium liberum Thibursicensium bure thermas Gallienianas[3] reformatas et excultas pecunia publica perfecit et dedicavit proconsule Lucio Naevio Aquilino clarissimo viro patrono municipi dedicantibus Naevio Balbino Aquilino legato Karthaginis cclarissimis vviris patronis publicis ad cuius operis musaeum plerique decuriones HS XLI milia CC conl[atis ------\n
EDCS-16300229.translation: [...] the municipality of Septimium Aurelium Severianum Antoninianum, fruitful, harmonious, free, of Thibursicensians, [...] the Gallienian baths, reformed and improved, completed and dedicated with public money, under the proconsul Lucius Naevius Aquilinus, most distinguished man, patron of the municipality, with Naevius Balbinus Aquilinus, legate of Carthage, most distinguished men, public patrons, towards which work, the museum, many decurions contributed 41,000 sesterces and 200 [...]
EDCS-16300229.summary: This inscription records the completion and dedication of reformed baths in the municipality of Thibursicensium by public funds, under the proconsul Lucius Naevius Aquilinus and with contributions from decurions, likely dating to the period of Gallienus (mid-3rd century CE).

EDCS-16300411.text_edition: \n Claudio Chresto viro egregio patrono perpetuo civitatis Hadrumetinae Dyscoli liberti patrono bene merenti qui vixit annos plus minus LV\n
EDCS-16300411.translation: To Claudius Chrestus, an excellent man, perpetual patron of the city of Hadrumetum, a well-deserving patron, who lived for more or less 55 years, erected by his freedmen.
EDCS-16300411.summary: This is a funerary monument honoring Claudius Chrestus, a patron of Hadrumetum, with his age at death and the dedicant identified as his freedmen.

EDCS-16300643.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nahania Saturnina, daughter of Ianuarius, a dutiful woman, lived for 83 years. She rests here.
EDCS-16300643.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and age of Nahania Saturnina, daughter of Ianuarius, who lived to be 83.

EDCS-16300644.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nahania Saturnina, daughter of Honoratus, a dutiful woman, lived for 67 years. She rests here.
EDCS-16300644.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and age of Nahania Saturnina, daughter of Honoratus, who lived to be 67.

EDCS-16300645.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nampame, daughter of Diofantes, a dutiful woman, lived for 41 years. She rests here.
EDCS-16300645.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and age of Nampame, daughter of Diofantes, who lived to be 41.

EDCS-16300646.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Octavia Victoria, daughter of Zabullus, a dutiful woman, lived for 21 years. She rests here.
EDCS-16300646.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and age of Octavia Victoria, daughter of Zabullus, who lived to be 21.

EDCS-16300648.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Pacuvius Saturnus, a dutiful man, lived for 85 years. He rests here.
EDCS-16300648.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and age of Quintus Pacuvius Saturnus, who lived to be 85.

EDCS-16300649.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aulus Pompeius Salvius, a veteran, a dutiful man, lived for 65 years. He rests here.
EDCS-16300649.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and age of Aulus Pompeius Salvius, a veteran, who lived to be 65.

EDCS-16300650.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nahanius Saturninus, son of Saturninus, a dutiful man, lived for 52 years. He rests here.
EDCS-16300650.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and age of Nahanius Saturninus, son of Saturninus, who lived to be 52.

EDCS-16300651.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Numisius Honoratus, son of Marcus, from the Arnensis tribe, a dutiful man, lived for 81 years. Buried on his own land.
EDCS-16300651.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and age of Lucius Numisius Honoratus, who lived to be 81 and was buried on his own property.

EDCS-16300667.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Mucius Agrasius, a dutiful man, lived for 60 years. He rests here. Sacred to the departed spirits. Remmia Fructuosa, a dutiful woman, lived for 63 years. Sacred to the departed spirits. Mucia Veneria, a dutiful woman, lived for 13 years, 6 months, and 11 days.
EDCS-16300667.summary: This monument records the deaths and ages of three individuals: Quintus Mucius Agrasius (60 years), Remmia Fructuosa (63 years), and Mucia Veneria (13 years, 6 months, 11 days).

EDCS-16300673.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Nahanius Martialis, dutiful, lived 110 years, is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Totonnonia Rosula, dutiful, lived 85 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16300673.summary: This inscription is a double funerary monument for Quintus Nahanius Martialis and Totonnonia Rosula, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-16300674.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Namgedde, daughter of Adiutor and Mena, dutiful, lived 14 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16300674.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Namgedde, a young girl, recording her parentage and age at death.

EDCS-16300679.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Nanneia Sperata, dutiful, lived 30 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16300679.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age at death of Nanneia Sperata.

EDCS-16300683.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Nepotius Rogatianus, dutiful, lived 95 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16300683.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age at death of Publius Nepotius Rogatianus.

EDCS-16300697.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Otacilia Fortunata, daughter of Lucius, dutiful, lived 48 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16300697.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, parentage, and age at death of Otacilia Fortunata.

EDCS-16300717.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Pettius Macrinus, dutiful, lived 75 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16300717.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age at death of Quintus Pettius Macrinus.

EDCS-16300720.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aulus Pompeius Dexter Crescentianus, dutiful, lived 3 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16300720.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age at death of the very young Aulus Pompeius Dexter Crescentianus.

EDCS-16300723.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pompeia Caesteiana, dutiful, lived 10 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16300723.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age at death of the young Pompeia Caesteiana.

EDCS-16300724.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pompeia Honorata.
EDCS-16300724.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Pompeia Honorata.

EDCS-16300726.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Pomponius, son of Quintus.
EDCS-16300726.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription for Quintus Pomponius, son of Quintus.

EDCS-16300730.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Pomponius Satur[...]
EDCS-16300730.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Pomponius Satur, with the text incomplete.

EDCS-16300731.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Pomponius Surricus lived piously for 65 years; he rests here.
EDCS-16300731.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Pomponius Surricus, recording his death at age 65.

EDCS-16300732.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Pomponius Victor lived piously for 75 years; he rests here.
EDCS-16300732.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Pomponius Victor, recording his death at age 75.

EDCS-16300733.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pomponia Victoria, daughter of Marcus, who lived piously for 27 years, may your bones rest well, may the earth be light upon you; she rests here.
EDCS-16300733.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pomponia Victoria, recording her death at age 27 and wishing her a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-16300748.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pullaiena Honorata, daughter of Sextus, lived piously for 68 years; she rests here.
EDCS-16300748.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pullaiena Honorata, recording her death at age 68.

EDCS-16300753.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Remmia Fructa lived piously for 65 years; she rests here. // To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Remmius Floridus lived piously for 55 years; he rests here.
EDCS-16300753.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of two individuals, Remmia Fructa (age 65) and Gaius Remmius Floridus (age 55).

EDCS-16300768.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sessia Honorata lived piously for 105 years; she rests here. // To the spirits of the departed. Napotia Mamonica lived piously for 27 years; she rests here.
EDCS-16300768.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of two individuals, Sessia Honorata (age 105) and Napotia Mamonica (age 27).

EDCS-16300822.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Mundicius Ianurius of the Rhodenian tribe lived piously for 70 years; he rests here.
EDCS-16300822.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Mundicius Ianurius, recording his death at age 70.

EDCS-16400156.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Servilius Faustus lived piously for 67 years; he rests here.
EDCS-16400156.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Servilius Faustus, recording his death at age 67.

EDCS-16400158.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Sallustius Adiutor lived piously for 93 years; he rests here.
EDCS-16400158.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Sallustius Adiutor, recording his death at age 93.

EDCS-16400159.translation: To the departed gods. Sedia, daughter of Caius, devout, lived 54 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16400159.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Sedia, daughter of Caius, who lived 54 years.

EDCS-16400164.translation: To the departed gods. [Name]rnina, devout, lived 100 years.
EDCS-16400164.summary: A damaged tombstone mentioning a devout woman named [Name]rnina who lived to be 100 years old.

EDCS-16400182.translation: To the departed gods. Quintus Saturninus Cabdolis, devout, lived 71 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16400182.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Quintus Saturninus Cabdolis, who lived 71 years.

EDCS-16400211.translation: To the departed gods. Aulus Terentius Felix of the tribe Quirina, an excellent and devout man, lived 64 years, is buried here. May your bones rest well. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-16400211.summary: A tombstone for Aulus Terentius Felix, an excellent and devout man who lived 64 years, including a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-16400213.translation: To the departed gods. Termas[ius] Pr[...
EDCS-16400213.summary: A fragmentary tombstone dedicating it to the departed gods and beginning to record the name Termas[ius] Pr[.

EDCS-16400475.text_edition: \n Caius Hil Felix sacerdos Saturni cultor dei exegit V Kalendas Novembres Sabino et Anullino consulibus per Baricem magg(istrum)\n
EDCS-16400475.translation: Gaius Hil[i]us Felix, priest of Saturn, devotee of the god, completed this on the 5th day before the Kalends of November, when Sabinus and Anullinus were consuls, through Barices, the magistrate.
EDCS-16400475.summary: A dedication recording the completion of a work by Gaius Hil[i]us Felix, priest of Saturn, during the consulship of Sabinus and Anullinus.

EDCS-16400497.text_edition: \n Ex auctoritate Quinti Iuni Rustici praefecti urbis\n
EDCS-16400497.translation: By the authority of Quintus Junius Rusticus, prefect of the city.
EDCS-16400497.summary: A legal or administrative statement issued under the authority of Quintus Junius Rusticus, the prefect of the city.

EDCS-16400547.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Olus Cosinius Fortis miles cohortis VI praetoriae piae vindicis Severianae praepo[situs ------\n
EDCS-16400547.translation: To the departed gods, sacred. Olus Cosinius Fortis, soldier of the VI Praetorian cohort, pious, vindicating, Severian, appointed [---].
EDCS-16400547.summary: A dedication to the departed gods for Olus Cosinius Fortis, a soldier in the VI Praetorian cohort.

EDCS-16600017.translation: To the departed gods. Venusta, daughter of Datta Taege, devout, lived 67 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16600017.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Venusta, daughter of Datta Taege, who lived 67 years.

EDCS-16600021.translation: To the departed gods. Veratia Spicula, devout, lived 28 years, 4 months, and 24 days, is buried here.
EDCS-16600021.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Veratia Spicula, who lived 28 years, 4 months, and 24 days.

EDCS-16600027.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Vibius Festus, son of Lucius, of the Arnensian tribe, pious, lived for 32 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16600027.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Vibius Festus, who lived to be 32 years old.

EDCS-16600046.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aulus Vitellius Felix Victorianus, pious, lived for 96 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16600046.summary: This is a tombstone for Aulus Vitellius Felix Victorianus, who lived to be 96 years old.

EDCS-16600268.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Primitivos sic pius vixit annis V mensibus VI hic situs est alumno bene merito Anicetus ex dispensatore fecit regionis sibi et suis\n
EDCS-16600268.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Primitivos lived piously for 5 years and 6 months, is buried here; Anicetus, formerly a quartermaster, made this for his well-deserving foster-son and his own family.
EDCS-16600268.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Primitivos, who died at a young age, and was dedicated by his foster-father Anicetus, possibly around the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-16700101.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatorum dominorum nostrorum Luci Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti et Marci Aurelli sic Antonini Bassiani Caesaris Imperatoris destinati templum Victoriae Augustorum cultores eius numinis fecerunt Iulius Emeritus flamen perpetuus impensis suis fecit anno provinciae CLVIII\n
EDCS-16700101.translation: For the health of our Emperors and Lords, Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Bassianus Caesar, destined Emperor, the devotees of their divine power built this temple to Victoria Augusta; Julius Emeritus, perpetual priest, built it at his own expense in the 158th year of the province.
EDCS-16700101.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple to Victoria Augusta by the devotees of the imperial cult and the perpetual priest Julius Emeritus, likely in the early 3rd century CE, during the reign of Septimius Severus and Caracalla.

EDCS-16800127.translation: Marcia Zabullca, daughter of Sabidius, pious, lived for [unknown age], is buried here. Saturninus Sergianus, pious, lived for 58 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16800127.summary: This is a double tombstone commemorating Marcia Zabullca and Saturninus Sergianus, the latter of whom lived to be 58 years old.

EDCS-16800129.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tannonius Felix, pious, lived for 11 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16800129.summary: This is a tombstone for Tannonius Felix, who died at the age of 11.

EDCS-16800148.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Magnius Primus, pious, lived for 56 years.
EDCS-16800148.summary: This inscription records the death of Lucius Magnius Primus, who lived to be 56 years old.

EDCS-16800150.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Magnia Eutycetis, pious, lived for 41 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16800150.summary: This is a tombstone for Magnia Eutycetis, who lived to be 41 years old.

EDCS-16800155.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Papirius Sarmato lived for 70 years.
EDCS-16800155.summary: This inscription records the death of Papirius Sarmato, who lived to be 70 years old.

EDCS-16800167.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Saufeia Victoria, pious, lived for 75 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16800167.summary: This is a tombstone for Saufeia Victoria, who lived to be 75 years old.

EDCS-16800187.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Saturnina the pious lived for 56 years and 10 months.
EDCS-16800187.summary: A funerary epitaph for Saturnina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16800189.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Morasius, son of Aevidus, pious, lived for 51 years.
EDCS-16800189.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Morasius, son of Aevidus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17700347.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-17700347.text_edition: \n Marco Fannio Marci filio Papiria Vitali |(centurioni) cohortis IIII Sygambrorum cohortis I Hispanorum misso honesta missione a divo Hadriano praefecto iuris dicundo flamini perpetuo qui ob honorem flaminatus HS X milia nummum rei publicae intulit et amplius ludorum scaenicorum diem et epulum dedit cui cum ordo statuam decrevisset titulo contentus sua pecunia posuit dono dedit\n
EDCS-17700347.translation: To Marcus Fannius Vitalis, son of Marcus, of the Papirian tribe, centurion of the IV Cohort of Sygambrians and the I Cohort of Hispaniards, honorably discharged by the deified Hadrian, perpetual priest, who for the honor of the priesthood contributed 10,000 sesterces to the public treasury and also provided a day of theatrical games and a banquet, and for whom the council decreed a statue, but content with the inscription, he erected it at his own expense. He gave it as a gift.
EDCS-17700347.summary: A dedicatory inscription honoring Marcus Fannius Vitalis, a prominent local official and military officer, who funded public works and games, likely dating to the 2nd century AD.
