EDCS-25001606.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Salvius, an imperial slave and assistant clerk, who lived to be 40 years old.

EDCS-25001610.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Africanus, assistant to Caesar's tabularii of the military treasury, lived piously for 22 years. His father, Africanus, made this.
EDCS-25001610.summary: This is a tombstone for Africanus, an assistant to the imperial treasury's scribes, erected by his father.

EDCS-25001615.translation: Attalus, servant of Caesar, a scribe, lived piously for 19 years. His pious mother made this.
EDCS-25001615.summary: This tombstone is for Attalus, a scribe and servant of Caesar, dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-25001617.translation: Clarus, servant of our Caesar, a scribe, lived for 25 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25001617.summary: This is a tombstone for Clarus, a scribe and servant of Caesar, who lived for 25 years.

EDCS-25001621.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Alexander, servant of our Caesar, a scribe, lived piously for 40 years. He rests here. Flavia Atalante made this for her husband from her own means.
EDCS-25001621.summary: This tombstone records Alexander, a scribe and servant of Caesar, who lived for 40 years, and was dedicated by his wife Flavia Atalante.

EDCS-25001626.translation: Crescens, servant of Augustus, formerly a courier, lived piously for 45 years. His daughter Felicitas made this for her pious father. He rests here.
EDCS-25001626.summary: This tombstone is for Crescens, a courier and servant of Augustus who lived for 45 years, erected by his daughter Felicitas.

EDCS-25001627.translation: Dionysius, a scribe, lived piously for 50 years. His son Dionysius made this out of piety.
EDCS-25001627.summary: This tombstone honors Dionysius, a scribe, who lived for 50 years, and was dedicated by his son.

EDCS-25001632.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Eusebes, lived piously for 4 years, 11 months, and 17 days. Hermeros made this for his subordinate, Eusebes, a scribe of Augustus.
EDCS-25001632.summary: This tombstone records Eusebes, who lived for 4 years, 11 months, and 17 days, and was dedicated by his superior, Hermeros, an imperial scribe.

EDCS-25001634.translation: Flavia Fortunata, his most pious mother. Primigenius, a scribe of Augustus, made this at his own expense. She rests here.
EDCS-25001634.summary: This tombstone honors Flavia Fortunata, erected by her son Primigenius, an imperial scribe.

EDCS-25001635.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Saturninus, a scribe of Augustus, lived piously for 25 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25001635.summary: This tombstone is for Saturninus, a scribe and servant of Augustus, who lived for 25 years.

EDCS-25001637.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Aelius Victor mesor agrorum pius vixit annis XXXXVIIII Quartio frater pius fecit hic situs est\n
EDCS-25001637.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Victor, surveyor of lands, lived piously for 49 years. His brother Quartio, also pious, made this. He rests here.
EDCS-25001637.summary: This tombstone records Publius Aelius Victor, a land surveyor, who lived for 49 years, and was erected by his brother Quartio.

EDCS-25001638.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Didymus, a slave of the Emperor, a land surveyor, devout, lived 46 years; he lies here. Iulia Primiginia, his wife, made this from her own means.
EDCS-25001638.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Didymus, an imperial slave and land surveyor, erected by his wife.

EDCS-25001640.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Flavius Dapnus, a freedman of the Emperor, a land surveyor, devout, lived 90 years. Iulia Fortunata made this for her most devoted husband; he lies here.
EDCS-25001640.summary: A tombstone commemorating Titus Flavius Dapnus, an imperial freedman and land surveyor, erected by his wife.

EDCS-25001641.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Victor, in charge of the Emperor's pack animals, devout, lived 60 years; he lies here.
EDCS-25001641.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Victor, who was responsible for the Emperor's pack animals, noting his piety and age.

EDCS-25001642.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Anteros, a slave of the Emperor, a runner, devout, lived 40 years. Flavia Tyche made this for her companion from her own means; he lies here.
EDCS-25001642.summary: A tombstone for Anteros, an imperial slave and runner, erected by his female companion Flavia Tyche.

EDCS-25001643.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Campanus, a slave of our Emperor, a runner, devout, lived 55 years; he lies here. Flavia made this for her devout companion.
EDCS-25001643.summary: A tombstone for Campanus, an imperial slave and runner, erected by his companion Flavia.

EDCS-25001644.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Chryseros, a slave of the Emperor, a runner, lived 60 years; he lies here.
EDCS-25001644.summary: A tombstone for Chryseros, an imperial slave and runner, stating his age at death.

EDCS-25001649.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fructus, a slave of our Emperor, a runner, devout, lived 31 years; he lies here.
EDCS-25001649.summary: A tombstone for Fructus, an imperial slave and runner, noting his piety and age at death.

EDCS-25001654.translation: Faustus, son of Speratus, a slave of Caesar and keeper of the temple stores, lived 5 years; he lies here.
EDCS-25001654.summary: A tombstone for Faustus, the son of an imperial temple keeper, who died at a very young age.

EDCS-25001655.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Synhetus, a temple keeper for the Emperor, lived 95 years; he lies here.
EDCS-25001655.summary: A tombstone for Synhetus, an imperial temple keeper, recording his long lifespan.

EDCS-25001656.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Marcus Cocceius Verecundus and Verus, worthy of remembrance for Pythagoras, freedman and procurator of the Emperor, who served well; he lies here.
EDCS-25001656.summary: A tombstone honoring two individuals, Marcus Cocceius Verecundus and Verus, for Pythagoras, an imperial freedman and procurator, likely erected by colleagues or subordinates.

EDCS-25001663.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Adaucta lived 45 years.
EDCS-25001663.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Adaucta at the age of 45.

EDCS-25001667.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Benenatus lived 4 years.
EDCS-25001667.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Publius Aelius Benenatus at the age of 4.

EDCS-25001673.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Quinta lived 31 years and rests here.
EDCS-25001673.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Aemilia Quinta at the age of 31.

EDCS-25001674.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Agileius Ampliatus lived 9 years and rests here.
EDCS-25001674.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Lucius Agileius Ampliatus at the age of 9.

EDCS-25001683.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Anicetus, a devoted servant of the Emperor, lived 2 years and rests here. Felix and Laetina, his parents, made this.
EDCS-25001683.summary: A tombstone for Anicetus, a young imperial freedman, made by his parents.

EDCS-25001685.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Anteros lived 4 years and 7 months.
EDCS-25001685.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Anteros at the age of 4 years and 7 months.

EDCS-25001686.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aprilis, a devoted servant of the Emperor, lived 60 years and rests here. Iucundus made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-25001686.summary: A tombstone for Aprilis, an imperial freedwoman, made by her husband Iucundus.

EDCS-25001687.translation: Attica, a devoted servant of Caesar, lived 10 years and rests here. Victoria made this for her dearest sister.
EDCS-25001687.summary: A tombstone for Attica, a freedwoman of Caesar, made by her sister Victoria.

EDCS-25001688.translation: Aucta, a devoted servant of our Emperor, lived 28 years and rests here. Dionysius, a fellow servant, made this for her merits.
EDCS-25001688.summary: A tombstone for Aucta, an imperial freedwoman, made by a fellow freedman, Dionysius.

EDCS-25001695.translation: Bathyllus, servant of the Emperor, lived 7 years and rests here.
EDCS-25001695.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Bathyllus, an imperial freedman, at the age of 7.

EDCS-25001699.translation: To the divine spirits. Sacred. Caecilia Nemesis, dutiful, lived for 4 years, 8 months, and [number] days.
EDCS-25001699.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilia Nemesis, recording her age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001700.translation: Caletyche, a dutiful servant of Augustus, lived for 30 years; she is laid here. Faustus for his dutiful fellow servant.
EDCS-25001700.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caletyche, a freedwoman of Augustus, with a dedication from Faustus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001705.translation: To the divine spirits. Sacred. Clemens, a dutiful servant of our emperor, lived for 33 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-25001705.summary: A funerary epitaph for Clemens, a slave of the emperor, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001709.translation: To the divine spirits. Sacred. Quintus Cornelius Felix Macrianus, dutiful, lived for 27 years; he is laid here. Appia Quadratilla, his dutiful wife, made this.
EDCS-25001709.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Cornelius Felix Macrianus, with a dedication by his wife, Appia Quadratilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001713.translation: To the divine spirits. Sacred. Epaphroditus, a dutiful child-slave of Augustus, lived for one year and 17 days. O sweet little cheeks! He is laid here.
EDCS-25001713.summary: A funerary epitaph for Epaphroditus, a young slave of Augustus, noting his very early death and affectionately recalling his features, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001714.translation: To the divine spirits. Sacred. Eugenes, a dutiful servant of Augustus, lived for 9 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-25001714.summary: A funerary epitaph for Eugenes, a slave of Augustus, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001715.translation: To the divine spirits. Sacred. Eutyches, dutiful, lived for 80 years; he is laid here. // To the divine spirits. Sacred. Amandus, dutiful, a servant of Augustus, lived for 80 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-25001715.summary: A funerary epitaph with two identical entries for Eutyches and Amandus, both dutiful and 80 years old, likely slaves of Augustus, suggesting perhaps a shared tomb or adjacent burials.

EDCS-25001716.translation: To the divine spirits. Sacred. Extricata, a dutiful servant of Augustus, lived for 40 years; she is laid here. Primus for his deserving wife.
EDCS-25001716.summary: A funerary epitaph for Extricata, a slave of Augustus, with a dedication from her husband Primus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001717.translation: To the divine spirits. Sacred. Favor, a dutiful servant of Augustus, lived for 70 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-25001717.summary: A funerary epitaph for Favor, a slave of Augustus, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001719.translation: To the divine spirits. Sacred. Faustinus, a dutiful servant of Augustus, lived for 18 years; he is laid here.
EDCS-25001719.summary: A funerary epitaph for Faustinus, a slave of Augustus, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001720.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Faustus, servant of the Emperor, lived piously for 21 years, 10 months, and 25 days; he rests here. Fortunatus, his father, made this for his pious son.
EDCS-25001720.summary: A tombstone set up by a father for his deceased son, Faustus, a freedman of the emperor, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-25001721.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felicula, a wife well-deserving, pious, lived 37 years and 4 months. Aelius Saturus made this for her; she rests here.
EDCS-25001721.summary: A tombstone for Felicula, the deceased wife of Aelius Saturus, who died at the age of 37.

EDCS-25001722.translation: Felix, servant of our Emperor, lived piously for 2 years; he rests here. Dassius and Nymphe, his parents, made this.
EDCS-25001722.summary: A tombstone for Felix, the deceased young son of Dassius and Nymphe, who was a servant of the emperor.

EDCS-25001725.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felix, servant of our Emperor, lived piously for 5 years; he rests here. Amiantus, his father, made this.
EDCS-25001725.summary: A tombstone for Felix, the deceased young son of Amiantus, who was a servant of the emperor.

EDCS-25001726.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felix, servant of the Emperor, lived piously for 22 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25001726.summary: A tombstone for Felix, a servant of the Emperor, who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-25001728.translation: Felix of Hermes, deputy of the Emperor, lived for 30 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25001728.summary: A tombstone for Felix, deputy of the Emperor (vicarius Augusti), who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-25001730.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavius Doryphorus lived piously for 70 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25001730.summary: A tombstone for Flavius Doryphorus, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-25001731.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Flavius Quartio lived piously for 50 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25001731.summary: A tombstone for Titus Flavius Quartio, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-25001732.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Flavius Successus lived piously for 35 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25001732.summary: A tombstone for Titus Flavius Successus, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-25001733.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Doris lived piously for 24 years; she rests here. May your bones rest well; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-25001733.summary: A tombstone for Flavia Doris, who died at the age of 24, with a common epitaph wishing her a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-25001734.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Felicula, dutiful, lived 70 years; she rests here.
EDCS-25001734.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Felicula, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-25001737.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia Spes, freedwoman of Augustus, dutiful, lived 30 years; she rests here. May your bones rest well.
EDCS-25001737.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Spes, a freedwoman of Augustus, noting her lifespan and burial, with a wish for her repose.

EDCS-25001739.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunatus, servant of Augustus, lived 26 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25001739.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fortunatus, a servant of Augustus, detailing his age at death and burial.

EDCS-25001742.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunatus, servant of Augustus, dutiful, lived 1 year, 6 months, 10 days; he rests here.
EDCS-25001742.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fortunatus, a servant of Augustus, recording his very young age at death and burial.

EDCS-25001745.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fortunata, dutiful, lived 8 years, 1 month, 20 days. Fortunatus her father made this for his dutiful daughter; she rests here.
EDCS-25001745.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fortunata, set up by her father Fortunatus, recording her young age and burial.

EDCS-25001755.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Hecate, dutiful, lived 29 years, 7 months, 5 days; she rests here.
EDCS-25001755.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hecate, detailing her age at death and burial.

EDCS-25001756.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Heliodorus, servant of Augustus, dutiful, lived 40 years; he rests here. Victoria his wife made this.
EDCS-25001756.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Heliodorus, a servant of Augustus, erected by his wife Victoria, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-25001766.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ianuaria, dutiful, lived 18 years, 8 months; she rests here.
EDCS-25001766.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ianuaria, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-25001771.translation: Gaius Julius Herma, freedman of Gaius, dutiful, lived 60 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25001771.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Herma, a freedman, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-25001772.translation: Iulius Rogatus made this. Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Rogata lived 30 years, 4 months. Iulius V[---]nus, 5 years old, rests here.
EDCS-25001772.summary: This inscription records the funerary monument erected by Iulius Rogatus, dedicating it to the departed spirits and listing the ages and burial of Iulia Rogata and Iulius V[---]nus.

EDCS-25001773.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Julius Valerius lived for 1 year, 2 months, 15 days.
EDCS-25001773.summary: A funerary inscription recording the short lifespan of a child named Julius Valerius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001774.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Iulia Campana, dutiful, lived for 35 years, 6 months, 8 days. She rests here.
EDCS-25001774.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Campana, a woman who lived to 35 years, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001775.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Iulia Campanu[la], lived for 5 years, 5 months, 10 days. She rests here.
EDCS-25001775.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Campanula, a child who lived to 5 years, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001776.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Iulia Gamice, dutiful, lived for 2 years. Iulius Caelestinus, her dutiful brother, lived for 1 year, 1 month. He rests here.
EDCS-25001776.summary: A funerary inscription for two siblings, Iulia Gamice and Iulius Caelestinus, likely children, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001778.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Iulia Sissonia, dutiful, lived for 35 years. She rests here. Titus Flavius Romanus made [this] for his most dutiful, most holy, most indulgent wife, and also for Fundania Minor, his mother-in-law. [She] lived for 90 years. She rests here.
EDCS-25001778.summary: A tombstone set up by Titus Flavius Romanus for his wife Iulia Sissonia and his mother-in-law Fundania Minor, both deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001779.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Iulia Ultrix, dutiful, lived for 28 years. She rests here. Her foster children made [this] for her, on account of her merits.
EDCS-25001779.summary: A funerary inscription by the foster children of Iulia Ultrix, honoring her merits, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001780.translation: To the sacred spirits of the departed. Laches, slave of our Caesar, dutiful, lived for 90 years. He rests here. Martialis made [this] for his deserving [father-in-law]. May his bones rest well.
EDCS-25001780.summary: A funerary monument for Laches, a slave of Caesar, erected by his son-in-law Martialis, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001781.translation: Laeta, slave of Caesar, dutiful, lived for 40 years. Dionysius made [this] for his dutiful wife. She rests here. May your bones rest well. May the earth be light on you.
EDCS-25001781.summary: A tombstone for Laeta, a slave of Caesar, erected by her husband Dionysius, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001783.translation: To the sacred spirits of the departed. Lesbia, slave of Augustus, dutiful, lived for 26 years. She rests here.
EDCS-25001783.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lesbia, a slave of Augustus, who died at 26, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001784.translation: To the spirits of the departed, sacred. Liberal[is...
EDCS-25001784.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription beginning with the name Liberalis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001785.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Licinia Ianuaria, devoted, lived for 37 years and 8 months.
EDCS-25001785.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinia Ianuaria, recording her age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001786.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucanus, devoted slave of our Caesar, lived for 85 years. Iulia Fortunata, devoted, lived for 65 years. They are laid here.
EDCS-25001786.summary: A joint funerary inscription for Lucanus and Iulia Fortunata, both slaves of Caesar, detailing their ages and burial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001787.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucilla Felicitas, devoted, lived for 13 years and 6 months. She is laid here.
EDCS-25001787.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucilla Felicitas, recording her young age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001791.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Maxima, devoted to Augustus, lived for 2 years, 8 months, and 9 days. She is laid here. Primigenius made this for his most devoted and dearest daughter, belonging to Augustus.
EDCS-25001791.summary: A funerary inscription for Maxima, daughter of Primigenius and likely a member of the imperial household (indicated by 'Aug(usti)'), recording her very young age and the dedication by her father.

EDCS-25001793.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Minucia Prima, who lived for 26 years. Nicodromus made this for his devoted wife, belonging to Augustus, and well-deserving. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Minicia Prima, who lived for 26 years. Nicodromus made this for his devoted wife, belonging to Augustus, and well-deserving. You were snatched away in your youth, my dearest wife. Life proved itself for you for twice ten years and six. Rome is your origin; it was your fate to be a Libyan. Now, pitiable one, you are led to the Stygian boat, and sorrowful Lethe dwells in your heart, so that you do not recognize me, pitiable one, the devoted one. It was Fortune's gift to keep you chaste, and you could have given both of us to Italy. You will renew many things with tears, O good and simple one, when I do not have you in sight as a companion. She is laid here. Prima.
EDCS-25001793.summary: A complex funerary epitaph for Minucia Prima, who died at 26, including a poem by her husband Nicodromus, who served Augustus, lamenting her death and their shared life.

EDCS-25001794.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Natalis, devoted slave of Augustus, lived for 20 years. Heliodorus, devoted slave of Augustus, lived for 25 years. Their mother made this. They are laid here.
EDCS-25001794.summary: A joint funerary inscription for Natalis and Heliodorus, slaves of Augustus, recording their ages and the dedication by their mother.

EDCS-25001804.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Primigenia, devoted, lived for 30 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-25001804.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Primigenia, recording her age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001806.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Protus, slave of our Caesar, lived for 3 years. Hilara, slave of our Caesar, lived for 2 years. Ianuarius, slave of our Caesar, made this for his children. They are laid here.
EDCS-25001806.summary: A funerary inscription for young children Protus and Hilara, slaves of Caesar, with a dedication from their father Ianuarius.

EDCS-25001808.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quieta, devoted slave of Augustus, lived for 19 years. She is laid here.
EDCS-25001808.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quieta, a slave of Augustus, recording her age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001810.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintio, devoted, lived for 18 years. He is laid here.
EDCS-25001810.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintio, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001812.translation: Repentinus lived 14 pious years; Victor lived 2 pious years; they lie here.
EDCS-25001812.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of two individuals, Repentinus and Victor, who are buried here.

EDCS-25001818.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Salvius lived 2 pious years; he lies here.
EDCS-25001818.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Salvius, who lived for 2 years.

EDCS-25001821.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Saturnina lived 18 pious years; she lies here. Primus her father made this.
EDCS-25001821.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Saturnina, who lived 18 years, and was made by her father, Primus.

EDCS-25001826.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Secunda, a servant of Augustus, lived 28 years; she lies here. Urbanus made this for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-25001826.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Secunda, a servant of Augustus, who lived 28 years, and was dedicated by her husband, Urbanus.

EDCS-25001828.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sosime lived 22 pious years. Albinus made this for his pious wife; she lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25001828.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Sosime, who lived 22 years, and was dedicated by her husband, Albinus.

EDCS-25001830.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Spes lived 28 pious years; she lies here.
EDCS-25001830.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Spes, who lived for 28 years.

EDCS-25001833.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Tertius, a servant of Augustus, lived 22 pious years; he lies here.
EDCS-25001833.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Tertius, a servant of Augustus, who lived for 22 years.

EDCS-25001834.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Theodorus, a well-deserving fellow servant of the household of Lucius Scribonius Ravi Cassianus.
EDCS-25001834.summary: This funerary inscription honors Theodorus, a fellow servant in the household of L. Scribonius Ravi Cassianus, who is buried here.

EDCS-25001835.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Thymelicus, a servant of Augustus, lived 7 pious years; he lies here. Felix his father made this for his most pious son.
EDCS-25001835.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Thymelicus, a servant of Augustus, who lived 7 years, and was made by his father, Felix.

EDCS-25001837.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Tullia Tertulla lived 32 pious years; she lies here. Phaenippus, a servant of Augustus, for his deserving wife.
EDCS-25001837.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Tullia Tertulla, who lived 32 years, and was dedicated by her husband, Phaenippus, a servant of Augustus.

EDCS-25001843.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Victor, our Emperor's slave, lived 18 years. His father Quintio, devoted, made this.
EDCS-25001843.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Victor, a slave of the emperor, erected by his devoted father Quintio.

EDCS-25001844.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Victor lived 5 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25001844.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Victor, who lived for 5 years and rests here.

EDCS-25001845.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Victor, our Emperor's devoted slave, lived 18 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25001845.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Victor, a devoted slave of the emperor, who lived for 18 years and rests here.

EDCS-25001846.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Victor, our Emperor's devoted slave, lived 27 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25001846.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Victor, a devoted slave of the emperor, who lived for 27 years and rests here.

EDCS-25001847.translation: Devoted Victoria lived 4 years. She rests here. Her devoted father Quartio made this.
EDCS-25001847.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Victoria, who lived for 4 years, erected by her devoted father Quartio.

EDCS-25001856.translation: Vitalis, devoted servant of the Emperor, lived 17 years. She rests here.
EDCS-25001856.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Vitalis, a devoted servant of the emperor, who lived for 17 years and rests here.

EDCS-25001858.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Ulpius Clarus, our Emperor's devoted freedman, lived 60 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25001858.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Marcus Ulpius Clarus, a devoted freedman of the emperor, who lived for 60 years and rests here.

EDCS-25001860.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Ulpia Musa, devoted, lived 45 years. She rests here.
EDCS-25001860.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Ulpia Musa, who lived for 45 years and rests here.

EDCS-25001861.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Ulpia Pacata, devoted, lived 31 years. She rests here. Acceptus made this for his beloved wife.
EDCS-25001861.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Ulpia Pacata, who lived for 31 years, erected by her husband Acceptus.

EDCS-25001862.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Ulpia Vitalis, devoted, lived 4 years, 3 months, and 9 days. She rests here.
EDCS-25001862.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Ulpia Vitalis, who lived for 4 years, 3 months, and 9 days, and rests here.

EDCS-25001863.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Volussia Vitalica, dutiful, lived 18 years, 4 months, 5 days. She rests here.
EDCS-25001863.summary: A tombstone recording the life span and burial of Volussia Vitalica.

EDCS-25001864.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Urbica, dutiful wife of Augustus, lived 30 years. She rests here. Vitalis made this for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-25001864.summary: A tombstone for Urbica, set up by her husband Vitalis, who also names Emperor Augustus as her owner, indicating her status.

EDCS-25001865.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Uttedius Felix, dutiful, lived 42 years, 3 months. Uttedia Alexandria made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-25001865.summary: A tombstone for Uttedius Felix, erected by his wife Uttedia Alexandria.

EDCS-25001878.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Julius Felix, a dutiful soldier of the First Legion Adjututrix, lived 55 years, 10 months. He rests here.
EDCS-25001878.summary: A tombstone for Julius Felix, a soldier of the I Adiutrix legion.

EDCS-25001880.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Hypnus, dutiful, lived 23 years. He rests here. Asiaticus, freedman of Augustus and procurator of the Assuritana region, made this for his dutiful and well-deserving foster son.
EDCS-25001880.summary: A tombstone for Hypnus, a freedman and foster son of Asiaticus, an imperial procurator.

EDCS-25001894.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Alexa, dutiful servant of Augustus, an assistant commentator, lived 21 years. She rests here.
EDCS-25001894.summary: A tombstone for Alexa, a servant of Augustus who worked as an assistant commentator.

EDCS-25001902.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Thoas, a most dutiful servant of Augustus and notary, lived 46 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25001902.summary: A tombstone for Thoas, a dutiful servant of Augustus and a notary.

EDCS-25001904.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Sucessa, a dutiful slave of Caesar, lived 36 years. Fortunatus the herald made this for his good wife. She rests here.
EDCS-25001904.summary: A tombstone for Sucessa, a slave of Caesar and wife of Fortunatus the herald.

EDCS-25001905.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Campester, dutiful servant of Augustus and trainer of couriers, lived 30 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25001905.summary: A tombstone for Campester, a servant of Augustus who trained couriers.

EDCS-25001922.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, sacred. Gessia Luscina, dutiful, lived 30 years. She rests here. Tiberius Claudius Matrillus, the physician, made this for his dutiful wife.
EDCS-25001922.summary: A tombstone for Gessia Luscina, erected by her husband Tiberius Claudius Matrillus, a physician.

EDCS-25001930.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelius Restitutus, dutiful, lived for 3 years and 6 months. He rests here.
EDCS-25001930.summary: A tombstone recording the short life of Aelius Restitutus, likely a child, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001933.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Aelius Viator, dutiful, lived for 36 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25001933.summary: A tombstone for Aelius Viator, who died at the age of 36, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001942.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Aelia Felicula, dutiful, lived for 80 years. Felix and Caletyche, her sons, made this for their dutiful mother. She rests here.
EDCS-25001942.summary: A tombstone set up by sons for their mother, Aelia Felicula, who lived to 80, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001950.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Annius Dicaeus lived for 50 years. Publius Annius Natalis, his freedman, made this for his well-deserving patron.
EDCS-25001950.summary: A tombstone erected by a freedman, Publius Annius Natalis, for his patron Publius Annius Dicaeus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001951.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. The dutiful servant of the Emperor lived for 17 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25001951.summary: A tombstone for a male imperial slave who died at age 17, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001952.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Antonia Haline, dutiful, lived for 72 years and 10 months. Publius Aelius Euhodus, freedman of the Emperor, for his well-deserving wife, with whom he lived for 40 years. She rests here.
EDCS-25001952.summary: A tombstone set up by Publius Aelius Euhodus, an imperial freedman, for his wife Antonia Haline, whom he had been married to for 40 years, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001954.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Arbuscula, dutiful, of our Emperor, lived for 53 years. She rests here.
EDCS-25001954.summary: A tombstone for Arbuscula, a woman associated with the imperial household, who died at age 53, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001959.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Atimetianus, dutiful, lived for 3 years and 6 months. He rests here.
EDCS-25001959.summary: A tombstone recording the short life of Atimetianus, likely a child, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001972.translation: Caius Caecilius Restutus, dutiful, lived for 19 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25001972.summary: A tombstone for Caius Caecilius Restutus, who died at age 19, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001974.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Cale, dutiful, servant of our Emperor, lived for 40 years. She rests here.
EDCS-25001974.summary: A tombstone for Cale, a female imperial slave, who died at age 40, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25001977.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Cassius Africanus, son of Lucius, devoted, lived 30 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25001977.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Lucius Cassius Africanus, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-25001982.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clarus, devoted servant of the Emperor, lived 40 years. Ianuarius, his son, made this for his most devoted father. He is buried here.
EDCS-25001982.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Clarus, a devoted imperial freedman, erected by his son Ianuarius.

EDCS-25001985.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudia Nape, devoted, lived 86 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25001985.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Claudia Nape, who lived 86 years.

EDCS-25001995.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Comisator, devoted servant of our Emperor, lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25001995.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Comisator, a devoted imperial freedman, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-25002002.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Cusp[---], devoted, lived 1 year and 10 months. He is buried here.
EDCS-25002002.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Lucius Cusp[---], who lived 1 year and 10 months.

EDCS-25002004.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Danae, lived 1 year, 6 months, and 20 days. She is buried here.
EDCS-25002004.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Danae, who lived 1 year, 6 months, and 20 days.

EDCS-25002006.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Dellia Flaccilla, devoted, lived 20 years and 8 months. She is buried here.
EDCS-25002006.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Dellia Flaccilla, who lived 20 years and 8 months.

EDCS-25002016.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Eutycha, devoted, lived 15 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-25002016.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Eutycha, who lived 15 years.

EDCS-25002017.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Extricatus, devoted servant of the Emperor, lived 81 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25002017.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Extricatus, a devoted imperial freedman, who lived 81 years.

EDCS-25002019.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fabia Iucunda, devoted, lived 34 years, 10 months, and 1 day. Fabius Felix, devoted, lived 33 years. They are buried here.
EDCS-25002019.summary: A funerary inscription recording the deaths of Fabia Iucunda and her brother Fabius Felix, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25002024.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Felicia the dutiful lived 50 years, and Badaeus the dutiful lived 60 years; here he lies buried.
EDCS-25002024.summary: This is a tombstone recording the deaths of Felicia and Badaeus, who lived 50 and 60 years respectively, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25002032.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Felix, dutiful servant of the Emperor, lived 40 years and 3 months; here he lies buried.
EDCS-25002032.summary: This tombstone records the death of Felix, an imperial servant, who lived 40 years and 3 months, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25002033.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Felix, dutiful servant of our Emperor, lived 2 years and 20 days; here he lies buried. Felix made this for his deserving son.
EDCS-25002033.summary: This tombstone records the death of a young boy named Felix, who lived 2 years and 20 days, and was commemorated by his father, also named Felix, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25002034.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Flavius Atimetus the dutiful lived 78 years; here he lies buried.
EDCS-25002034.summary: This tombstone records the death of Titus Flavius Atimetus, who lived 78 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25002043.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Flavia Fusca the dutiful lived 40 years; here she lies buried. Flavius Onesimus made this for his most dutiful wife and for himself.
EDCS-25002043.summary: This tombstone records the death of Flavia Fusca, who lived 40 years, and was made by her husband Flavius Onesimus for both of them, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25002046.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Fortunatus Corinthianus, dutiful servant of the Emperor, lived 13 years; here he lies buried. May your bones rest well; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25002046.summary: This tombstone records the death of Fortunatus Corinthianus, an imperial servant, who lived 13 years, and includes a common epitaph wishing the deceased peace, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25002050.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Fortunata, servant of the Emperor...
EDCS-25002050.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone recording the death of Fortunata, an imperial servant, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25002051.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Furfuleia Tertulla the dutiful lived 27 years; here she lies buried.
EDCS-25002051.summary: This tombstone records the death of Furfuleia Tertulla, who lived 27 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25002052.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Furnia... lived...
EDCS-25002052.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone recording the death of someone named Furnia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25002053.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Galla, servant of the Emperor, the dutiful, lived 7 years; here she lies buried.
EDCS-25002053.summary: This tombstone records the death of Galla, an imperial servant, who lived 7 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25002054.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Getulicus, a devoted household slave of the Emperor, lived 4 years, 8 months, and 4 days; he is buried here.
EDCS-25002054.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Getulicus, a slave of the Emperor, who died in childhood.

EDCS-25002056.translation: To the Divine Shades. Helpis, devoted, lived five years and 3 months; she is buried here.
EDCS-25002056.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Helpis, who died in childhood.

EDCS-25002066.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ismarus, devoted, lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25002066.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ismarus, who died at age 25.

EDCS-25002067.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iucundus, devoted, lived 8 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25002067.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iucundus, a child, likely associated with the imperial family.

EDCS-25002069.translation: Tiberius Iulius Rufinus, devoted, lived 27 years and 3 months; he is buried here.
EDCS-25002069.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Iulius Rufinus, who died at age 27 years and 3 months.

EDCS-25002076.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Quesita, devoted, lived 55 years, 11 months, and 16 days.
EDCS-25002076.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Quesita, who died at age 55.

EDCS-25002077.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Tertulla, devoted, lived 36 years and 2 months; she is buried here.
EDCS-25002077.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Tertulla, who died at age 36.

EDCS-25002079.translation: Lucius Iunius Vitalis lived 90 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25002079.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Iunius Vitalis, who died at the age of 90.

EDCS-25002084.translation: Martialis, devoted, lived 5 years, 4 months, and 12 days; he is buried here.
EDCS-25002084.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Martialis, who died in childhood.

EDCS-25002086.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Matila, devoted, lived 24 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25002086.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Matila, who died at age 24.

EDCS-25002093.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Octavia Lochias lived 65 years; she is buried here. Philomusus, a servant of the Emperor Augustus and a member of his household, made this for his devoted mother-in-law.
EDCS-25002093.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Octavia Lochias, erected by her devoted mother-in-law, Philomusus, a freedman of the Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-25002096.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Philumenus the devoted lived 11 months and 11 days; he is buried here.
EDCS-25002096.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Philumenus, who died at the age of 11 months and 11 days.

EDCS-25002098.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Poletice the devoted lived 60 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25002098.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Poletice, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-25002104.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Primus, a devoted servant of our Emperor, lived 80 years; he is buried here. Primigenia, his devoted partner, made this for him as he deserved.
EDCS-25002104.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Primus, a devoted servant of the Emperor, erected by his devoted partner, Primigenia.

EDCS-25002107.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Primula, a devoted servant of the Emperor Augustus, lived 9 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25002107.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Primula, a devoted servant of the Emperor Augustus, who died at the age of 9.

EDCS-25002111.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Privata, a devoted servant of the Emperor Augustus, lived 17 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25002111.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Privata, a devoted servant of the Emperor Augustus, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-25002114.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Pullaienus Proculus the devoted lived 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25002114.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Lucius Pullaienus Proculus, who died at the age of 65.

EDCS-25002115.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Rabiria Spes the devoted lived 37 years; she is buried here. Nicolaus made this for his dearest wife, a servant of the Emperor.
EDCS-25002115.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Rabiria Spes, erected by her husband Nicolaus, a servant of the Emperor.

EDCS-25002129.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Secunda, a devoted servant of the Emperor Augustus, lived 2 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-25002129.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Secunda, a devoted servant of the Emperor Augustus, who died at the age of 2.

EDCS-25002130.translation: [Sacred to the Divine Spirits.] [Name missing], a devoted servant of [our Emperor] Augustus, lived 25 years. Secur[us] made [this] for his wife.
EDCS-25002130.summary: This incomplete epitaph records the burial of a woman, likely the wife of Securus, who was a devoted servant of the Emperor Augustus and died at age 25.

EDCS-25002132.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Gaius Selicius Victor lived piously.
EDCS-25002132.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Selicius Victor, a pius individual, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25002133.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. Senila lived piously for one year and eight months. She is buried here.
EDCS-25002133.summary: A funerary inscription for Senila, who lived piously for one year and eight months, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25002134.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred. You whoever reads this inscription, whether pious or unkind, weep for my age, which I ought not to have left such light nor such underworld gods. For what reason do you speak? I was obedient to my dear husband, whose character and chastity I always served out of timidity, and whose affection I preserved, because he truly deserved me, who loved me so chastely. I lived simply and chastely, in whose modesty no one could boast or be angry with my dear husband. Whatever you, a chaste woman, can do, live so chastely; for I know you can be dearly loved if you live chastely according to my custom. For after my death, I leave the memory of many years to my husband. But I give thanks to the gods above that while I lived, they denied me nothing of my pleasures, because I myself deserved them. Severa, a pious servant of Augustus, lived 24 years, 6 months, and 11 days. She is buried here. Her husband made this for her, who deserved it.
EDCS-25002134.summary: A lengthy epitaph for Severa, a pious servant of Augustus, detailing her virtuous life and devotion to her husband, and serving as a moral guide for other women; she lived 24 years, 6 months, and 11 days. The inscription was made by her deserving husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100291.text_edition: \n Tesseram pagi Minervi Marcus Grattius Marci filius Papiria magister pagi de sua pecunia dedit\n
EDCS-25100291.translation: The marker of the district of Minerva. Marcus Grattius, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, as district magistrate, dedicated this at his own expense.
EDCS-25100291.summary: A dedication of a boundary marker for the district of Minerva by Marcus Grattius, a district magistrate, funded from his own money, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100292.translation: The roofs of the Bassiana estate shine, known by the name Baiae, and gleam even more with the brightness of the light. Sidonius (?) arranged to make... for the honored dedication... opposite...
EDCS-25100292.summary: An incomplete inscription that seems to describe the shining roofs of the Bassiana estate, named Baiae, and mentions a dedication arranged by someone named Sidonius (?), likely related to a building or property improvement in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25100336.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-25100336.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini divi Antonini filii et Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Aureli Augustorum liberorumque Antonini Augusti Agrius Samsera ob honorem decurionatus sui inlatis legitimis ex HS CCducentis milibus nummum ampliata pecunia statuas memoriae temporum quattuor decreto decurionum posuit item dedicationis die epulum decurionibus dedit\n
EDCS-25100336.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, son of the divine Antoninus, and of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius, and of Antoninus Augustus and his children. Agrius Samsera, in honor of his magistracy, with the legal contribution of two hundred thousand sesterces, and with additional funds, erected statues as a memorial of the times, by decree of the decurions. On the day of the dedication, he also provided a feast for the decurions.
EDCS-25100336.summary: Agrius Samsera dedicated statues and provided a feast for the decurions for the safety of the Emperors Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Lucius Aurelius, using his own funds supplemented by legal contributions, in honor of his magistracy, dating to the reign of Marcus Aurelius and Commodus.

EDCS-25200086.translation: Lucius Mutius Faustinus, son of Lucius, of the Arnensian tribe, lived piously for 74 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25200086.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Mutius Faustinus, who lived piously for 74 years, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25200094.text_edition: \n Hadriani Augusti domini locus Camerino et Nigro consulibus sub cura Agathae liberti\n
EDCS-25200094.translation: The property of Hadrian Augustus, lord. In the consulship of Camerinus and Niger, under the supervision of Agathus, a freedman.
EDCS-25200094.summary: A boundary or ownership inscription indicating land belonging to Hadrian Augustus, supervised by his freedman Agathus, dating to the consulship of Camerinus and Niger (likely in the early 2nd century AD).

EDCS-25200096.translation: Of Emperor Antoninus Augustus... No. 751, workshop... Under Emperor Antoninus and... under the supervision of Agatha.
EDCS-25200096.summary: An incomplete inscription mentioning Emperor Antoninus Augustus, a workshop number, and supervision by Agatha, likely related to imperial administration or property during Antoninus Pius's reign.

EDCS-25200097.translation: In the consulship of Orfitus and Priscinus, from the account of Callistus. No. 61. Certus...
EDCS-25200097.summary: An incomplete inscription mentioning the consuls Orfitus and Priscinus, an account of Callistus, and a number, likely an administrative record or ledger entry from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25200114.translation: Sextus Veturius, a veteran of the Silian cavalry wing, lived 70 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25200114.summary: This inscription is a tombstone recording the life and burial of Sextus Veturius, a veteran of the Silian cavalry wing.

EDCS-25200115.translation: [Aedile, quaestor, priest of Jupiter] Cornelius Vitalis lived 31 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25200115.summary: This epitaph records the life of Cornelius Vitalis, who held several magistracies and priesthoods, and includes a common epitaph formula.

EDCS-25200116.translation: Veturia Martha, daughter of Sextus, a priestess of Caelestis, lived 95 years here.
EDCS-25200116.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Veturia Martha, identified as a priestess of the goddess Caelestis.

EDCS-25200121.translation: Marcus Aemilius Primus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, lived [ ] years; he rests here.
EDCS-25200121.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Marcus Aemilius Primus, identified by his tribe and father's name.

EDCS-25200122.translation: Gaius Aurelius Memor, son of Gaius, son of Aurelius Medicus, two brothers, lived 7 years; they are buried here.
EDCS-25200122.summary: This epitaph commemorates two brothers, Gaius Aurelius Memor and his brother whose name is lost, who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-25200125.translation: Quintus Caecilius, son of Quintus, Po[
EDCS-25200125.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely an epitaph, recording the name Quintus Caecilius and his father.

EDCS-25200126.summary: This fragmentary inscription is likely an epitaph for Caninia Sura, noting her dutifulness and her lifespan.

EDCS-25200128.translation: Gaius Cornelius Felix, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, dutiful, lived 50 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25200128.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Gaius Cornelius Felix, noting his dutifulness and lifespan.

EDCS-25200131.translation: Egnatia Helena, daughter of Marcus, dutiful, lived 45 years; she rests here.
EDCS-25200131.summary: This inscription is an epitaph for Egnatia Helena, noting her dutifulness and lifespan.

EDCS-25200134.translation: Gorgonia Fortunata, dutiful, lived 80 years // Gaius Aelius Saturninus, dutiful, lived [ ] years
EDCS-25200134.summary: This inscription appears to be a double tombstone, recording the lives of Gorgonia Fortunata and Gaius Aelius Saturninus.

EDCS-25200136.translation: Gaius Julius Felix, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, dutifully lived for nine years; he is laid here.
EDCS-25200136.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of a young boy named Gaius Julius Felix, who died at the age of nine.

EDCS-25200140.translation: Iulia Saturnina, wife of Daphnis, freedman of Tiberius Claudius Hilarion, lived for sixty-something years; she is laid here; may your bones rest well; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-25200140.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Saturnina, wife of a freedman, recording her age at death and offering wishes for her repose, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25200144.translation: Gaius Petronius Acceptus, dutifully, lived for forty-three years.
EDCS-25200144.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Gaius Petronius Acceptus, who died at the age of forty-three.

EDCS-25200145.translation: Quarta, daughter of Vetus Ianuarius, lived for twenty-two years.
EDCS-25200145.summary: A tombstone recording the life of Quarta, daughter of Vetus Ianuarius, who died at the age of twenty-two.

EDCS-25200147.translation: Rustica, daughter of Polio, from Iscilitana, lived for sixty years; she is laid here.
EDCS-25200147.summary: A tombstone for Rustica, daughter of Polio, recording her age and burial, likely from a Roman provincial context.

EDCS-25200151.summary: A fragmentary inscription, likely a tombstone, beginning with the name Servi[--].

EDCS-25200152.translation: For Servilia Venusta, daughter of Servilius Saturninus, lived for fifty years; she is laid here.
EDCS-25200152.summary: A tombstone for Servilia Venusta, daughter of Servilius Saturninus, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-25200155.translation: Sextus Veturius Extricatus lived for fifty-three years; he is laid here.
EDCS-25200155.summary: A funerary inscription for Sextus Veturius Extricatus, stating his age at death and burial.

EDCS-25200157.translation: Lucius Vitupius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, dutifully lived for sixty-seven years; he is laid here.
EDCS-25200157.summary: A tombstone recording the life and burial of Lucius Vitupius Severus, who died at the age of sixty-seven.

EDCS-25200165.translation: Mundicia Processa, daughter of Quintus, dutiful, lived for fifty-two years; may your bones rest well.
EDCS-25200165.summary: A tombstone for Mundicia Processa, daughter of Quintus, recording her age and offering a wish for her repose.

EDCS-25200167.translation: Cornelia Tyra, dutiful, lived for 14 years.
EDCS-25200167.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Cornelia Tyra, who died at the age of fourteen.

EDCS-25200169.translation: Gaius Octavius Festus, dutiful, lived for 18 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-25200169.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gaius Octavius Festus, who died at the age of eighteen.

EDCS-25200729.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Octavius Sabb[---] TIMI[---]
EDCS-25200729.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Octavius Sabb[---] dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25300087.inscription_type: carmina, tituli fabricationis, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-25300087.translation: [---] many citizens have always enjoyed [---] in civic life, and the majesty of the unconquered divine Mars, crafted by hand, stands firm; the divine power of Ingenui saw something similar to himself, which was reformed into something better, a certain image with a more venerable countenance, and all the signs demonstrate that such a work, perfected, is religion itself, which makes his own camp shine perpetually, with Cilonius as the maker, whose love still grows in number among the great, because he prepares this for him to have a happy life and at the same time shows it with better fame, and he also made a vow willingly with his spirit. For he gave a gymnasium and wine to Martensibus, and thus not undeservedly, because he paid the offering to be presented to the deity as it certainly commanded, and he produced his own image with his money. // To Mars the Unconquered by Cilonius // To Mars the Unconquered by Cilonius
EDCS-25300087.summary: This dedication, made by Cilonius to Mars the Unconquered, records the construction of a gymnasium and the donation of wine, intended to enhance the prosperity and reputation of the soldiers' camp.

EDCS-25300092.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Parthicus Arabicus, Parthicus Adiabenicus, with tribunician power for the fourth time, emperor for the eighth time, consul for the second time, proconsul. Alfius Secundus, perpetual priest, erected two equestrian statues, worth 12,000 sesterces, which Lucius Alfius Felix, his father, had promised for double the sum as an honorary gift for the perpetual priesthood, by permission of the council, from his own liberality.
EDCS-25300092.summary: This inscription records the erection of two equestrian statues by Alfius Secundus, honoring his father Lucius Alfius Felix, and commemorating the emperor Septimius Severus, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25300094.translation: For Tiberius Apraius Felix, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, Paratus, aedile, perpetual priest, a good man, lover and protégé of his municipality, because of his incomparable gift of projectiles, in honor of his aedileship, and also for the great construction of a bathing establishment from his sheer liberality, the people, with contributions of money, erected this; the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-25300094.summary: This monument, erected by public subscription and approved by the decurions, honors Tiberius Apraius Felix for his generosity as an aedile and for funding the construction of a bathing complex.

EDCS-25300350.translation: [---]estius, husband, and his wife, fulfilled their vow willingly.
EDCS-25300350.summary: A fragmentary inscription recording a vow fulfilled by a husband and wife, likely a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-25300419.text_edition: \n Quinto Caecilio Quinti filio Arnensi Marcello Dentiliano Xviro stlitibus iudicandis tribuno militum legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis quaestori provinciae Africae aedili curuli candidato divi Hadriani praetori candidato eiusdem legato provinciae Cretae Cyrenarum legato provinciae Hispaniae proconsuli provinciae Cretae Cyrenarum legato legionis XII fulminatae legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Galliae Aquitanicae coloniae patrono Thibiucenses aere conlato\n
EDCS-25300419.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Quintus son Arnensis Marcellus Dentilianus, member of the XV viri for judging lawsuits, military tribune of the XI Claudian Pia Fidelis legion, quaestor of the province of Africa, curule aedile, candidate for the same, praetor, candidate for the same, legate of the province of Crete and Cyrene, legate of the province of Hispania, proconsul of the province of Crete and Cyrene, legate of the XII Fulminata legion, legate of Augustus as proconsul of the province of Gallia Aquitania, patron of the colony. The people of Thibiuca, with contributions of money, erected this.
EDCS-25300419.summary: This extensive honorary inscription celebrates the extensive career of Quintus Caecilius Quintus son Arnensis Marcellus Dentilianus, detailing his military, judicial, and administrative posts, and his patronage of the colony of Thibiuca, likely dating to the Hadrianic or Antonine period.

EDCS-25401735.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Parthici maximi Germanici maximi Britannici maximi pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate imperatoris III consulis IIII patris patriae proconsulis et Iuliae Domnae Augustae Piae Felicis matris Augusti et castrorum et senatus et patriae totiusque domus divinae Sentius Felix Repostus ob honorem fili sui Luci Senti Felicis Repostiani flamonii perpetui sive XI primatus et magisterii non administrati sua pecunia fecit\n
EDCS-25401735.translation: For the health of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the third time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, proconsul, and of Iulia Domna Augusta Pia Felix, mother of the emperor, of the camps, of the senate, and of the whole country, and of the entire divine household. Sentius Felix Repostus made this at his own expense for the honor of his son Lucius Sentius Felix Repostianus, perpetual priest, and holder of the XI primatus and magistracy, which he did not administer.
EDCS-25401735.summary: Sentius Felix Repostus dedicated this to ensure the health of Emperor Caracalla and Empress Julia Domna, commemorating his son Lucius Sentius Felix Repostianus's honorary priesthood, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25401760.translation: For Aurelius Antoninus, son of Augustus, of both orders.
EDCS-25401760.summary: A brief dedication to Aurelius Antoninus, son of Augustus, likely referring to the Emperor Caracalla and indicating his connection to both senatorial and equestrian classes.

EDCS-25500463.translation: To Sextus Cornelius Felix Pacatus, son of Caius, of the Quirina tribe, former commissioner of roads, military tribune with the broad stripe of the Third Legion Cyrenaica, and patron of the colony, at public expense by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-25500463.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by public funds to Sextus Cornelius Felix Pacatus, a local official and military tribune, who was also a patron of the colony.

EDCS-25501714.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Augusti Luci Severi et Marci An<t=I>onini [et] G[etae] Caesaris Augusti et Iuliae Domnae Augustae Lurius Seius veteranus legionis II adiutricis piae <f=I>idelis votum solvit no et BS consulibus Tauro et verre \n
EDCS-25501714.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety of the Emperor Caesar Augustus Lucius Severus and Marcus Antoninus Caesar Augustus and Geta Caesar Augustus and Julia Domna Augusta, Lurius Seius, a veteran of the Second Legion Adjutant, pious and faithful, fulfilled his vow, with Taurus and Verre as consuls, and [consuls] Tauro and Verre.
EDCS-25501714.summary: This is a votive inscription by a veteran to Jupiter for the welfare of the imperial family, dating to the consulship of Taurus and Verre, likely in the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-25501733.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Antonius Rufus Honorati filius Tromentina Veiis Arnensis Thignicae genius veritatis pius vixit annis hic situs est ossa tibi bene quiescant terra tibi levis sit \xce\x9f\xe1\xbc\xb5\xce\xb7 \xcf\x80\xce\xb5\xcf\x81 \xcf\x86\xe1\xbd\xbb\xce\xbb\xce\xbb\xcf\x89\xce\xbd \xce\xb3\xce\xb5\xce\xbd\xce\xb5\xe1\xbd\xb5, \xcf\x84\xce\xbf\xe1\xbd\xb7\xce\xb7 \xce\xb4\xe1\xbd\xb2 \xce\xba\xce\xb1\xe1\xbd\xb6 \xe1\xbc\x80\xce\xbd\xce\xb4\xcf\x81\xe1\xbf\xb6\xce\xbd\n
EDCS-25501733.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Antonius Rufus, son of Honoratus, of the Tromentina tribe, of Veiis Arnensis Thignicae, the spirit of truth, pious, lived X years. He lies here. May your bones rest well, may the earth be light upon you. May the divine power protect you, and the gods favor you.
EDCS-25501733.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Antonius Rufus, recording his lifespan and expressing wishes for his peaceful repose, also containing Greek blessings for the deceased.

EDCS-25501785.text_edition: \n Ex auctoritate Imperatoris Vespasiani Caesaris Augusti patris patriae fines provinciae sic novae et veteres derecti qua Fossa regia fuit per Rutilium Gallicum consulem pontificem et Sentium Caecilianum praetorem legatos Augusti pro praetore\n
EDCS-25501785.translation: By the authority of Emperor Vespasian Caesar Augustus, father of the country, the boundaries of the new and old provinces were rectified where the Royal Ditch was, through Rutilius Gallicus, consul and pontifex, and Sentius Caecilianus, praetor, legates of Augustus with proconsular authority.
EDCS-25501785.summary: This inscription records the rectification of provincial boundaries under Emperor Vespasian, carried out by imperial legates Rutilius Gallicus and Sentius Caecilianus.

EDCS-25600116.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Bruttius Exoratus, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 90 years. Helvia, his wife, made this. He lies here.
EDCS-25600116.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Bruttius Exoratus, a veteran of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his wife.

EDCS-25600117.translation: Lucius Caelius Legatimus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, a soldier in the Third Augustan Legion, in the century of Fundanius Severus, lived 40 years, served 17 years. He lies here. Iulia Rustica, his wife, placed this.
EDCS-25600117.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Caelius Legatimus, a soldier in the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his wife.

EDCS-25600118.translation: Tiberius Claudius Manduccus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, lived [X] years.
EDCS-25600118.summary: This is a tombstone fragment for Tiberius Claudius Manduccus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-25600119.translation: Publius Durmius Firmus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, lived 22 years, served 4 years. Publius Durmius Firmus, veteran, father, erected this monument for his son.
EDCS-25600119.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Publius Durmius Firmus, a soldier of the Third Augustan Legion, erected by his veteran father who also bore the same name.

EDCS-25600664.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, Parthicus Maximus, Britannicus Maximus, Germanicus Maximus, with tribunician power for the 20th time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, restored [this].
EDCS-25600664.summary: This inscription records the restoration activities of Emperor Marcus Aurelius, detailing his imperial titles and powers up to his 20th tribunician power and 4th consulship.

EDCS-25600720.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Augusti filius Augustus tribunicia potestate XVI Asprenas consul proconsul VII vir epulonum viam ex castris hibernis Tacapes muniendam curavit legio III Augusta CI\n
EDCS-25600720.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of Augustus, with tribunician power for the 16th time, consul Asprenas for the 7th time, member of the college of seven epulones, ensured that the road from the winter camp to Tacapes be constructed, Legio III Augusta.
EDCS-25600720.summary: This inscription records Emperor Augustus's order, given through Consul Asprenas, for the construction of a road from the winter camp to Tacapes, carried out by Legio III Augusta.

EDCS-25600731.text_edition: \n Sexto Pullaeno Sexti filio Arnensi Floro Caeciliano praefecto iure dicundo sacerdoti Cererum anni CLXX IIviro quinquennali flamini perpetuo coloniae Iuliae Karthaginis pagus et civitas Thuggensis patrono decreto decurionum pecunia publica curatoribus Lucio Gallio Optato Sallustio Dato\n
EDCS-25600731.translation: To Sextus Pullaenus Florus Caecilianus, son of Sextus, of the Arnian tribe, who served as prefect of justice, priest of Ceres for the 170th year, quinquennial duovir, perpetual priest of the colony of Carthage; the pagus and civitas of Thugga, as patron, by decree of the decurions, at public expense, with Lucius Gallius Optatus and Sallustius Dato as curators.
EDCS-25600731.summary: This inscription records a public monument erected by the pagus and civitas of Thugga in honor of Sextus Pullaenus Florus Caecilianus, a prominent official in the colony of Carthage, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25600738.translation: To the perpetual priest, a citizen and exemplary man of honorable memory, for his exceptional love for the citizens and his fatherland, both orders rewarded the merits of a good citizen and patron, whom with the authority of their decree they had honored with an equestrian statue. The republic of the municipality of Septimius Aurelius Thuggensis erected (this statue) for eternal remembrance of mutual affection.
EDCS-25600738.summary: This inscription records the erection of an equestrian statue by the municipality of Thugga to honor a former perpetual priest and patron for his love of the citizens and fatherland, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25600919.translation: Under Emperor Antoninus Augustus, our lord, 343 [units of the imperial mint?]; officina of Regia, with Gallicanus and Vetus as consuls.
EDCS-25600919.summary: This inscription records the production of an item under Emperor Antoninus Augustus and consuls Gallicanus and Vetus, likely related to imperial workshops in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25600920.translation: Under Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, our lord, 5xx [units?]; officina of Agrippa, with Gallicanus and Vetus as consuls.
EDCS-25600920.summary: This inscription records the production of an item under Emperor Antoninus Pius and consuls Gallicanus and Vetus, likely related to imperial workshops in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25600921.translation: Under Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, our lord, 517 [units?]; officina of Agrippa, with Gallicanus and Vetus as consuls.
EDCS-25600921.summary: This inscription records the production of an item under Emperor Antoninus Pius and consuls Gallicanus and Vetus, likely related to imperial workshops in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25600924.translation: Under Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, our lord, [number missing] units; officina of Iunius; with Barbarus and Regulus as consuls.
EDCS-25600924.summary: This inscription records the production of an item under Emperor Antoninus Pius and consuls Barbarus and Regulus, likely related to imperial workshops in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25600929.translation: 305 [units?]; officina of Genius Montis; under Emperor Commodus Augustus (for the second time) and Victorinus (for the second time) as consuls; a boundary marker of Maximus, the procurator.
EDCS-25600929.summary: This inscription records a boundary marker related to an imperial officina and a procurator, dated to the consulship of Commodus and Victorinus, likely in the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25600930.translation: 53 [units?]; new officina Aureliana; under Anullinus (for the second time) and Frontonus as consuls; a boundary marker of Athenodorus, the procurator.
EDCS-25600930.summary: This inscription records a boundary marker related to a new imperial officina, dated to the consulship of Anullinus and Frontonus, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25600953.translation: Sacred to the shades of the underworld. Agrius Zopatus, a pious veteran, lived 70 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600953.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Agrius Zopatus, a pious veteran who lived to 70 years old, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25600955.text_edition: \n Lucius Flaminius Decimifilius Arnensis miles legionis III Augustae centuria Iuli Longi dilecto lectus ab Marco Silano militavit annis XIX in praesidio ut esset in salto Philomulsiano ab hostem sic in pugna occissus sic vixit pie annis XL hic situs est\n
EDCS-25600955.translation: Lucius Flaminius, son of Decimus, of the Arnian tribe, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, in the first century of Longus Iulius, was chosen by Marcus Silanus. He served for 19 years on guard duty in the Philomulsian pass against the enemy. He was killed in battle. He lived piously for 40 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-25600955.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Flaminius, a soldier of the III Augusta legion, who served for 19 years and died in battle at the age of 40, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25600956.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Fructa lived about 30 years. May her bones rest well here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Modianus, veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, pious, lived about 67 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25600956.summary: This inscription records the burial of two individuals, Aemilia Fructa and Gaius Julius Modianus, a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion, likely from the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-25600959.translation: Lucius Silicius Optatus lived 50 years; intercepted on his journey. The Simithu veterans, while delaying, made this for him from their own means.
EDCS-25600959.summary: This epitaph records the death of Lucius Silicius Optatus, who died while traveling, and was commemorated by the Simithu veterans, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-25600969.translation: Lucius Atinius Primus, pious, lived 68 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25600969.summary: This is an epitaph for Lucius Atinius Primus, recording his age at death and burial, likely from the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-25600970.translation: Aviania Muntana, daughter of Lucius, lived 50 years.
EDCS-25600970.summary: This is an epitaph for Aviania Muntana, daughter of Lucius, recording her age at death, likely from the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-25600972.translation: Publius Cluventius Felix lived 106 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25600972.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Publius Cluventius Felix, noting his exceptional age, likely from the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-25600974.translation: Gaius Cornelius Primus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, pious, lived about 60 years.
EDCS-25600974.summary: This epitaph records the life of Gaius Cornelius Primus, son of Gaius, a member of the Quirina tribe, noting his age, likely from the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-25600975.translation: Sextus Cornelius Reperitanus, pious, lived 50 years.
EDCS-25600975.summary: This epitaph records the life of Sextus Cornelius Reperitanus, noting his age, likely from the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-25600977.translation: Marcus Cossutius Adiutor, pious, lived 75 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25600977.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Marcus Cossutius Adiutor, noting his age, likely from the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-25600978.translation: Gaius Cossutius Primus, son of Gaius, pious, lived 9 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25600978.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Gaius Cossutius Primus, son of Gaius, noting his young age at death, likely from the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-25600981.translation: Gnaeus Domitius Optatus, son of Gnaeus, of the Quirina tribe, pious, was snatched from his parents by his talent. He lived 18 years. He rests here.
EDCS-25600981.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Gnaeus Domitius Optatus, son of Gnaeus, a young man noted for his talent, who died at 18, likely from the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-25600991.translation: Lucius Manlius Rogatus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, dutiful, lived 55 years; he rests here.
EDCS-25600991.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Manlius Rogatus, recording his name, parentage, tribal affiliation, lifespan, and burial place.

EDCS-25600993.translation: Munatia Quarta, dutiful, lived 65 years; she rests here.
EDCS-25600993.summary: This is a tombstone for Munatia Quarta, recording her name, epithet, lifespan, and burial place.

EDCS-25600994.translation: Mustia Faustina, daughter of Marcus, dutiful, lived 15 years.
EDCS-25600994.summary: This is a tombstone fragment for Mustia Faustina, recording her name, parentage, epithet, lifespan, and burial place (incomplete).

EDCS-25600996.translation: Papiria Quinta, daughter of Lucius, dutiful, lived...
EDCS-25600996.summary: This is a tombstone fragment for Papiria Quinta, recording her name, parentage, epithet, and the beginning of her lifespan.

EDCS-25600998.translation: Plotia Lapa, daughter of Gaius, lived 70 years; she rests here.
EDCS-25600998.summary: This is a tombstone for Plotia Lapa, recording her name, parentage, lifespan, and burial place.

EDCS-25601003.translation: Histonia Probata, daughter of Publius, dutiful, lived 77 years; she rests here.
EDCS-25601003.summary: This is a tombstone for Histonia Probata, recording her name, parentage, epithet, lifespan, and burial place.

EDCS-25601007.translation: To Rubria Honorata, dutiful, Titus Flavius Postimus [erected this] for his most cherished wife, who lived 36 years.
EDCS-25601007.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Titus Flavius Postimus to his wife Rubria Honorata, recording her name, epithet, lifespan, and relationship to the dedicant.

EDCS-25601008.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Sallustius Cai filius Quirina Fortunatianus Costobius eo quod inter Costobocos nutritus sit \n
EDCS-25601008.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gaius Sallustius Fortunatianus Costobius, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, because he was raised among the Costoboci.
EDCS-25601008.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the underworld deities (Dis Manibus) by Gaius Sallustius Fortunatianus Costobius, explaining his ethnic origin and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-25601014.translation: Vipsania Quinta, dutiful, lived 32 years; she rests here.
EDCS-25601014.summary: This is a tombstone for Vipsania Quinta, recording her name, epithet, lifespan, and burial place.

EDCS-25601179.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae Veneri Concordiae Augustae sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Traiani Hadriani Augusti patris patriae Quintus Maedius Severus patronus pagi et civitatis nomine suo templum quod promiserat ampliata pecunia a fundamentis exstruxit\n
